entries.title: Dashboard
entries.overview: The Dashboard is your command centre. Every time you log in, start here to see how your business is performing today — sales, stock health, expenses, and activity across all your branches.

before_you_start.text: You must be logged in to your business.
If you have multiple branches, use the Branch filter to focus on one branch or view all.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Select a specific branch to filter all dashboard data to that branch only. Choose 'All Branches' to see the combined view.
elements.notes: Changing this filter reloads all KPI cards and charts automatically.

elements.name: Refresh button
elements.description: Reloads all dashboard data from the server to show the latest numbers. Use this if you've just completed a sale or made changes and want to see updated figures.

elements.name: Sales Today card
elements.description: Shows the total gross sales amount for today in TZS. The mini chart below the number shows the sales trend over the last 8 data points.

elements.name: Revenue MTD card
elements.description: Total net revenue from the 1st of this month to today — gross sales minus refunds and credit notes. Compare against your monthly target to gauge trading performance. A detailed daily breakdown is shown in the Revenue / Sales Trend chart below.

elements.name: Expenses MTD card
elements.description: Total expenses recorded via the Expenses module from the 1st of this month to today. Compare against Revenue MTD for a rough profitability check — a detailed category breakdown is in the Expense Breakdown panel below.

elements.name: Stock Value card
elements.description: Shows the total monetary value of all stock currently in your inventory, and how many product variants are being tracked.

elements.name: Low Stock card
elements.description: Shows how many product variants are below the stock alert threshold (5 units). The sub-label shows how many of those are critically low (2 units or below).

elements.name: Active Branches card
elements.description: Shows how many branches recorded at least one sale this month. Sub-label shows transfers made today.

elements.name: Revenue / Sales Trend chart
elements.description: A line chart showing daily sales totals for the last 30 days. Useful for spotting patterns, slow days, or growth.

elements.name: Top Products panel
elements.description: Shows the 5 products that generated the most revenue this week. The bar next to each product shows its revenue relative to the top performer.

elements.name: Low Stock Alerts panel
elements.description: Lists up to 5 products that are running low on stock. Items highlighted in red are critical (2 units or below). Go to Stock to replenish.

elements.name: Sales by Branch panel
elements.description: Shows how much revenue each branch has generated this month, ranked with a bar chart. Useful if you manage multiple locations.

elements.name: Expense Breakdown panel
elements.description: Shows your top expense categories for this month with amounts and percentage of total spend.

elements.name: Recent Activity panel
elements.description: Shows the last 8 business events — sales, transfers, and alerts — with timestamps. Gives you a quick view of what has happened today.

elements.name: Global Search box
elements.description: Type to search across products, customers, receipts, and transfers. Suggestions appear automatically after 2 characters. Press Enter or click Search to run the full search.

elements.name: Search button
elements.description: Runs the search for whatever is typed in the search box and shows results below in two columns: Products, and Other Matches (receipts, customers, transfers).

elements.name: Open Full Search link
elements.description: Takes you to the dedicated Search page where you can run more detailed searches with additional filters.

elements.name: Quick Actions bar
elements.description: Three shortcut buttons at the top: New Sale (opens POS), New Transfer (opens transfer creation), and Stock Count (opens count wizard). Provides fast access to the most common workflows without navigating the sidebar.

elements.name: Daily Summary sentence
elements.description: A single sentence summarising today's activity — for example: 'Today you made 12 sales totaling TZS 450,000, with 3 pending approvals.' Updates when the dashboard data is refreshed.

elements.name: Pending Sync counter
elements.description: Shows the number of offline transactions waiting to be synced. Visible in the toolbar area. If this number is stuck above zero, check your internet connection or go to the Offline page.

elements.name: Open Shifts counter
elements.description: Shows how many shifts are currently open across all branches. Visible in the toolbar area next to Pending Sync.

elements.name: Sales Trend percentage badge
elements.description: A small badge on the Sales Today card showing the day-over-day percentage change (e.g. ▲ 12% or ▼ 5%). Green for positive, red for negative. Helps you quickly see if today is better or worse than yesterday.

elements.name: Margin percentage badge
elements.description: Shown on the Revenue MTD card. Displays the gross margin percentage for the month so far.

elements.name: Critical Low Stock badge
elements.description: A sub-label on the Low Stock card showing how many items are critically low (2 units or below). These items need urgent restocking.

elements.name: Transfers Today counter
elements.description: Shown as a sub-label on the Active Branches card. Shows the number of stock transfers created today.

elements.name: Low Stock severity indicators
elements.description: In the Low Stock Alerts panel, items are colour-coded by severity: red border and pulsing badge for critical (≤2 units), amber for low (≤5 units). Helps prioritise which items to restock first.

elements.name: Expense Breakdown progress bars
elements.description: Each expense category in the Expense Breakdown panel shows a progress bar indicating its percentage of total expenses. The percentage is also displayed as text.

elements.name: Activity type icons
elements.description: Each entry in the Recent Activity panel has a colour-coded icon: green tick for sales, blue arrows for transfers, red exclamation for alerts. Makes it easy to scan activity type at a glance.

elements.name: Recent Sales table
elements.description: A compact table at the bottom showing the most recent completed sales. Columns: receipt number, time, total amount, and status badge. Click a row to navigate to the receipt.

elements.name: Search result summary
elements.description: When using the Global Search box, a summary line appears showing the count of matching Products, Receipts, and Customers. Helps you know which category has results before opening full search.

steps: Look at the Sales Today card for the total.
Check the Revenue / Sales Trend chart to see if today's figure is typical.
If you manage multiple branches, use the Branch filter to check each one.
Check the Low Stock card to see how many items need attention.
Look at the Low Stock Alerts panel to see which specific products are low.
Go to Stock > Products to take action.

common_tasks.task: Search for a product or customer
steps: Type in the Global Search box — suggestions appear after 2 characters.
Click a suggestion or press Enter to search.
For more options, click 'Open Full Search'.

warnings: Dashboard data does not update automatically while you are on the page — click Refresh to see the latest figures.
The Low Stock threshold is set at 5 units. Items at 2 or below are flagged as critical.
The Sales Today card shows gross sales (before any expenses or returns). For profit figures, look at the Revenue MTD and Expenses MTD cards together.

related_pages.reason: You must be logged in before accessing the Dashboard.
Use Search for detailed lookups across products, customers, and receipts.
For deeper analysis beyond the dashboard summaries.
Act on low-stock alerts by going to Stock.
View the full list of system notifications and alerts.

entries.title: Approvals
entries.overview: Review and act on pending approval requests. Each decision card features an urgency left border — amber for pending, emerald for approved, and red for rejected — with per-type colored icon containers that distinguish stock adjustments, refunds, discounts, and transfers at a glance. Action buttons use distinct colors: emerald for Approve, red for Reject, and amber for Delegate. Approval requests are created automatically when transactions exceed configured thresholds. Uses the ListPage layout with card/table views, a KPI decision dashboard, and bulk action capabilities.

elements.name: Pending KPI
elements.description: Amber KPI card with Clock icon showing count of requests still awaiting a decision. Uses animated FlipCounter. This is the primary urgency indicator — monitor it regularly.

elements.name: Approved today KPI
elements.description: Emerald KPI card with CheckCircle icon showing how many approvals were approved today. Uses animated FlipCounter.

elements.name: Rejected KPI
elements.description: Red KPI card with XCircle icon showing rejected count in the current view. Uses animated FlipCounter.

elements.name: Total queue KPI
elements.description: Gold KPI card with ListTodo icon showing the total number of items in the queue. Uses animated FlipCounter.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Free-text search across request data. Useful for finding approvals linked to a specific receipt number, supplier reference, or product name.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, or EXPIRED. EXPIRED means the approval window timed out before anyone acted — the original transaction was automatically blocked and cannot be recovered from this page.

elements.name: Action type filter
elements.description: Filter by the type of action that triggered the request: STOCK_ADJUSTMENT, STOCK_COUNT, REFUND, DISCOUNT, TRANSFER, or PURCHASE. Each type has a unique color-coded icon badge (Package, ClipboardCheck, RotateCcw, Percent, Truck, ShoppingCart).

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restricts results to requests created within the selected date window. Useful for end-of-month reviews or when investigating a specific period.

elements.name: Approve button
elements.description: Emerald button with CheckCircle icon. Immediately approves the request. The system notifies the original requester and the blocked transaction is allowed to complete. This action is final and cannot be undone. Only appears for PENDING items.

elements.name: Reject button
elements.description: Red button with XCircle icon. Opens a prompt where you must enter a rejection reason. Once confirmed, the original transaction is permanently blocked and the requester is notified with your reason. Only appears for PENDING items.

elements.name: Delegate button
elements.description: Amber button with Forward icon. Opens the Delegate modal. Only appears for PENDING items.

elements.name: Delegate modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Delegate button. Shows the approval being delegated in the header and a user search/select. Choosing a user and confirming reassigns the approval to their queue. ESC or backdrop click closes without delegating.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card view (decision cards with urgency stripes, type badges, hero numbers) and table view (compact rows with status dots and inline actions).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of approval records. Combine with filters to work through the queue efficiently.

elements.name: Bulk actions bar
elements.description: Appears when one or more items are selected. Shows selection count with nvi-pop animation, Bulk Approve (emerald, CheckCircle icon) and Bulk Reject (red, XCircle icon) buttons. Select All checkbox in table header selects all pending items on current page.

elements.name: Decision cards (card view)
elements.description: Each approval is a Card with: left urgency stripe (amber=pending, green=approved, red=rejected, grey=expired), color-coded type badge at top, reference number in mono font, hero quantity/amount number, target name, requester with User icon and relative time, and contextual action buttons for PENDING items. Uses nvi-card-hover animation.

elements.name: Table view
elements.description: Wrapped in Card component. Columns: checkbox, type (icon badge), target (reference + name), status (colored dot + label), requester (User icon), amount (right-aligned mono), created date, actions. Sortable headers. Row hover highlight.

elements.name: Empty state
elements.description: When no approvals match filters, shows EmptyState with floating CheckCircle icon and message 'All caught up — no pending approvals'. Uses nvi-float animation.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills: amber for PENDING, green for APPROVED, red for REJECTED. Non-pending items use nvi-status-fade animation.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each approval request has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. APR-001). Shown in mono font in both card and table views.

before_you_start.text: You need the approvals.write permission to approve or reject requests. Without it, the Approve and Reject buttons are visible but disabled.
Approval thresholds (the limits that trigger a request) are configured in Business Settings. If you are unsure why a request was generated, check the threshold settings there.

steps: Set the Status filter to PENDING to see only unapproved requests.
Find the request — use Search or the Action filter to locate it faster if the queue is long.
Click Approve. The requester is notified automatically and the transaction proceeds.

common_tasks.task: Reject a request with a reason
steps: Set the Status filter to PENDING and locate the request.
Click Reject — a prompt appears asking for a rejection reason.
Enter your reason and confirm. The transaction is blocked and the requester is notified with your reason.

common_tasks.task: Review all approvals for a specific action type
steps: Use the Action type filter to select the category (e.g. DISCOUNT or REFUND).
Optionally add a date range to narrow the period.
Use the Status filter to separate PENDING items from historical decisions.

warnings: Expired requests cannot be reactivated. If a request expires, the original user must retry the transaction — a new approval request will be generated.
Approvals are final. Once you click Approve, the action proceeds immediately with no undo. Double-check the request detail before approving large adjustments or refunds.
Only users with the approvals.write permission can approve or reject. If the buttons are disabled, your role may be branch-scoped and the request belongs to a different branch.

entries.title: Attachments
entries.overview: Upload and manage documents linked to purchases or purchase orders — such as supplier invoices, delivery notes, signed contracts, or proof of payment. Each document you select shows its own attachment list. Files are stored securely and can be retrieved at any time for auditing, dispute resolution, or record-keeping.

elements.name: Target type selector
elements.description: Choose whether to search for a Purchase or a Purchase Order. This controls which records appear in the Target document selector below (inside the modal).

elements.name: Target document selector
elements.description: SmartSelect showing all records of the chosen type. Each option includes the supplier name, reference number, and date to help you identify the right document. The attachment list only loads after you select a target document (inside the modal).

elements.name: File input
elements.description: Opens your device's file browser. Select the document you want to upload. File size limits are set by your subscription plan — check the Business Settings subscription panel for your current limits (inside the modal).

elements.name: "Upload" button (header) + modal
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a file upload modal. The modal contains: target type selector (purchase / purchase order), target document selector, drag-and-drop upload zone with "Browse files" fallback, selected-file preview row, and an Upload submit button. Previously this was an inline CollapsibleSection on the page — moved into a modal. **Fix**: the file-input `accept` attribute was narrowed to `.pdf,.jpg,.jpeg,.png` to match the backend's actual allowed MIME types — previously it accepted Word/Excel/CSV/WebP which the backend would reject with a 400 error. Upload flow still uses the 2-step presign pattern (POST /presign → client PUTs to S3 → POST /attachments to register).

elements.name: Attachment list
elements.description: Shows all files linked to the selected document. Columns include filename, file size, upload version, and status (ACTIVE or REMOVED). Click a filename to download the file.

elements.name: Remove button
elements.description: Marks an attachment as REMOVED. The file is no longer shown by default but is retained in the audit log for compliance. This cannot be undone from the UI — contact support if you need to restore a removed file.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Loads the next page of attachment records when there are more files than the initial batch.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout and table layout for the attachment list. Table view is better when reviewing many attachments at once.

elements.name: Active count KPI
elements.description: Number of non-removed attachments currently loaded for the selected document type. Use this to confirm an upload succeeded or to spot documents that still need files attached.

elements.name: Total size KPI
elements.description: Combined file size in MB of all loaded attachments. Monitor this against your subscription storage limit.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (Upload form)
elements.description: The upload form is hidden behind an 'Upload' button. Includes a drag-and-drop zone and file browser (inside the modal).

elements.name: File type filter
elements.description: Filter attachments by type: All, Images (JPEG/PNG/WebP), Documents (PDF/DOC/DOCX), or Spreadsheets (XLS/XLSX/CSV).

elements.name: Drag-and-drop upload zone
elements.description: Drop files directly onto the upload area or click to browse. Supported formats: PDF, JPEG, PNG, WebP, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX, CSV (inside the modal).

elements.name: File versioning
elements.description: Upload a new version of an existing file. The version history is maintained and accessible. Each version shows its version number (v1, v2, etc.) and upload date.

elements.name: Bulk download
elements.description: Select multiple files using checkboxes and download them all at once. A 'Select All' checkbox selects all files matching the current filter.

elements.name: Storage tracking
elements.description: A KPI card showing total storage used in MB. Helps monitor storage consumption against subscription limits.

elements.name: File type icons
elements.description: Each attachment displays an icon based on its file type (PDF icon, image icon, spreadsheet icon, document icon) for quick visual identification.

elements.name: MIME type display
elements.description: The file's MIME type is shown on each attachment card and in table view.

elements.name: Version history panel
elements.description: Click 'View Versions' on an attachment to see all previous versions with download links and upload timestamps.

before_you_start.text: You need the attachments.write permission to upload or remove files.
At least one purchase or purchase order must exist before you can attach files. Create a purchase or PO first from the Purchases page.
You must select a target document before the attachment list loads or the Upload button becomes active.

common_tasks.task: Upload an invoice to a purchase
steps: Set Target type to Purchase.
Select the purchase from the Target document selector (search by supplier or reference).
Click the file input and select the invoice file from your device.
Click Upload. The file appears immediately in the attachment list below.

common_tasks.task: View and download all documents on a purchase order
steps: Set Target type to Purchase Order.
Select the purchase order from the Target document selector.
The attachment list loads. Click any filename to download it to your device.

warnings: Removing an attachment is permanent from a user perspective — there is no restore button in the UI. Only support can recover a removed file.
The Upload button is inactive until you have selected a target document. If it appears greyed out, select a document from the Target document selector first.

entries.title: Audit Logs
entries.overview: An immutable, cryptographically chained log of every action taken in the system — by users, by automated jobs, and by background processes. Every write operation (create, update, delete) generates a log entry capturing who did it, from which device, with what outcome, and what changed (before/after snapshot). Use audit logs to investigate disputes, verify compliance, trace a specific change, or understand the sequence of events during an incident. Logs cannot be modified or deleted — any gap in the hash chain indicates a potential integrity issue.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts results to log entries generated by users operating within a specific branch. Useful for branch managers who only need to review their own branch's activity.

elements.name: User filter
elements.description: Shows only log entries generated by a specific user. Use this when investigating a particular staff member's recent actions — combine with a date range to narrow to a relevant time window.

elements.name: Role filter
elements.description: Filters by the role the user held at the moment the action was taken. Because role assignments can change over time, this reflects the historical role at the time of the event — not the user's current role.

elements.name: Action filter
elements.description: Filters by the type of database operation: CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, READ, LOGIN, LOGOUT, EXPORT, and others. For example, filter to DELETE to see all records that were removed.

elements.name: Outcome filter
elements.description: Filters by the result of the action: SUCCESS or FAILURE. Filtering to FAILURE is useful for identifying permission denials, validation errors, or repeated failed login attempts.

elements.name: Resource Type filter
elements.description: Narrows results to a specific entity type, such as Sale, Transfer, User, Product, or Approval. Combine with Resource ID to find all log entries for one specific record.

elements.name: Resource ID filter
elements.description: Finds all log entries related to a single record, identified by its ID. Enter or paste the ID directly, or use the typeahead search to look it up by name. Requires Resource Type to be selected first.

elements.name: Advanced filters toggle
elements.description: Reveals a second row of technical filters: correlation ID (all logs from one HTTP request chain), request ID (one specific request), session ID (all actions in one login session), device ID (all actions from a registered offline device), offline flag (actions taken while offline), and approval status. Use these when tracing a complex incident.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Limits results to a specific time window. The system stores logs indefinitely — always apply a date range when searching to avoid loading unnecessarily large result sets.

elements.name: Activity filter buttons
elements.description: Toggle switches to suppress noisy, routine log entries: guard permission checks, dashboard data refreshes, and background auth token renewals. Hiding these reveals the meaningful user-initiated actions more clearly.

elements.name: Log card / row
elements.description: Summarises a single log entry: a human-readable action narrative (e.g. 'User Jane updated Product SKU-042'), an outcome badge (SUCCESS or FAILURE), timestamp, branch, user, and resource type/ID. Click or expand to see the full before/after snapshot, metadata, and hash.

elements.name: Diff highlights
elements.description: Visible inside an expanded log entry for UPDATE actions. Shows each field that changed with the old value (from) and new value (to) side by side. Fields that were not changed are omitted to keep the diff concise.

elements.name: View Chain button
elements.description: Loads all log entries that share the same correlation ID as this entry — i.e., all actions triggered by a single HTTP request chain. For example, approving a sale may trigger a stock movement, a receipt, and a notification — View Chain shows all of those together in sequence.

elements.name: Related links
elements.description: Contextual deep links to the relevant module page for the log's resource type. For example, a log entry for a Sale includes a link to the Receipts page filtered to that receipt. Use these to jump from the audit trail directly to the affected record.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between three views: Table (dense rows with outcome dot, action badge, actor avatar, and relative timestamp), Cards (event summary cards with hover animations), and Timeline (grouped by day using nvi-timeline CSS with action-colored dots and connecting lines).

elements.name: Forensic detail panel
elements.description: The star of the page. When you click any log entry, a sticky side panel slides in from below showing a forensic investigation card. The header has color-coded action badges (emerald=create, blue=update, red=delete, amber=auth, purple=approval), outcome badge with icon, resource breadcrumb (type > ID), relative and absolute timestamps, and actor avatar with role badge. The hero section is the before/after diff: a two-column layout showing field labels (human-readable, not raw keys) with before values in red strikethrough and after values in green bold. Added fields show green background with dash in the before column; removed fields show red background with dash in the after column. For CREATE actions, a single 'Created values' column appears; for DELETE actions, a single 'Deleted values' column with red tint. Below the diff: a collapsible metadata section with trace IDs as compact mono-font badges, hash chain badges with Link icon, and a 'View chain' button.

elements.name: KPI strip
elements.description: Four metric cards with icon containers: Total entries (FileText icon, gold), Success rate as percentage (CheckCircle icon, emerald), Unique actors (Users icon, blue), and Active filters (Filter icon, amber). Uses Card component with staggered entrance animation.

elements.name: Activity by user leaderboard
elements.description: A Card showing the top 10 most active users with avatar initials, name, action count, and a proportional bar chart. Click Load to fetch the data on demand.

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Numbered pagination to navigate through large result sets. Results load 50 entries per page.

elements.name: Activity by user section
elements.description: A collapsible section showing a ranked list of users by action count. Click 'Load' to fetch the data. Shows each user's name, avatar, and total number of actions in the filtered period.

elements.name: Export CSV button
elements.description: Export the currently filtered audit logs as a CSV file for offline analysis or compliance reporting.

elements.name: Export PDF button
elements.description: Export the currently filtered audit logs as a PDF document for formal reporting or archiving.

elements.name: Activity type presets
elements.description: A dropdown with 17 preset activity filters (Sales, Purchases, Stock Adjustments, Transfers, etc.) for quickly narrowing the log to a specific area of the business.

elements.name: Noise toggles
elements.description: Three toggles to show or hide system noise: Guard Checks (permission verifications), Dashboard Reports (dashboard data loads), and Auth Refresh (token refreshes). Hidden by default to keep the log focused on business actions.

elements.name: Card view (ViewToggle)
elements.description: A card view option alongside the default table view. Cards show an audit narrative summary with diff highlights for each event.

elements.name: No auto branch filter
elements.description: The audit logs page loads all events across all branches by default. Business-level operations like imports, settings changes, and subscription events don't belong to any specific branch — they only appear when no branch filter is active. Use the branch filter to narrow results to a specific branch if needed.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'audit.read' permission to access this page.
Audit logs are read-only and cannot be modified, deleted, or exported from this view.
Apply a date range before searching — the system stores logs indefinitely and unbounded queries can be slow.

common_tasks.task: Find all actions taken by a specific user
steps: Open the User filter and select the user.
Set a date range to the relevant period.
Review the results — use the Action filter to narrow to specific operation types if needed.
Expand any row to see the full before/after diff.

common_tasks.task: Investigate what changed on a specific record
steps: Select the Resource Type for the record (e.g. Product).
Enter or search for the Resource ID.
Review the log entries — each UPDATE will show what changed via Diff highlights.
Use View Chain to see any linked actions that were triggered by the same operation.

common_tasks.task: Trace the full sequence of an operation
steps: Find the primary log entry for the action.
Click 'View Chain' to load all correlated log entries.
Review the full sequence in chronological order to understand every step that was triggered.

warnings: Audit logs are append-only and cannot be edited or deleted by anyone — this is by design for compliance.
Each log entry is hash-chained to the previous one. A gap or mismatch in the chain is a serious integrity signal.
The Resource ID filter requires the Resource Type to be selected first — searches without a type may return no results.

entries.title: Categories
entries.overview: Manage your product taxonomy with card and table views. Categories display Folder/FolderOpen icons to distinguish top-level from child entries, GitBranch hierarchy visualization, ProgressBar product count bars, and hover animations. Labeled TextInput fields and CollapsibleSection forms keep creation and editing structured.

elements.name: KPI – Categories
elements.description: Total categories loaded in the current filter scope, displayed in a Card with a FolderTree icon in a rounded gold container. Without filters, this is your complete taxonomy size. Staggered entrance animation on page load.

elements.name: KPI – Active
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE categories shown with a CheckCircle icon in an emerald container. Only ACTIVE categories appear in the Category Selector when creating products. INACTIVE categories are hidden from those flows.

elements.name: KPI – Parent Groups
elements.description: Count of top-level categories with no parent, displayed with a GitBranch icon in a blue container. These are your root taxonomy groups (e.g. 'Beverages', 'Snacks').

elements.name: KPI – Filtered Status
elements.description: Reflects the current Status filter with a Filter icon in an amber container so you can tell at a glance which category set you are browsing.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Find categories by typing part of the name. Search triggers when you press Enter or click the Search button — not automatically while typing. Useful when navigating a large taxonomy to locate a specific group.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Narrows results by category status: ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED. Uses SmartSelect dropdown for selection.

elements.name: Category Name Input
elements.description: Labeled TextInput field for the new category name. This name appears in the Category Selector on the Products page, in report filters, and in POS navigation. Uses placeholder text 'e.g. Beverages' for guidance.

elements.name: Parent Selector
elements.description: Optional SmartSelect with labeled heading. Assigns this category under an existing parent. Placeholder reads 'Leave empty for top-level'.

elements.name: Create Category Button
elements.description: Creates the category with a Plus Lucide icon and adds it to the list immediately. Styled with nvi-cta nvi-press rounded-xl. Requires catalog.write permission.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card grid and table layout for the categories list.

elements.name: Edit Button (per category)
elements.description: Pencil Lucide icon button that opens inline editing. In card view, the card gets a gold ring highlight (ring-2 ring-gold-500/30) when in edit mode. Uses labeled TextInput fields. Requires catalog.write permission.

elements.name: Save / Cancel (inline edit)
elements.description: Save uses Check Lucide icon, Cancel uses X Lucide icon. Both styled as rounded-xl buttons. The parent selector excludes the category itself to prevent self-parent loops. Edit card slides in with nvi-slide-in-bottom animation.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Category form)
elements.description: The create form is hidden behind a collapsible section. Expands to reveal a Card with labeled TextInput and SmartSelect fields. Form slides in with nvi-slide-in-bottom animation. Closes automatically after successful creation.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills shown next to each category name in both card and table views. Green for ACTIVE, amber for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED.

elements.name: ActionButtons (table view)
elements.description: Pencil Lucide icon buttons in the actions column. Opens inline editing for that category row.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view that sort the list. Available columns: Name, Parent, Products, Status. Click a header to sort ascending, click again for descending.

elements.name: Bulk selection checkboxes
elements.description: Custom Checkbox component on each category in both card and table views. In card view, integrated into the header row next to the category name. Select multiple to enable bulk actions.

elements.name: Bulk actions bar
elements.description: Appears when categories are selected. Shows animated selection count (nvi-pop). CheckCircle Lucide icon for Activate, Archive Lucide icon for Archive. Both styled as rounded-xl bordered buttons. Cancel clears selection.

elements.name: Hierarchy indicator
elements.description: GitBranch Lucide icon shown next to parent category name in both card and table views. In card view, top-level categories show italic 'Top-level category' text, while child categories show the GitBranch icon with parent name. Folder icon for top-level, FolderOpen for child categories.

elements.name: Product count ProgressBar
elements.description: Each category shows a labeled ProgressBar with product count number and a proportional bar relative to the largest category. In card view, label and count are on the same row above the bar. In table view, count and compact bar sit side by side.

before_you_start.text: catalog.read is required to list categories; catalog.write is required to create or edit them.
Plan your top-level groups first, then child categories — hierarchy is hard to restructure later.
Search for an existing category before creating a new one to avoid duplicates.

steps: Enter the category name.
Leave the Parent selector empty.
Click Create Category and confirm the success banner.

common_tasks.task: Move a category under a different parent
steps: Find the category in cards or table view.
Click Edit and select the new parent.
Click Save and verify the parent label updates.

warnings: Renaming a category affects how products appear in filters and reports — confirm with your team before changing names.
The parent selector blocks self-parent assignment, but circular hierarchies can still be created through multiple edits.
Categories cannot be deleted from this page — archive them instead.

related_pages.reason: Products require a category before they can be created.
Variants inherit category context through their parent products.

entries.title: Products
entries.overview: Create and manage product records. Products are displayed in card or table view with hover animations, image zoom, and staggered entrance effects. Create products via the labeled form or the Product Wizard.

elements.name: KPI – Products
elements.description: Total products matching current filters. Without any filters, this is your full catalogue size. Products without variants cannot be sold — a product with zero variants will not appear in the POS.

elements.name: KPI – Active
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE products in the current view. Only ACTIVE products are visible in POS search and sales flows. If a product is missing from the POS, check here to confirm its status.

elements.name: KPI – With Images
elements.description: Count of products that have at least one active image. Products without images display a placeholder in the POS and customer-facing views. Use this to identify which products still need photos uploaded.

elements.name: KPI – Catalog Focus
elements.description: Shows which category filter is active — or 'All Categories' when none is selected. Helps you orient yourself when browsing a large catalogue. If you need to work within a specific category, set the Category Filter below to lock to it.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Find products by name. Search triggers when you press Enter or click the Search button — not automatically while typing. Use this to quickly navigate a large catalogue to a specific product for editing, image upload, or to open the View Related panel and check stock.

elements.name: Category Filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to products in a specific category. Use this when you need to review or update all products in one section of your catalogue — for example, checking all 'Beverages' have images before a promotion.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter by product status: ACTIVE (visible in POS and sales), INACTIVE (hidden from POS), or ARCHIVED (soft-deleted, removed from all live flows). Filter to INACTIVE to find products accidentally deactivated.

elements.name: Has Variants Filter
elements.description: Filter to products that do or do not have at least one variant. Products with no variants cannot appear in the POS — use 'No Variants' to find products that still need variants created before they can be sold.

elements.name: Has Images Filter
elements.description: Filter to products that do or do not have at least one active image. Use 'No Images' to create a work list of products that still need photos — unimaged products appear as placeholders in the POS and customer-facing views.

elements.name: Open Wizard Button
elements.description: Navigates to the Product Wizard for guided product + variant creation in a single flow.

elements.name: Product Name Input
elements.description: The display name as it will appear on receipts, in reports, and in POS search results. Has a visible label above the field. Make it specific enough to distinguish from similar products (e.g. 'Coca-Cola 500ml' rather than just 'Coke').

elements.name: Description Input
elements.description: Optional text description visible in product detail views — not printed on receipts. Has a visible label above the field. Use it for internal notes such as supplier codes, storage instructions, or key specifications.

elements.name: Category Selector
elements.description: Required. Assigns the product to a category, which determines how it appears in POS category navigation and in reports. A product cannot be saved without selecting a category. Create the category first if it does not exist.

elements.name: Create Product Button
elements.description: Creates the product record and adds it to the list with a tactile press animation. The product has no variants yet — after creation, go to the Variants page or use the Product Wizard to add SKUs with pricing, barcodes, and stock.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card grid (with images and related panels) and compact table view. Smooth view switching preserves filter and sort state.

elements.name: View Related Button (per product)
elements.description: Expands a panel showing the product's variants, stock on hand (in active branch), and recent stock movements.

elements.name: Primary Image Upload (per product)
elements.description: Uploads and sets the product's main display image (PNG or JPG, max 20 MB). This image appears in the POS product grid and on receipts if image printing is enabled. The first image uploaded automatically becomes the primary.

elements.name: Additional Image Upload (per product)
elements.description: Uploads extra images — only available if your subscription allows multiple images per product.

elements.name: Make Primary Button (per image)
elements.description: Promotes an existing non-primary image to become the product's main display image. The previously primary image is demoted to a secondary image. Use this to swap the display image without deleting the current one.

elements.name: Remove Button (per image)
elements.description: Deactivates the selected image — it is no longer served as the product photo but is not permanently deleted. If you remove the primary image, the next remaining image is automatically promoted to primary.

elements.name: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Navigate pages and adjust page size for the product list.

elements.name: Create product button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with Plus icon. Opens the Create Product modal. Only shown to users with the catalog.write permission.

elements.name: Create Product modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Create product header button. Contains 'Open wizard' and 'Bulk import' shortcut buttons at the top, plus three fields: name, description, category. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful create.

elements.name: Edit Product modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened by clicking the Pencil action on a product card or table row. Shows the primary image thumbnail alongside editable fields: name, description, category, status. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Changes are applied via a single Save button — no live-save.

elements.name: Image drag-and-drop upload
elements.description: A drop zone on each product card where you can drag and drop image files or click to browse. Supports JPEG, PNG, and WebP. The first image uploaded becomes the primary product image automatically.

elements.name: Image gallery
elements.description: Shows all uploaded images for a product. The primary image has a 'Primary' badge. Hover over any image to see 'Make Primary' and 'Remove' buttons. Removing the primary image promotes the next image automatically.

elements.name: RelatedNotesPanel
elements.description: A notes thread attached to each product card. Team members can add timestamped notes visible to everyone — useful for recording supplier info, quality issues, or restocking reminders.

elements.name: Duplicate Product action
elements.description: Creates a copy of the product with all its variants, pricing, and settings. The copy is named '[Product Name] (Copy)'. Saves time when creating similar products.

elements.name: Bulk Import link
elements.description: A button that navigates to the Imports page. Use this for importing many products at once via CSV.

elements.name: Last Sold timestamp
elements.description: Shown on each product card and in the table view. Displays relative time (e.g. '2h ago', '3d ago') since the product was last sold. Helps identify slow-moving or dormant products.

elements.name: Related Records panel
elements.description: Expandable panel on each product card showing three sections: Variants (list of all variants), Stock on Hand (current quantities per branch), and Recent Movements (latest stock changes). Click 'View Related' to expand.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Name, Category, Status, and Last Sold date.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills on each product. Green for ACTIVE, amber for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED.

elements.name: ActionButtons (table view)
elements.description: Icon buttons in the actions column: Edit, View Related, and Duplicate.

elements.name: Variant count badge
elements.description: A small badge on each product card showing how many variants it has.

elements.name: Image thumbnail (table view)
elements.description: A small 32x32 product image preview in the first column of the table view.

elements.name: Duplicate Product button
elements.description: Copy icon button on each product card and table row. Opens the Create Product modal pre-filled with the original product's name ('… (copy)'), description, and category. You can adjust and save as a new product.

before_you_start.text: At least one category must exist — create categories first at /catalog/categories.
catalog.read is required to list products; catalog.write is required to create products and manage images.
Use the Product Wizard (/catalog/products/wizard) when setting up a new product with variants and pricing in one flow.

steps: Enter the product name.
Select a category.
Click Create Product and confirm the success banner.
Switch to Cards view.
Find the product card.
Click the Primary Image file input and select a PNG or JPG under 20 MB.
The image appears on the card once uploaded.

warnings: Category is required — you cannot create a product without selecting one first.
The first image uploaded must be the primary image; additional images require a subscription that allows multiple images.
View Related loads live stock data — if the branch scope is wrong, the numbers shown will be misleading.
Product images are served publicly — do not upload sensitive or confidential material.

related_pages.reason: Categories must exist before products can be created.
Variants are created after the product exists.
The wizard guides full product + variant creation in one flow.

entries.title: New Product Wizard
entries.overview: A 4-step guided form with a visual step indicator, labeled input fields, and smooth step transitions. Create a new product with one or more variants and set opening stock quantities. A product is the parent item (e.g. 'Bottled Water'); variants are the sellable versions (e.g. '500ml', '1L'). Each variant gets its own SKU, price, cost, VAT setting, and optional barcode. You can set initial stock quantities per variant per branch on step 3.

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Uses the shared WizardSteps component with numbered circles for each step and checkmarks for completed steps. Shows your position across the four steps: Product → Variants → Initial Stock → Review. Each step must be completed before advancing. Click Back at any step to return and make changes before submitting.

elements.name: Product name input
elements.description: Required. Has a visible label above the field. The product's display name — shown in POS product search, inventory reports, transfer forms, and the catalogue. Use a name that your staff will recognise and search for quickly.

elements.name: Description input
elements.description: Optional. Has a visible label above the field. A longer description of the product — for example materials, intended use, or key features. Displayed on product detail pages and in some report exports.

elements.name: Category selector
elements.description: Assign the product to a category. Categories group products in the POS product browser, catalogue filters, and reports. Categories must exist before using this wizard — create them from the main Catalog page.

elements.name: Variant row
elements.description: One row per variant displayed as a Card with hover animation. A variant is a distinct, sellable version of the product — for example 'Small', '500ml', or 'Blue'. Each variant has its own name, SKU (stock keeping unit — must be unique in your catalogue), default sale price, minimum price (the floor below which discounts cannot go), default cost (used for margin calculations), VAT mode, stock tracking toggle, and optional barcode.

elements.name: VAT mode selector (per variant)
elements.description: INCLUSIVE — the sale price already includes VAT; the system extracts the VAT portion at sale time. EXCLUSIVE — VAT is added on top of the price at the point of sale. EXEMPT — no VAT applies to this variant. Choose according to the local tax treatment for this specific product.

elements.name: Track stock toggle (per variant)
elements.description: When enabled, every sale, receipt, transfer, and adjustment for this variant creates a stock movement record and affects the physical stock count. Disable only for service items or non-physical products you do not want to track — for example a delivery fee line item.

elements.name: Barcode input + Scan button
elements.description: Enter a barcode number manually or click the Scan button (with Lucide scan icon) to use the device camera to read it. The barcode is used at POS for barcode scanner lookup — scanning a product at the till finds its variant automatically. Each barcode must be unique across your entire catalogue.

elements.name: Base unit / Sell unit selectors
elements.description: Base unit is how you receive and track stock (e.g. Case). Sell unit is how you sell it at POS (e.g. Bottle). The conversion factor tells the system how many sell units make one base unit (e.g. 24 bottles per case — enter 24). If you buy and sell in the same unit, set both to the same unit and set conversion factor to 1. Units must be configured in Business Settings before they appear here.

elements.name: Add Variant button
elements.description: Adds another variant row to the product. Includes a Plus icon. Use this for products that come in multiple sizes, colours, flavours, or packaging options — each becomes a separately tracked SKU in your catalogue with its own stock level.

elements.name: Remove Variant button
elements.description: Removes that variant row before the product is created. Includes a Trash icon. At least one variant is required — you cannot remove the last row.

elements.name: Stock lines (Initial Stock step)
elements.description: One row per variant per branch. Enter the opening stock quantity for each variant at each branch. This creates an opening stock movement record that sets the starting inventory level. If you are onboarding an existing product, enter the quantity currently on hand at each branch.

elements.name: Review step
elements.description: Full summary of all data entered: product name, category, each variant's name, SKU, prices, and cost, plus initial stock quantities per branch. Review every field carefully — clicking Submit creates all records permanently. There is no bulk undo after submission.

elements.name: Next / Back buttons
elements.description: Move forward or backward between wizard steps with tactile press animation. Back includes a ChevronLeft icon, Next includes a ChevronRight icon. Back does not discard your current step's data.

elements.name: Submit button
elements.description: Creates the product, all variants, and the initial stock movement records. The product is immediately available in POS product search, receiving selectors, transfer forms, and the catalogue. Requires the catalog.write permission.

elements.name: Visual ProgressBar
elements.description: A progress indicator showing your current step: Product Info, Variants, Initial Stock, Review.

elements.name: MetricComparison component
elements.description: In the review step, shows side-by-side comparison of variant prices, costs, and margins.

elements.name: Max variant limit warning
elements.description: Warning shown when approaching the maximum number of variants allowed per product.

elements.name: Duplicate SKU error
elements.description: Error shown when a variant SKU already exists in the system.

elements.name: Initial Stock step (INITIAL_STOCK reason)
elements.description: Set opening stock per variant per branch. Stock added here creates a stock purchase cost expense.

elements.name: Unit Help Panel
elements.description: An expandable 'How do units work?' panel that appears next to the unit selectors. Explains base unit, sell unit, and conversion factor with a concrete example. Includes a tip that if you don't plan on using conversions, leave both units as Piece.

before_you_start.text: You need the catalog.write permission.
At least one category must exist before using this wizard. Create categories from the main Catalog page.
Units (base and sell) must be configured in Business Settings before they appear in the unit selectors.
Barcode scanning requires camera permission to be granted in your browser.

common_tasks.task: Add a new product with one variant
steps: On the Product step, enter the product name and select a category. Click Next.
On the Variants step, the first row is pre-filled — enter the SKU, default price, minimum price, and default cost. Set the VAT mode and enable Track stock. Add a barcode if applicable. Click Next.
On the Initial Stock step, enter the opening quantity for each branch. Click Next.
On the Review step, verify all details. Click Submit to create the product.

common_tasks.task: Add a product with multiple variants (e.g. sizes)
steps: On the Variants step, fill in the first variant row (e.g. 'Small / SKU-001').
Click Add Variant to add a second row (e.g. 'Large / SKU-002') and fill in its details.
Repeat for as many variants as needed — each must have a unique SKU.
Continue to Initial Stock and enter quantities for each variant at each branch.

common_tasks.task: Set up a product with different base and sell units (e.g. case and bottle)
steps: On the Variants step, set Base unit to Case and Sell unit to Bottle.
Set the conversion factor to the number of bottles per case (e.g. 24).
Stock is tracked in Cases — when a Bottle is sold, the system deducts 1/24 of a Case from inventory.

warnings: SKUs must be unique across your entire catalogue. Duplicate SKUs are rejected on submission — each variant must have a distinct SKU before you can proceed.

Initial stock entered here creates opening stock movement records. If you enter the wrong quantity, correct it with a manual stock adjustment later — there is no way to edit initial stock after submission.
Conversion factor must be a positive number greater than zero. Set it to 1 if base and sell units are the same. An incorrect conversion factor causes quantity errors in stock tracking and reports.

entries.title: Variants
entries.overview: Manage SKU-level records for your products with card and table views featuring hover animations and staggered entrance transitions. Each variant holds its own pricing, unit configuration, barcodes, branch availability, and image. Labeled form fields with Lucide icons guide data entry. Barcode scanning, reassignment, and label printing tools are grouped into collapsible Card panels.

elements.name: KPI – Variants
elements.description: Total variants in scope based on current filters, displayed in a Card with bold numeric styling. This is the count available for label printing when using Select All. If the number does not match your expected catalogue, check the Status or Branch filter.

elements.name: KPI – Active
elements.description: Count of ACTIVE variants in the current page, displayed in a Card with bold numeric styling. Only ACTIVE variants appear in POS search and stock flows. If a variant is missing from the POS, filter here to ACTIVE to confirm it exists and is enabled.

elements.name: KPI – With Barcode
elements.description: Count of variants with at least one active barcode assigned, displayed in a Card with bold numeric styling. Variants without barcodes cannot be found by scanning in the POS — use this number to identify how many variants still need barcodes added or printed.

elements.name: KPI – Branches
elements.description: Number of branches loaded for branch availability management, displayed in a Card with bold numeric styling. Each variant can be marked available or unavailable per branch — this tells you how many branches the current availability context covers.

elements.name: Product Selector (Create form)
elements.description: Required. The parent product this variant belongs to. Only ACTIVE and INACTIVE products appear. Once a variant is saved, its parent product cannot be changed — if you select the wrong product, you will need to create a new variant.

elements.name: Variant Name Input
elements.description: Required display name with a visible label above the field. Appears on receipts and in the POS (e.g. '1 Litre', 'Red – Large', '500ml'). Be specific enough that cashiers can distinguish between variants of the same product at a glance.

elements.name: SKU Input
elements.description: Optional internal stock-keeping unit code used for purchase orders, imports, and inventory tracking. If left blank the system will generate an identifier. Reassigning an SKU later requires a written reason and may trigger an approval workflow.

elements.name: Barcode Input
elements.description: Optional barcode to assign at creation time. Can also be filled by scanning with the Scan Assign New button. Barcodes enable quick product lookup at the POS — variants without barcodes must be found by name search.

elements.name: Scan Assign New Button
elements.description: Activates the device camera to scan a barcode and automatically fills the Barcode Input field with the scanned code. Shown with a Scan icon and tactile press animation. Use this instead of typing the barcode manually to avoid transcription errors.

elements.name: Default Price Input
elements.description: The standard selling price for this variant in TZS. This is what the POS suggests by default when this variant is added to a sale. If a price list applies to the customer, the price list price overrides this default at the point of sale.

elements.name: Min Price Input
elements.description: Floor price for this variant in TZS. If a cashier tries to sell below this price, the POS displays a warning and may require manager approval depending on your business's pricing rule configuration.

elements.name: VAT Mode Selector
elements.description: Controls how VAT is applied to this variant's price: INCLUSIVE (VAT is included in the displayed price), EXCLUSIVE (VAT is added on top at checkout), or EXEMPT (no VAT applies). This setting affects how tax is calculated on receipts and in financial reports.

elements.name: Base Unit Selector
elements.description: The unit in which stock quantities are tracked in the system (e.g. Each, Kg, Litre). Stock movements, adjustments, and counts are all recorded in this unit. Choose it to match how you physically count and store the item.

elements.name: Sell Unit Selector
elements.description: The unit in which the variant is sold (e.g. Pack, Box). Can differ from the base unit.

elements.name: Conversion Factor Input
elements.description: How many base units equal one sell unit. Disabled and locked to 1 when both units are the same.

elements.name: Create Variant Button
elements.description: Saves the new variant and adds it to the catalogue with a tactile press animation and Plus icon. The variant starts with zero stock on hand — make an opening stock adjustment or receive a purchase to add initial stock before selling.

elements.name: Barcode Reassign – Barcode Selector
elements.description: Selects the existing barcode to move to a different variant. The dropdown shows the barcode value and the variant it currently belongs to — confirm you have selected the right code before proceeding.

elements.name: Barcode Reassign – New Variant Selector
elements.description: The variant that will take ownership of the barcode after reassignment. The barcode is unlinked from its current variant and assigned here. The previous variant will have no barcode until one is manually added.

elements.name: Barcode Reassign – Reason Input
elements.description: Required. A written explanation for why this barcode is being moved to a different variant (e.g. 'Old SKU discontinued — barcode reassigned to replacement variant'). Stored in the audit log alongside the reassignment event.

elements.name: Submit Reassignment Button
elements.description: Submits the reassignment with a Check icon and tactile press animation. May return 'approval required' if your business policy requires a review.

elements.name: Scan Start / Restart Button
elements.description: Activates the device camera with a Play icon and tactile press animation. Used for two purposes: scanning a barcode to look up which variant it belongs to, or scanning to fill the barcode field during variant creation. Click Restart if the scanner freezes or loses focus.

elements.name: Scan Stop Button
elements.description: Stops the active camera scan session with a Square icon and returns the scanner to idle. The last scan result remains visible — use this after completing a lookup or barcode assignment.

elements.name: Scan Result Panel
elements.description: Shows the product, variant name, and SKU found for the most recently scanned barcode.

elements.name: Print A4 Labels Button
elements.description: Generates barcode labels for selected variants and triggers an A4 print dialog. Shown with a Printer icon and tactile press animation.

elements.name: Print Thermal Labels Button
elements.description: Generates barcode label sheets formatted for thermal label printers and opens the print dialog with a Printer icon and tactile press animation. Labels are sized for standard thermal roll widths. Only selected (checked) variants are included in the print job.

elements.name: Variant Checkbox (per card)
elements.description: Selects this variant for inclusion in the next label print job. Tick the variants you need labels for, then click Print A4 Labels or Print Thermal Labels. Clear all checkboxes between different print batches to avoid printing unwanted labels.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Filter the variant list by name or SKU code. Search triggers when you press Enter or click the Search button — not automatically while typing. Use this to quickly find a variant for editing, barcode assignment, or to verify details before printing labels.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter by variant status: ACTIVE (available in POS and stock flows), INACTIVE (hidden from POS), or ARCHIVED (soft-deleted, removed from all live flows). If a variant is missing from the POS, filter to INACTIVE to find it.

elements.name: Branch Filter
elements.description: Narrows the variant list to the availability context of a specific branch. Use this when managing which variants are marked available at a particular location, then use the Availability Filter to refine further.

elements.name: Availability Filter
elements.description: Filter to variants that are marked available (or unavailable) in the currently selected branch. Use 'Unavailable' to find variants that have been blocked from sale at this location and decide whether to re-enable them.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card grid and table view for the variants list.

elements.name: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Navigate pages and adjust page size.

elements.name: Create variant modal
elements.description: Opened from the 'Create variant' button in the page header. Centered modal dialog with the full create form (product, name, SKU, barcode, pricing, VAT mode, units, branch availability, track-stock toggle). ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful save.

elements.name: Edit details button (per card)
elements.description: Each variant card has an 'Advanced' edit button that opens the Variant edit modal. Inside: base unit / sell unit / conversion factor, SKU reassign field, and image upload. Barcodes and branch availability remain inline on the card so day-to-day operations stay quick.

elements.name: Variant edit modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog for detailed variant edits. Contains the Unit help panel, unit selectors and conversion factor, SKU reassign field (requires a reason prompt, may route to approval), and image upload. Saves happen live per field — there is no Save button. ESC or backdrop click closes.

elements.name: Generate Barcode button
elements.description: Auto-generates a unique barcode for the variant. The generated code is immediately assigned and displayed as a barcode chip on the card.

elements.name: Stock-on-hand display
elements.description: Shows the current stock quantity for each variant, visible in both card and table views. Helps identify which variants need restocking.

elements.name: Stock warning indicator
elements.description: An AlertTriangle Lucide icon shown in red next to variants whose stock is at or below 5 units. Appears in both card and table views to flag low-stock items.

elements.name: Margin column
elements.description: Color-coded gross margin percentage calculated from price minus cost. Emerald-green for positive margins, red for negative. Visible in both table and card views to help identify unprofitable variants at a glance.

elements.name: Track Stock toggle (per card)
elements.description: An inline toggle on each variant card to enable or disable stock tracking. Changes take effect immediately when toggled.

elements.name: Branch availability checkboxes
elements.description: A grid of checkboxes on each variant card showing which branches the variant is available at. Check or uncheck to control where the variant can be sold.

elements.name: Image upload (per variant)
elements.description: Each variant card has an image upload button with an Upload icon. Click to browse for an image file (JPEG, PNG). The image is displayed as a thumbnail with nvi-img-zoom hover effect on the card and in the table view.

elements.name: Barcode chips
elements.description: Assigned barcodes are shown as rounded-xl chip badges on each variant card. Active barcodes have a gold border, inactive ones are dimmed with a muted border. Multiple barcodes can be assigned to a single variant.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills on each variant. Green for ACTIVE, amber for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED. In card view, the status can be changed via an inline dropdown.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting. Available columns include Product, Variant, SKU, Price, Cost, Margin, Status.

elements.name: ActionButtons (table view)
elements.description: Pencil icon buttons in the actions column of table view. Opens the Variant edit modal for that row.

elements.name: Unit Help Panel
elements.description: An expandable explanation panel in both the create form and inline card edit sections, explaining how base unit, sell unit, and conversion factor work with examples.

before_you_start.text: Parent products must exist — create products first at /catalog/products.
Units must be configured in settings before you can set base/sell units on variants.
catalog.write is required for all create, update, barcode, and image operations.

steps: Select the parent product.
Enter the variant name.
Set base unit and sell unit (and conversion factor if they differ).
Optionally enter a price and barcode.
Click Create Variant.
Tick the checkbox on each variant card you want to label.
Click Print A4 Labels or Print Thermal Labels.
The browser print dialog opens with the generated label sheet.

warnings: Barcode reassignment may require approval — the result panel will indicate if approval is needed before the change takes effect.
SKU reassignment also requires a reason and may trigger an approval workflow.
Conversion factor must be a positive non-zero number when base unit differs from sell unit.
Camera scanning requires the browser to have permission to access the device camera.
Label printing includes all checked variants — clear selections between different print jobs.

related_pages.reason: Products must exist before variants can be created.
View stock levels after setting up variants.

entries.title: Customers
entries.overview: Customer relationship profiles with gradient avatar containers (blue-to-purple), per-contact colored icons (Phone in emerald, Mail in blue, TIN in amber), WhatsApp action in emerald, purple price list pills, and amber outstanding balance warnings. Status dots with glow shadows. Timeline events colored by type (emerald sales, red refunds). Edit mode uses blue ring highlight.

elements.name: KPI — Customers (Users icon, gold)
elements.description: Total number of customer records matching the current filters. Displayed with a gold Users icon container. If no filters are set, this is your total customer count.

elements.name: KPI — Active (UserCheck icon, emerald)
elements.description: Count of customers with ACTIVE status. Displayed with an emerald UserCheck icon container. Only ACTIVE customers appear in POS customer selectors — if a customer is missing at POS, check their status here.

elements.name: KPI — Price list linked (Tag icon, blue)
elements.description: Count of customers with a price list assigned. Displayed with a blue Tag icon container. These customers automatically get their custom prices applied at POS when they are selected during a sale.

elements.name: KPI — Current filter (Filter icon, amber)
elements.description: Shows which status filter is currently active, or 'All statuses' if no filter is set. Displayed with an amber Filter icon container.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filter customers by name, phone, or email. Search triggers when you press Enter or click the Search button — not automatically while typing. Use this to locate a specific customer quickly when your list is large.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Narrow the list to ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED customers. ARCHIVED customers are hidden from POS selectors but their purchase history and credit records are preserved.

elements.name: Balance due filter
elements.description: Filter to customers with an outstanding credit balance (Yes) or a clear balance (No). Use Yes to identify all customers who currently owe money — useful before making collection calls or running credit settlement sessions.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all active search and filter inputs and reloads the full customer list. Use this to start a fresh search after narrowing results too far.

elements.name: View toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card layout (profile cards with full detail and activity timelines) and table layout (compact rows for scanning many customers). Edit actions work the same from either view — they open the Customer edit modal.

elements.name: Customer profile card
elements.description: Each customer is displayed as a profile card with a large avatar (deterministic colour based on name), bold name with a coloured status dot (green=active, grey=inactive/archived), icon-led contact row (Phone, Mail, Hash for TIN), price list tag badge, expandable notes, and an activity timeline toggle. Cards use hover animation and edit mode shows a gold ring highlight.

elements.name: Contact row (card view)
elements.description: Compact icon-led contact information: Phone icon with number and WhatsApp link button (MessageCircle icon), Mail icon with email, Hash icon with TIN. Missing contact info shows in muted text.

elements.name: Price list badge (card view)
elements.description: If a customer has a price list assigned, it appears as a blue Tag badge showing the price list name. Helps staff quickly identify customers with negotiated pricing.

elements.name: Activity snapshot (card view)
elements.description: Shows 'Last purchase: X ago' or 'No purchases yet' in muted text with a ShoppingCart icon. Only appears after the timeline has been loaded for the customer.

elements.name: Notes section (expandable, card view)
elements.description: Customer notes appear as a truncated preview by default. Click 'Show notes' to expand the full text. Uses expand animation.

elements.name: Name field (create form)
elements.description: Required. Uses TextInput with label. The customer's display name — shown in POS customer selectors, receipts, and reports.

elements.name: Phone field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Uses TextInput with label. Includes a hint about country code format (+255...).

elements.name: Email field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Uses TextInput with label and email type. Stored for reference and for any future email notification features.

elements.name: TIN field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Uses TextInput with label. Required for issuing formal tax invoices to business customers.

elements.name: Notes field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Uses TextInput with label. Free-text notes visible to all staff who can see the customer record.

elements.name: Default price list (create form)
elements.description: Uses AsyncSmartSelect with label. Optionally link the customer to a price list. Price lists must be created first on the Price Lists page.

elements.name: Create Customer button (UserPlus icon)
elements.description: Saves the new customer record with a UserPlus icon. Uses press animation. The customer is immediately available in POS customer selectors. Requires the customers.create permission.

elements.name: Edit button (Pencil icon, per customer)
elements.description: Opens the Customer edit modal centered on screen. Works the same from either card or table view. Requires the customers.update permission.

elements.name: Archive button (Archive icon, per customer)
elements.description: Sets the customer status to ARCHIVED with a confirmation dialog. The customer is removed from POS selectors but all history and credit records are preserved. Requires the customers.update permission.

elements.name: Anonymise button (EyeOff icon, per customer)
elements.description: Permanently removes all personal data with a confirmation dialog. Uses danger variant styling. Transaction history is preserved but is no longer identifiable. Requires the customers.anonymize permission.

elements.name: Save / Cancel buttons (edit modal)
elements.description: Save commits the edited fields and refreshes the list on success. Cancel (or ESC, or clicking the backdrop) discards all changes and closes the modal without saving.

elements.name: Pagination controls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of customers. Adjust page size as needed. Use this if a customer does not appear in the initial results.

elements.name: Create customer button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with UserPlus icon. Opens the Customer create modal. Only shown to users with the customers.create permission.

elements.name: Customer create modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Create customer header button. Contains name, phone, email, TIN, notes, and default price list fields. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful create.

elements.name: Customer edit modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Pencil action on any customer. Shows an avatar alongside the same fields as create plus a status selector (ACTIVE / INACTIVE / ARCHIVED). Save commits and refreshes the list; ESC or backdrop click cancels.

elements.name: Activity Timeline (expandable)
elements.description: Click the Clock icon toggle on a customer card to expand the activity timeline. Shows purchase and refund history as a mini vertical timeline with dots, amounts, and relative timestamps. Uses expand and stagger animations.

elements.name: WhatsApp link (MessageCircle icon)
elements.description: An inline link next to the phone number that opens WhatsApp with the customer's number pre-filled. Only appears when the customer has a phone number on file. Uses emerald styling.

elements.name: AvatarInitials (large in cards, xs in table)
elements.description: A circular avatar showing the customer's initials with deterministic colour. Large size in card view serves as the visual anchor. Extra-small in table view for compact rows.

elements.name: Status dot
elements.description: A small coloured dot next to the customer name. Green for ACTIVE, gold for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED. Replaces the previous StatusBadge pills in card view.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Name, Phone, Email, and Status.

elements.name: Empty state (floating Users icon)
elements.description: When no customers exist, a centred empty state with a floating Users icon and a prompt to create the first customer.

elements.name: Sensitive data warning
elements.description: A gold hint text at the top of the page reminding users that customer data is sensitive and should be handled carefully.

before_you_start.text: You need the customers.create permission to add new customers.
You need the customers.update permission to edit or archive a customer.
You need the customers.anonymize permission to permanently remove a customer's personal data.
Price lists must be created first on the Price Lists page before they can be assigned to customers here.
Anonymisation is irreversible — personal data cannot be recovered after this action. Use it only for genuine privacy or legal deletion requests.

steps: Fill in the Name (required) and optionally phone, email, TIN, and notes.
If this customer has negotiated prices, select their price list from Default price list.
Click Create Customer. The customer is immediately available at POS.

common_tasks.task: Assign a price list to a customer
steps: Find the customer card and click Edit.
Select the price list from the Default price list field.
Click Save. The next time this customer is selected at POS, their custom prices apply automatically.
Find the customer card and click Archive.
Confirm the action. The customer is removed from POS selectors but all history and credit records are kept.
Find the customer card and click Anonymise.
Read the warning carefully — this action is permanent.
Confirm. All personal data is removed immediately. Transaction history is retained but is no longer personally identifiable.
Set Balance due filter to Yes.
The list shows only customers who currently owe money.
Use this list to run credit settlement sessions or make collection calls.

warnings: Anonymisation is permanent and cannot be undone. Once personal data is removed, it cannot be recovered — confirm with your manager before using this feature.
