Archiving a customer hides them from POS but does not delete their purchase history or credit balance. The credit balance is still visible here for settlement.

Assigning a price list to a customer overrides branch-level pricing at POS. If the customer's prices seem wrong, check both the customer's price list assignment here and the branch's price list assignment on the Branch Settings page.

entries.title: Expenses
entries.overview: Expense tracking with red amount hero (money leaving the business), per-category colored icon containers across 7 distinct colors (Shipping blue, Utilities amber, Rent purple, Payroll emerald, Transfer Fee cyan, General neutral, Other muted). Create form organized into colored sections (blue Details, red Financial, amber Notes). Transfer fee links show source-to-destination with colored branch pills.

elements.name: KPI strip
elements.description: Four metric cards at the top: Total expenses (count), Total amount (sum in red), Category filter (current selection), and Branch scope (current branch). Each has a color-coded icon container.

elements.name: View toggle (cards / table)
elements.description: Switch between card view and table view. Card view shows receipt-style cards with amount as the hero element. Table view shows a sortable data table wrapped in a card.

elements.name: Expense card
elements.description: Receipt-style card showing the expense amount in large red text as the visual anchor, a color-coded category badge with icon (General=Package, Shipping=Truck, Utilities=Zap, Rent=Home, Payroll=Users, Transfer Fee=ArrowLeftRight, Other=HelpCircle), branch with MapPin icon, relative time with actual date, receipt reference with FileText icon, and expandable notes.

elements.name: Category badge
elements.description: Color-coded pill on each expense: General (grey), Shipping (blue), Utilities (amber), Rent (purple), Payroll (emerald), Transfer Fee (yellow), Other (muted). Each includes a contextual Lucide icon.

elements.name: Transfer fee link
elements.description: For TRANSFER_FEE category expenses, shows the source and destination branch with arrow icons. Helps trace which inter-branch transfer generated the fee.

elements.name: "Create expense" button (header)
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a modal for creating an expense. The modal has three grouped sections: Details (branch, category, title), Financial (amount, expense date, receipt ref), and Notes (free-text note). Previously this was an inline collapsible-section form on the page — it has been moved into a modal to reduce page clutter.

elements.name: Branch selector (create form)
elements.description: Choose which branch this expense belongs to. Expenses are tracked per branch so they appear correctly in branch-level cost reports. You must have access to the selected branch.

elements.name: Category selector
elements.description: Select the category that best describes the expense. RENT — premises costs; UTILITIES — electricity, water, internet; PAYROLL — staff wages and salaries; SHIPPING — delivery and freight costs; TRANSFER_FEE — bank or mobile money transaction fees; GENERAL — anything that does not fit a specific category; OTHER — use sparingly and add a note. Category is used for grouping in reports — choose consistently for meaningful expense analysis.

elements.name: Amount input
elements.description: The full amount of the expense. Required — cannot be zero. For partial payments or installments, record each payment as a separate expense entry.

elements.name: Currency input
elements.description: The currency the expense was paid in. Defaults to your business currency. If you pay expenses in a foreign currency, enter the amount as-paid — the system stores it without converting, so use your reporting tools to handle multi-currency reconciliation.

elements.name: Note input
elements.description: Optional free-text description of what the expense was for. Be specific — for example 'April rent for Main Street branch' or 'Electricity bill — ref INV-2024-04'. This note appears in the expense list and in exports. Long notes are truncated on cards with a show more toggle.

elements.name: Receipt reference input
elements.description: Optional reference number from a physical receipt or invoice. Use this to link the system record to a paper document — for example the invoice number from your landlord or utility provider. Helps with audits and reconciliation.

elements.name: Expense date picker
elements.description: The date the expense was incurred (not necessarily today). Defaults to today. Change this if you are recording a past expense — the date determines which reporting period the expense falls into.

elements.name: Save Expense button
elements.description: Creates the expense record immediately with a Receipt icon. Expenses cannot be edited after saving — verify the amount, date, and category before clicking. If a mistake is made, you will need to record a correcting entry.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Filter the expense list by keyword. Searches across the note text, receipt reference, and category names.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restrict the expense list to a specific branch. Useful for branch managers reviewing their own operating costs.

elements.name: Category filter
elements.description: Filter the expense list by category — for example, show only RENT or PAYROLL expenses. Use this when totalling a specific cost type for a period.

elements.name: Status filter (transfer/direct)
elements.description: Filter expenses by source: 'transfer' shows auto-created transfer fee expenses, 'direct' shows manually created expenses. Helps separate operational costs from transfer fees.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restrict results to expenses incurred within the selected date window. Shown with CalendarRange icons. Use this for monthly reporting or end-of-period cost reviews.

elements.name: Empty state
elements.description: When no expenses exist, shows a floating Receipt icon with a message encouraging the user to record their first expense.

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of expense records. Use filters first to narrow the result set before paging.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Date, Branch, Category, and Amount.

elements.name: STOCK_COST category
elements.description: A new expense category for stock purchase costs. Created automatically when stock is added via adjustments with purchase-type reasons (Unrecorded Purchase, Initial Stock) or opening stock imports.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each expense has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. EXP-001). Shown below the branch on cards and in the table.

before_you_start.text: You need the expenses.write permission to record expenses.
The branch must exist before you can record an expense against it. Create branches from the Branch Settings page.

steps: Select the branch the rent applies to.
Set Category to RENT and enter the amount and currency.
Set Expense date to the date the rent was paid.
Add the landlord's invoice number in Receipt reference (optional but recommended).
Click Save Expense.

common_tasks.task: View all utility expenses for a branch this month
steps: Set Branch filter to the specific branch.
Set Category filter to UTILITIES.
Set the date range From/To to cover the current month.
The filtered list shows all utility expenses in that period.
Set Category to TRANSFER_FEE.
Enter the fee amount and the currency it was charged in.
Add a note identifying the related transaction — for example 'Transfer fee for supplier payment to ABC Ltd on 2026-03-01'.
Click Save Expense.

warnings: Expenses cannot be edited after saving. If an amount or date was entered incorrectly, record a correcting entry and flag the error for your accountant — there is no delete function either.
Expenses do not affect stock levels or the purchase ledger. For goods received from suppliers, use the Purchase Orders workflow — not Expenses.
The currency is stored as-entered without conversion. When reconciling foreign-currency expenses in reports, filter by currency and apply your own exchange rate calculation.

entries.title: Exports
entries.overview: Data export command center with status-colored job borders (amber pending, blue running, emerald completed, red failed) and amber worker status indicators. Run bulk exports with visual job tracking and download completed files as CSV, Excel, or PDF.

elements.name: Export type selector
elements.description: Choose what data to export. Each export type has a unique color identity for visual distinction. STOCK — current stock levels by variant and branch. PRODUCTS — full product and variant catalogue. OPENING_STOCK — opening stock movement records. PRICE_UPDATES — price change history. SUPPLIERS — full supplier directory. BRANCHES — branch list with details. CUSTOMERS — customer directory. RECEIPTS — sales receipt records. PURCHASES — purchase records. AUDIT_LOGS — full system audit trail (requires acknowledgement checkbox). USERS — user list. ROLES — role and permission definitions (inside the modal).

elements.name: Branch selector (export)
elements.description: Optionally restrict the export to a specific branch. Leave blank to export data across all branches. Stock and receipt exports are especially useful filtered by branch for branch-level reviews (inside the modal).

elements.name: Audit acknowledgement checkbox
elements.description: Required before you can create an AUDIT_LOGS export. By ticking this, you confirm that you understand the compliance implications of exporting a full audit trail, which contains sensitive operational history (inside the modal).

elements.name: "Create export" button (header) + modal
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a modal for requesting a new export job. The modal contains type selector (Stock / Products / Audit Logs / Full Data Dump, etc.), format selector (CSV / Excel / PDF), branch selector (hidden for EXPORT_ON_EXIT type), and an audit acknowledgement checkbox (shown only for AUDIT_LOGS type). Previously this was an inline card on the page — moved into a modal to reduce clutter. Submission queues an async background job that the worker picks up and runs; results appear in the Export Jobs list below.

elements.name: Search input (job history)
elements.description: Filter the export job history by keyword, matching against the export name, type, and description. Useful when looking for a specific export you ran previously among many job records.

elements.name: Status filter (job history)
elements.description: Filter export jobs by status. Each status has a matching border color on job cards. PENDING (amber) — queued but not yet running. RUNNING (blue) — currently being processed. COMPLETED (emerald) — finished and ready to download. FAILED (red) — encountered an error; check the worker status panel and retry.

elements.name: Type filter (job history)
elements.description: Narrows the job list to a specific export type (e.g. STOCK, SALES, CUSTOMERS, AUDIT). Use this when you need to find all exports of one kind — for example, every stock snapshot exported this quarter.

elements.name: Branch filter (job list)
elements.description: Narrows the job list to exports that were scoped to a specific branch. Use this when reviewing export history for a particular location or confirming that a branch-level report was generated.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Limits the job history to exports created within the selected date window. Set this to find exports from a specific reporting period without scrolling through the full history.

elements.name: Download button (per job)
elements.description: Downloads the completed export file (CSV or ZIP) for that job. Only available once the job status is COMPLETED.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout (more detail per job including status badge, file size, and download link) and table layout (more compact, better for scanning many jobs at once).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Numbered page navigation for the export job history list. Each page loads 25 job records.

elements.name: Worker status panel
elements.description: Shows the current state of the export processing queue with amber-themed styling: how many jobs are pending, how many are running, and how many have failed. If jobs are stuck in PENDING for a long time, or if FAILED count is rising, the export worker may need to be restarted — contact support.

before_you_start.text: You need the exports.manage permission to create exports.
Large exports run asynchronously — the job appears in the history list below and can take several minutes before the Download button becomes available.

steps: Set Export type to STOCK.
Optionally select a branch to restrict the export to one location.
Click Create Export. The job appears in the history list below.
Wait for the status to change to COMPLETED, then click Download.
In the export job history, set Status filter to COMPLETED.
Find the export you want using the Type filter or Date range.
Click Download to save the CSV or ZIP file.

warnings: AUDIT_LOGS export requires the acknowledgement checkbox to be ticked — this is a compliance safeguard. The exported file contains sensitive operational history.
If export jobs are stuck in PENDING status or the Failed count is rising in the worker status panel, the export processing worker may be down. Contact support to investigate.

entries.title: Import Data
entries.overview: Guided step-by-step data import page. Choose from 11 import types (categories, products, opening stock, price updates, status updates, suppliers, branches, users, customers, units, stock counts), review the format with a column guide and tips, upload or paste CSV data, preview validation results, and apply the import. Each import type has its own icon, color, and detailed documentation built into the page.

elements.name: Import type cards
elements.description: A grid of 11 selectable cards, one for each import type. Each card shows an icon, the import type name, a one-sentence description, and a column count badge. Click a card to select that import type — the card highlights with a colored ring. Selecting a type reveals the format guide and upload steps below.

elements.name: Format guide — What this import does
elements.description: Plain-language explanation of what the selected import creates or updates. Written for non-technical business users. Appears after selecting an import type.

elements.name: Column guide table
elements.description: A table listing every column for the selected template: column name, whether it is required or optional, a description of what goes in that column, and an example value. For templates with dynamic branch columns, your branch names appear as additional columns with explanations.

elements.name: Important notes panel
elements.description: Tips, warnings, and common mistakes for the selected import type. Displayed in an amber-tinted panel to draw attention. Includes what happens when the import is applied.

elements.name: Download blank template button
elements.description: Downloads a CSV file with just the column headers for the selected import type. For opening stock, price updates, status updates, and stock counts, the template is pre-filled with your existing data from the server.

elements.name: Download example button
elements.description: Downloads a CSV file with column headers and sample data rows. Use this to understand the expected format before preparing your own data.

elements.name: CSV file upload
elements.description: Click to select a .csv or .txt file from your computer. The file contents are loaded into the text area below. You can also paste CSV data directly into the text area instead.

elements.name: CSV text area
elements.description: Paste or review your CSV content here. The system auto-previews after you stop typing for 1 second. You can also upload a file using the button above.

elements.name: Preview import button
elements.description: Validates your CSV data and shows a preview with valid/invalid row counts and detailed error messages. Use this to check your data before applying.

elements.name: Apply import button
elements.description: Applies the validated import to your inventory. Disabled if there are validation errors. Shows a confirmation dialog before proceeding. This action is irreversible — records are created or updated immediately.

elements.name: Preview results panel
elements.description: Shows four stat cards (total rows, valid, invalid, errors), a progress bar showing validity percentage, and either a green success message or a scrollable error table with row numbers and issue descriptions.

elements.name: Copy errors button
elements.description: Copies all import validation errors to your clipboard in 'Row X: message' format. Useful for pasting into a spreadsheet to fix issues.

elements.name: Import history section
elements.description: Collapsible section at the bottom showing past imports with type, valid/invalid counts, who ran it, and when. Useful for auditing what data has been imported and by whom.

before_you_start.text: You need the exports.manage permission to run imports.
Prepare your CSV file carefully — match the exact column headers for the import type. Download a template or example to see the expected format.
For opening stock, price updates, status updates, and stock counts, the template is pre-filled with your existing data. Modify what you need and re-upload.

common_tasks.task: Import products from a CSV file
steps: Click the Products card in Step 1.
Read the format guide and column descriptions in Step 2.
Download the blank template or example to understand the format.
Prepare your CSV with the correct columns.
Upload the file or paste the CSV content in Step 3.
Review the preview results — fix any errors and re-upload if needed.
When preview shows zero errors, click Apply import.
Click the Price updates card in Step 1.
Click Download blank template — this downloads your current prices pre-filled.
Open the CSV in a spreadsheet, change the prices you need.
Upload the modified file in Step 3.
Preview to confirm only the changed rows are detected.
Click Apply import to update prices.

common_tasks.task: Set opening stock for a new branch
steps: Click the Opening stock card in Step 1.
Download the template — it shows all your variants with branch columns.
Fill in the stock quantities for the new branch column.
Upload and preview to check for errors.
Apply the import to create opening balance stock movements.

warnings: Apply import is irreversible — imported records are created or updated immediately. Always preview first and ensure zero errors before clicking Apply.
Opening stock import ADDS to existing stock — it does not replace. If a variant already has stock, the imported quantity is added on top.
For products import, variants without a price cannot be sold at POS. Make sure to include prices for all sellable variants.

entries.title: Accept Invitation
entries.overview: Use this page if you have been invited to join a business by an owner or manager. The invitation link sent to your email will take you here automatically with the token pre-filled. Enter your name and set a password to create your account.

before_you_start.text: You must have received an invitation email from a business owner or manager.
If you are registering a new business (not joining one), use the Sign Up page instead.

elements.name: Token field
elements.description: The invitation token from your email. If you arrived via the invitation link, this is pre-filled and cannot be edited. If you are entering the token manually, paste it from the email.

elements.name: Full Name field
elements.description: Enter your full name. This is how you will appear to other users in the system.

elements.name: Password field
elements.description: Set a password for your new account. Must meet the minimum requirements shown below.

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Create Account button
elements.description: Submits the invitation token, your name, and your password to create your account. If successful, you will be redirected to Login after a moment.

elements.name: Token read-only state
elements.description: When the invitation token is provided in the URL, the token field is pre-filled and read-only.

elements.name: Password requirements hint
elements.description: Shows the minimum password requirements below the password field.

elements.name: Auto-redirect on success
elements.description: After successful invitation acceptance, you are automatically redirected to the login page after a brief delay.

common_tasks.task: Accept an invitation and create your account
steps: Click the invitation link in the email you received.
Confirm the token field is filled (it should be automatic).
Enter your full name.
Set a password that meets the requirements.
Click Create Account.
You will be redirected to Login — sign in with the email the invitation was sent to and your new password.

warnings: Invitation tokens expire after a set period. If your token has expired, ask the business owner to send a new invitation.
The email you use to log in after this step is the email the invitation was sent to — not one you enter on this page.
If the invited email already has an account, the existing user is linked to the business automatically — no new account is created. The invite page will confirm the link and redirect to login.

related_pages.reason: After creating your account, log in here.

entries.title: Login
entries.overview: This is the sign-in page. Enter your email and password to access your business account. If your account is linked to more than one business, you will be asked to choose which business to open.

before_you_start.text: You need a registered account. If you don't have one, sign up first.
If you were invited by a business, use the Accept Invite page instead.

elements.name: Email field
elements.description: Enter the email address you registered with.

elements.name: Password field
elements.description: Enter your account password.

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Forgot password link
elements.description: Takes you to the Password Reset page where you can request a reset link by email.

elements.name: Sign In button
elements.description: Submits your email and password. If correct, you are taken directly to the Dashboard.

elements.name: Business selector
elements.description: Appears only if your email is linked to more than one business. Choose which business you want to open.
elements.notes: The system remembers the last business you used and pre-selects it for you.

elements.name: Continue button
elements.description: Appears after the business selector. Confirms your business choice and completes the login.

elements.name: Create Business link
elements.description: Takes you to the Sign Up page if you want to register a new business account.

elements.name: Two-stage login flow
elements.description: Login has two stages: first enter email and password, then select which business to log into if you belong to multiple businesses.

elements.name: Suspended business indicator
elements.description: In the business selector, suspended businesses show a SUSPENDED label and are disabled — you cannot log into a suspended business.

elements.name: Last business memory
elements.description: The system remembers which business you last logged into and pre-selects it in the dropdown.

elements.name: Return-to redirect
elements.description: If you were redirected to login from another page, you will be sent back to that page after successful login.

steps: Enter your email address.
Enter your password.
Click Sign In.
If prompted, select your business and click Continue.

warnings: If you enter the wrong password too many times, your account may be temporarily locked.
If you belong to multiple businesses, you must select one to proceed — you cannot be logged into more than one business at the same time.

related_pages.reason: Email verification is required before you can log in for the first time.
First-time login redirects here to complete business setup.
You land here after a successful login.

entries.title: Notes
entries.overview: Notion-style note management with amber tint on pinned notes, colored visibility buttons (red Private, blue Branch, emerald Business), cycling tag colors across 6 hues, per-type colored link chips, and purple reminder section with left border accent. Editor toolbar uses blue active states. Templates shown with purple LayoutTemplate icons.

elements.name: Total notes KPI (StickyNote icon, gold)
elements.description: The total count of notes under the current filters. Displayed with a StickyNote icon in a gold-tinted icon well.

elements.name: Active KPI (CheckCircle2 icon, emerald)
elements.description: Count of notes that have not been archived. This is the working set of notes that appear in the default list view.

elements.name: Linked notes KPI (Link icon, blue)
elements.description: Count of notes that have at least one entity link attached. Linked notes surface contextually when viewing the related record.

elements.name: Pinned KPI (Pin icon, amber)
elements.description: Count of notes currently pinned. Pinned notes always appear at the top of the card grid for quick access.

elements.name: Search input (Search icon)
elements.description: Filters notes by keyword in title and body. Has a Search icon prefix. Results update as you type with 350ms debounce. Combine with Tag or Visibility filters for precise searching.

elements.name: Tag filter (Tag icon)
elements.description: Narrows the list to notes carrying a specific tag. Has a Tag icon prefix. Tags are free-text labels you assign when creating a note — consistent tagging makes this filter powerful.

elements.name: Visibility filter
elements.description: Shows only notes with a selected visibility level: PRIVATE (yours alone), BRANCH (shared with a specific branch), or BUSINESS (visible to all users). PRIVATE notes are hidden from everyone else including admins.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts the list to notes scoped to a specific branch. Only relevant when Visibility is BRANCH or unfiltered.

elements.name: "New note" button (header) + modal
elements.description: Accent-colored button in the page header opens a modal for composing a note. The modal contains: title input, 3-state visibility selector (PRIVATE/BRANCH/BUSINESS) with color-coded active states, rich-text editor (with Markdown preview toggle), branch selector (enabled only when visibility=BRANCH), tags input, links subsection (type dropdown + typeahead + add-button + colored chips for Product/Variant/Branch/Supplier/Customer/PurchaseOrder/Purchase/Transfer with remove-on-click), and a conditional "Save as template" sub-action visible only when title+body are filled. Previously this was an inline collapsible-section form — moved into a modal. The same modal handles both create and edit flows, opened via editingId state or the header button.

elements.name: Note card (card view)
elements.description: Each note renders as a content-preview card with: pin indicator (top-right, gold when pinned, hidden until hover when unpinned), bold title with visibility badge (Lock/Building2/Globe icon + label), 3-line body preview (HTML stripped), coloured tag chips with Tag icon, indicators row showing link count (Link icon), reminder count (Bell icon), shared badge (Share2 icon), author name and relative time (Clock icon). Cards use nvi-card-hover lift animation. Click to expand inline.

elements.name: Expanded card detail
elements.description: When a card is clicked, it expands to span the full grid width showing: full rendered HTML body, status badge, branch name, linked entity chips (each with a type-specific icon: Package=Product, Layers=Variant, Building2=Branch, Truck=Supplier, Users=Customer, ShoppingCart=PurchaseOrder, FileText=Purchase, ArrowLeftRight=Transfer), reminder panel, sharing panel, and edit/archive action buttons.

elements.name: Title input (TextInput)
elements.description: A short headline summarising the note. Uses the standard TextInput component with label. Appears prominently in the card and search results (inside the modal).

elements.name: Rich text editor toolbar
elements.description: The note body uses a Tiptap rich text editor. The toolbar uses Lucide icon buttons: Bold, Italic, List (bullet), Heading3. Active formatting shows a gold highlight. The editor has a minimum height of 120px (inside the modal).

elements.name: Visibility selector (icon buttons)
elements.description: Three toggle buttons showing Lock (Private), Building2 (Branch), Globe (Business) icons with labels. The active option has a gold border and glow. Controls who can read the note (inside the modal).

elements.name: Branch selector (note form)
elements.description: Only active when Visibility is BRANCH. Determines which branch's users can see this note (inside the modal).

elements.name: Tags input (Tag icon prefix)
elements.description: Enter tags as a comma-separated list. Has a Tag icon prefix. Tags are free-form — the Tag filter uses these exact values, so consistency matters (inside the modal).

elements.name: Link type selector
elements.description: Choose the entity type to link: Product, Variant, Branch, Supplier, Customer, PurchaseOrder, Purchase, or Transfer (inside the modal).

elements.name: TypeaheadInput (links)
elements.description: After selecting a link type, search for the specific record. Results appear as you type — select one to stage it (inside the modal).

elements.name: Link chips (icon + name + X)
elements.description: Linked entities display as rounded chips with a type-specific icon, entity name, and a red X on hover to remove. Each chip uses nvi-press for tactile feedback (inside the modal).

elements.name: Save / Update button (Send icon, CTA)
elements.description: Creates the note or saves changes. Shows a Send icon and spinner during save. Uses nvi-cta styling.

elements.name: Save as template (Bookmark icon)
elements.description: Appears when title and body are filled. Enter a template name and click to save the current form as a reusable template (inside the modal).

elements.name: Body preview toggle (Eye/Pencil icon)
elements.description: Toggle between editing mode (Pencil icon) and preview mode (Eye icon). Preview renders formatted HTML/markdown output (inside the modal).

elements.name: Template card grid
elements.description: Grid of saved templates — "Use template" applies it to the current note modal; "Delete template" removes the template. **Fix**: delete template now prompts a confirmation dialog (previously a silent one-click delete).

elements.name: Reminder panel (Bell icon header)
elements.description: Expandable panel inside an expanded note card: schedule a reminder via DateTimePickerInput + channel checkboxes (IN_APP / EMAIL / WHATSAPP — availability gated by subscription tier). **Fix**: cancelling a scheduled reminder now prompts a confirmation dialog (previously a silent one-click delete). Kept inline per convention (per-card context, no modal extraction).

elements.name: Sharing panel (Share2 icon header)
elements.description: Expandable panel inside an expanded note card: share the note with a specific user via dropdown, view existing share list with remove buttons. **Fix**: unshare action now prompts a confirmation dialog (previously silent). Kept inline per convention (per-card context, no modal extraction).

elements.name: Pin button (Pin icon)
elements.description: Pin icon in the top-right of each card. Gold and filled when pinned, hidden until hover when unpinned. Pinned notes sort to the top of the grid. In table view, pin icon appears in the first column.

elements.name: Edit button (Pencil icon)
elements.description: Pencil icon on a note card opens the same note modal pre-populated with that note's data for editing.

elements.name: Archive button (Archive icon)
elements.description: Archive icon on a note card prompts a confirmation dialog before soft-archiving the note (status → ARCHIVED). Manager permission + ownership-based authorization enforced on backend.

elements.name: ViewToggle (cards/table)
elements.description: Switch between card grid layout (content previews with expand) and table layout (compact rows with icons for visibility, links, reminders).

elements.name: Empty state (StickyNote icon, floating)
elements.description: When no notes exist, shows a large floating StickyNote icon with a title and description inviting the user to create their first note, plus a New Note CTA button.

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Cursor-based page navigation with page size selector.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'notes.write' permission to create or edit notes.
To link a note to an entity (e.g. a supplier), that record must already exist in the system.
PRIVATE notes are only visible to you — not even admins can read them. Choose BUSINESS or BRANCH if you want to share.

common_tasks.task: Create a note linked to a supplier
steps: Enter a title and body describing the issue or observation.
Set visibility to BUSINESS or BRANCH as appropriate.
In the Links section, select 'Supplier' as the link type.
Type the supplier's name in the typeahead and select the result.
Click 'Add Link', then 'Save Note'.

common_tasks.task: Add a reminder to an existing note
steps: Find the note in the list and click 'View/Edit'.
Open the Reminder panel.
Pick a date and time using the DateTimePicker.
Select the desired notification channels (IN_APP, EMAIL, and/or WHATSAPP).
Click 'Add Reminder', then 'Update Note'.

common_tasks.task: Find all urgent notes for a specific branch
steps: Set the Tag filter to 'urgent' (or your team's equivalent tag).
Set the Branch filter to the branch in question.
Review results — use ViewToggle to see more notes per screen if needed.

warnings: PRIVATE notes are visible only to you — even business owners and admins cannot read them. Do not use PRIVATE for notes that your team needs to act on.
Archived notes disappear from the default list. They are not deleted, but there is no 'archived' view toggle — you would need to know the note exists to find it via search.
Reminders fire once at the scheduled time and do not repeat. If you need recurring reminders, create multiple reminder entries.

entries.title: Notifications
entries.overview: Your notification command center — a real-time stream with live updates via server-sent events. Four KPI cards provide at-a-glance intelligence: Unread (amber Bell icon), Security alerts (red ShieldAlert icon), Action required (gold AlertTriangle icon), and Total (blue Inbox icon). Below the KPIs, a filters card lets you search, filter by status/priority, and set date ranges. Five tabs organize the stream: All (every notification), Security (with a red unread-count badge when alerts are pending), Unread, Action Required, and Read. Security notifications display with red left borders and ShieldAlert icons within the same card layout as regular notifications. Date grouping (Today/Yesterday/This Week/Older) applies within each tab. Notifications arrive instantly — a pulsing green Live badge in the header confirms the SSE connection is active.

elements.name: Unread KPI
elements.description: Amber Card with Bell icon container showing count of unread notifications. This is the primary attention number — when non-zero, the navigation bell badge also shows. Reading or bulk-marking as read decrements this counter.

elements.name: Security alerts KPI
elements.description: Red Card with ShieldAlert icon container showing total security alert count. Covers login detections, permission changes, token reuse, and suspicious activity. Non-zero means the Security Alerts zone is visible.

elements.name: Action required KPI
elements.description: Gold Card with AlertTriangle icon container showing count of ACTION_REQUIRED priority notifications. These are tasks blocked until you act — approvals, sync conflicts, etc. Aim to keep this at zero.

elements.name: Total KPI
elements.description: Blue Card with Inbox icon container showing total notification count across all priorities and statuses.

elements.name: Live connection badge
elements.description: Green pulsing dot with Wifi icon and 'Live' text in the page header when the SSE stream is connected. Switches to amber 'Reconnecting...' with WifiOff icon if the connection drops. Reconnection is automatic with exponential backoff.

elements.name: Sound toggle
elements.description: Volume2/VolumeX icon button in the header actions area. Toggles the audio ping (880Hz tone) when new notifications arrive via the real-time stream. Preference persists in localStorage.

elements.name: Notification preferences link
elements.description: Settings icon button in the header that navigates to Business Settings where you configure which notification types you receive.

elements.name: Filters card
elements.description: Card-wrapped ListFilters with search input, Status filter (All/Unread/Read), Priority filter (All/Action Required/Warning/Info/Security), and From/To date range pickers. All inputs use rounded-xl styling with nvi-border tokens.

elements.name: Stream tabs
elements.description: Pill-style Tabs component below the filters with five options: All, Security, Unread, Action Required, Read. The Security tab shows a red badge with the unread security count when there are pending security alerts. Each tab filters the unified notification stream — security notifications appear inline with their red left borders and ShieldAlert icons. When on the Security tab, a 'Mark all security read' button appears in the bulk actions area.

elements.name: Bulk actions bar
elements.description: Appears when items are selected (nvi-pop animation). Shows Select All checkbox, selection count, and three action buttons with icons: Mark Read (Eye), Archive (Archive), and Mark All Read (CheckCheck, gold CTA). When nothing is selected but unread items exist, a minimal bar shows Select All and Mark All Read.

elements.name: Date group headers
elements.description: Notifications are grouped by date with section headers: Today, Yesterday, This week, Older. Each header has a Clock icon, uppercase label, and a horizontal divider line.

elements.name: Notification card
elements.description: Each notification is a Card with: left border color indicating priority (red=ACTION_REQUIRED, amber=WARNING, blue=INFO), a checkbox for selection, an event-type icon (ShoppingCart for sales, Package for stock, Truck for transfers, User for users, Key for auth, etc.), bold title with unread gold dot, priority StatusBadge, message text, relative time + absolute timestamp with Clock icon, and an Eye button to mark read (visible only on unread items). Cards use nvi-card-hover animation. Read items show at 75% opacity.

elements.name: Mark Read button (per item)
elements.description: Eye icon button on unread notification cards. Marks a single notification as read with nvi-press animation. Shows a dots spinner while processing.

elements.name: Empty state
elements.description: Shown when no notifications match the current tab/filter combination. Floating Bell icon (nvi-float) with 'All caught up' title and 'No notifications right now' description.

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Cursor-based pagination at the bottom. The inbox loads 20 items per page by default with a page size selector.

before_you_start.text: No special permissions are required — every user has a personal notification inbox.
The real-time stream reconnects automatically if the connection drops. Notifications sent during a gap are delivered on reconnect.
Security alerts should be reviewed as soon as possible — they may indicate unauthorised access.

steps: Click 'Mark All Read' to clear the entire inbox at once.
Alternatively, filter by Status = UNREAD, select all with the Select All checkbox, then click 'Mark Selected Read'.

common_tasks.task: Find and respond to an action-required notification
steps: Use the Priority filter and select ACTION_REQUIRED.
Review each item — many include a direct link to the relevant page (e.g. an approval).
Click the link or navigate to the relevant module to complete the action.

common_tasks.task: Archive old notifications after a busy period
steps: Set a date range to isolate the period you want to clean up.
Click 'Select All' to check all visible items.
Click 'Archive Selected'.

warnings: Security alerts appear in the dedicated Security tab — do not dismiss them without investigation, as they may indicate account compromise.
Archiving is not reversible from the UI — archived notifications are removed from the default inbox view.
Mark All Read affects all notifications, not just the currently filtered view — use carefully if you want to keep some items unread.

entries.title: Offline Mode
entries.overview: Device management dashboard with color-coded sections: blue device zone with health cards and sync controls, amber offline limits zone with warning indicators, emerald PIN security zone, and purple receipt history zone. Each section has a distinct colored left border accent and themed icon containers.

elements.name: Device name input
elements.description: A human-readable label for this specific device (e.g. 'Main Counter Tablet', 'Warehouse POS'). Located in the blue device zone with a blue left border. This name appears in the server's device registry and in audit logs, making it easy to trace which device performed an offline action.

elements.name: Register Device button
elements.description: Registers this device with the server for offline use. After registration, the device receives an offline token that allows local authentication and operation without an active internet connection. Requires the device to be online at the time of registration.

elements.name: Revoke Device button
elements.description: Revokes this device's offline registration and permanently clears all locally stored offline data, including the queue of unsynced actions. Only use this after syncing — any pending actions in the queue will be lost when the device is revoked.

elements.name: PIN input
elements.description: Enter a 4-8 digit numeric PIN. Located in the emerald PIN zone with an emerald left border and emerald-themed icon container. This PIN is stored securely on the device and used to authenticate offline login when the server cannot be reached.

elements.name: Set PIN button
elements.description: Saves the entered PIN to the device's local secure storage. From this point on, users can log in offline using this PIN instead of their normal credentials. Changing the PIN replaces the previous one immediately.

elements.name: Clear PIN button
elements.description: Removes the stored offline PIN from this device. After clearing, offline login will not be possible until a new PIN is set. Use this if a staff member who knew the PIN has left or if you suspect the PIN was compromised.

elements.name: Sync Now button
elements.description: Manually triggers a sync of the offline queue to the server. Normally sync happens automatically when the device reconnects, but you can force it here to confirm the queue is clear before going offline again. The pending actions count should drop to zero after a successful sync.

elements.name: Pending actions count
elements.description: The number of offline actions (sales, transfers, etc.) queued locally and not yet sent to the server. This should reach zero after every sync. A persistently non-zero count indicates sync is blocked — check the Sync blocked warning.

elements.name: Queue stats panel
elements.description: Displays current queue usage: number of items and total payload size in bytes, compared against the maximum values allowed by your subscription. If you approach the limit, sync before continuing to operate offline.

elements.name: Sync blocked warning
elements.description: Appears when automatic sync has been paused — typically because a previous sync attempt produced unresolvable conflicts. While sync is blocked, new offline actions still queue but do not sync. Go to the Offline Conflicts page to resolve the blocking conflicts.

elements.name: Last sync timestamp
elements.description: Shows the date and time of the most recent successful sync. If this timestamp is very old and the pending count is non-zero, the device may have been offline for an extended period — ensure sync completes before approaching your subscription's offline duration limit.

elements.name: Receipt history table
elements.description: Lists receipts that were issued offline in the purple receipt zone with a purple left border. Each row shows the local receipt number assigned at the time of the sale and the official server-assigned receipt number once the action was synced. Use this to look up a receipt issued during an outage.

elements.name: Device status
elements.description: Shows the server-side status of this device's registration: ACTIVE (registered and permitted to operate offline), REVOKED (registration was manually revoked), or EXPIRED (the offline token exceeded the subscription's maximum offline duration).

elements.name: Offline limits info
elements.description: Displays the offline constraints defined by your subscription plan in the amber limits zone with an amber left border: maximum continuous offline duration (hours), maximum number of sales allowed while offline, and maximum total transaction value allowed while offline. Exceeding these limits will cause the offline token to expire.

elements.name: Queue usage percentage
elements.description: Shows the offline queue usage as a percentage of maximum capacity — items count and total bytes used.

elements.name: Pending count in queue
elements.description: Number of offline actions waiting to be synced to the server.

elements.name: Last sync timestamp
elements.description: Shows when the device last successfully synced with the server.

elements.name: Receipt history panel
elements.description: Lists receipts created while offline, showing the local receipt number and the final receipt number assigned after sync.

elements.name: Cache truncated warning
elements.description: A warning shown when the offline data cache has been truncated to save space.

elements.name: Sync blocked warning
elements.description: A warning shown when sync is blocked due to version mismatch or server issues.

elements.name: View Conflicts link
elements.description: Navigates to the Offline Conflicts page to review and resolve actions that failed during sync.

elements.name: PIN input form
elements.description: Set or clear the offline PIN that protects POS and stock adjustment access when offline. Includes Enable PIN and Clear PIN buttons.

elements.name: Offline limits display
elements.description: Shows configured offline limits: maximum hours, maximum transactions, and maximum total value allowed before sync is required.

elements.name: Safeguards section
elements.description: Information panel explaining offline data encryption and risk warnings about working without connectivity.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'offline.write' permission to register or configure offline mode.
Your subscription must include offline access — if the Register button is disabled, check Business Settings → Subscription.
The device must be online at least once to register and to sync the queue. Offline mode only works after initial registration.

common_tasks.task: Register this device for offline use
steps: Make sure you are connected to the internet.
Enter a descriptive name for this device in the Device name input.
Click 'Register Device'.
Device status will change to ACTIVE — the device can now operate offline.
Enter a 4–8 digit PIN in the PIN input.
Click 'Set PIN'.
Test the PIN by logging out and attempting an offline login.
Ensure the device is online.
Click 'Sync Now'.
Wait for the sync to complete — the Pending actions count should reach zero.
If the count does not reach zero, check the Sync blocked warning and resolve conflicts.

warnings: Revoking a device clears all locally stored data and the entire unsynced queue — ensure the queue is empty (Pending = 0) before revoking.
If sync is blocked, new actions continue to queue but nothing will be sent to the server until conflicts are resolved on the Offline Conflicts page.
Do not use the same PIN as any bank card, email, or other account — the PIN is stored locally and protects access to business data.

entries.title: Offline Conflicts
entries.overview: Conflict resolution queue with per-type colored zones: purple for approval blocks (Shield icon), red for price variance (DollarSign icon), blue for VAT changes (Receipt icon). Each conflict has a colored left border, hero reason badge, and type-specific resolution buttons. Resolved state shows emerald CheckCircle.

elements.name: Total conflicts KPI
elements.description: The number of offline actions that failed server-side validation and are waiting for resolution. This number must reach zero before automatic sync can resume normally.

elements.name: Approval Required KPI
elements.description: Count of conflicts where the action needed manager approval at the time it was processed, displayed in a purple-tinted zone. These cannot be auto-applied — a manager must approve them via the Approvals module.

elements.name: Price Variance KPI
elements.description: Count of conflicts where a sale was processed offline at a price that falls outside the configured acceptable variance, displayed in a red-tinted zone. These can be overridden (force through the offline price) or dismissed.

elements.name: Conflict card
elements.description: Summarises a single failed action with a colored zone matching the conflict type (purple for APPROVAL_REQUIRED, red for PRICE_VARIANCE, blue for VAT_CHANGED): the action type, the conflict reason, the server's error message, and the full original payload so you can review exactly what was attempted.

elements.name: Retry button
elements.description: Re-submits the action to the server with no modifications. This succeeds only if the underlying rule violation has since been resolved (e.g. the price range was widened by a manager). If the same rule still applies, the retry will fail again.

elements.name: Override Price button
elements.description: Only available for PRICE_VARIANCE conflicts, styled with red theming. Forces the action through using the offline price, bypassing the price variance rule. This is recorded in the audit log. Use only when you are certain the offline price was correct and authorised.

elements.name: Sync Approval button
elements.description: Only available for APPROVAL_REQUIRED conflicts, styled with purple theming. Moves the action into the Approvals queue so a manager can review and approve it. The action will be applied once approved, or discarded if rejected.

elements.name: Dismiss button
elements.description: Permanently discards this conflict and the underlying action. The sale, transfer, or other record it represents will not be created. This cannot be undone — only dismiss if you are certain the action should be abandoned.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Appends the next batch of conflict records to the list. Appears when there are more conflicts than the initial page load.

elements.name: Conflict card layout
elements.description: Each conflict shows the action type (SaleComplete, PurchaseDraft, StockAdjustment), status badge, error message, and resolution buttons.

elements.name: Resolution options
elements.description: Each conflict has resolution buttons that vary by conflict type. For example, VAT_CHANGED conflicts only offer Dismiss. Other conflicts may offer Retry or Override.

elements.name: Approval ID display
elements.description: If a conflict involves a pending approval, the approval ID is shown for cross-referencing with the Approvals page.

elements.name: Conflict status badges
elements.description: Coloured badges showing conflict status: Applied, Conflict, Failed, Pending, Rejected.

elements.name: Load More pagination
elements.description: Load additional conflict records beyond the initial page.

before_you_start.text: You need the 'offline.write' permission to view and resolve conflicts.
The device must be online — this page cannot be used while offline.
Conflicts appear only after a sync attempt fails. If there are no conflicts shown, your offline queue synced cleanly.

steps: Open the conflict card and review the payload — check what item was sold and at what price.
If the offline price was authorised, click 'Override Price' to force it through.
If the sale should not be recorded, click 'Dismiss' to abandon the action.

common_tasks.task: Send an offline sale for manager approval
steps: Find the conflict with reason APPROVAL_REQUIRED.
Click 'Sync Approval' to move it to the Approvals queue.
Notify the relevant manager to review the approval in the Approvals module.

warnings: Dismissed conflicts are permanently lost — the action will not be recorded anywhere in the system. Do not dismiss without careful review.
Override Price bypasses pricing rules and is logged in the audit trail — only use it when explicitly authorised.
Resolving all conflicts is required to unblock automatic sync. Until this page is clear, new offline actions queue but do not sync.

entries.title: Business Setup
entries.overview: This guided setup runs once the first time you log in after registering. It walks you through 5 steps: setting up your business profile, configuring branches, customising receipts, inviting your team (with role, name, phone, and branch access), and a final ready screen. You cannot skip to the main system until both the business profile and branch setup are marked complete. The layout uses a two-panel design with a sidebar wizard on desktop and a progress bar on mobile.

before_you_start.text: You must have verified your email address before reaching this page.
You need the business.update and settings.write permissions to save changes. As the business owner, you have these by default.

elements.name: Sidebar wizard steps (WizardSteps, desktop only)
elements.description: Left sidebar shows a vertical step indicator with icons: Building2 (Business Profile), MapPin (Branch Setup), Receipt (Receipt Setup), Users (Invite Team), CheckCircle (Ready). Each step shows a label and description. Completed steps display a green Check icon. A ProgressBar at the bottom shows overall progress as a percentage.

elements.name: Mobile progress bar (ProgressBar)
elements.description: On mobile and tablet, a compact ProgressBar replaces the sidebar. Shows current step number, step label, and a coloured fill bar.

elements.name: Welcome message (Step 1 — Business Profile)
elements.description: A highlighted banner with a friendly welcome message explaining the setup will take a couple of minutes.

elements.name: Business Name field (Step 1 — TextInput, Building2 icon)
elements.description: Your business name. Pre-filled from registration — update it here if you want to change it.

elements.name: Currency selector (Step 1 — SmartSelect, Globe icon)
elements.description: Select the currency your business operates in. Pre-populated with common options including TZS, KES, UGX, USD, EUR, GBP, ZAR. Default is TZS.

elements.name: Timezone selector (Step 1 — SmartSelect, Clock icon)
elements.description: Your business timezone. Lists African timezones first for convenience. Defaults to Africa/Dar_es_Salaam.

elements.name: Display size selector (Step 1)
elements.description: Optional font scale chooser with 4 sizes: Small, Default, Large, Extra Large. Includes a live preview so you can see how text will look. Your choice is saved locally and applied immediately. You can change this later from your Profile page.

elements.name: Save & Continue button (Step 1, ChevronRight icon)
elements.description: Saves your business name, currency, and timezone, then advances to Step 2 (Branch Setup). Uses nvi-press animation. You can return to these settings any time via Business Settings.

elements.name: Branch cards (Step 2 — Card with Building2, MapPin, Phone icons)
elements.description: Each existing branch is displayed as a mini Card with nvi-card-hover animation. Shows branch name, address (with MapPin icon), and phone (with Phone icon). Default branch has an emerald badge. Uses nvi-stagger for entrance animation. If no branches exist, an EmptyState with Building2 icon is shown.

elements.name: Edit Branch section (Step 2 — Card with SmartSelect + TextInputs)
elements.description: Select a branch from the SmartSelect dropdown, then edit its Name, Address, and Phone using labelled TextInput fields in a 3-column grid. Click Save Branch Details to update.

elements.name: Add Another Branch section (Step 2 — Card with TextInputs + Plus icon)
elements.description: A separate Card form to create a new branch. Fill in Name (required), Address, and Phone using labelled TextInput fields, then click the Add Branch button (with Plus icon).

elements.name: Back / Continue buttons (Step 2, ChevronLeft / ChevronRight icons)
elements.description: Back returns to Step 1. Continue marks branch setup as complete and advances to Step 3. At least one branch is required before Continue works.

elements.name: Receipt Format toggle (Step 3 — Receipt icon header)
elements.description: Two toggle buttons: Thermal (POS) and A4 / Letter. Selected format is highlighted with an accent border and background.

elements.name: Receipt Header field (Step 3 — Textarea)
elements.description: Optional multi-line text shown at the top of every receipt. Example: VAT registration info.

elements.name: Receipt Footer field (Step 3 — TextInput)
elements.description: Optional single-line text shown at the bottom of every receipt. Default is 'Thank you for your business.'

elements.name: Receipt preview (Step 3 — Card glow=false)
elements.description: A live mini receipt mockup showing the business name, header text, sample line items, total, and footer text. Updates as you type to give a realistic preview.

elements.name: Invite row cards (Step 4 — Card with nvi-card-hover, nvi-stagger)
elements.description: Each invite is a Card containing: Email (TextInput, required), Role (SmartSelect with Shield icon), Name (TextInput, optional), Phone (TextInput, optional), and branch access checkboxes (if multiple branches exist). Branch checkboxes use Building2 icons and toggle-style labels. A Trash2 icon button removes the row. Uses nvi-stagger for entrance animation.

elements.name: Add another invite button (Step 4 — UserPlus icon)
elements.description: Adds a new empty invite row. Full-width dashed border button with UserPlus icon. Invites are sent via POST /users/invite with email, roleId, name, phone, and branchIds.

elements.name: Send Invites button (Step 4 — ChevronRight icon)
elements.description: Sends all valid invite rows (those with email and role filled). Uses nvi-press animation. If no rows are filled, skips directly to the Ready screen.

elements.name: Ready screen (Step 5 — CheckCircle icon, emerald)
elements.description: A centred completion screen with a large emerald CheckCircle icon using nvi-bounce-in and nvi-float animations. Pulsing rings create a celebratory effect. Five decorative dots animate in with nvi-stagger. Shows 'You're all set!' heading and rotating status messages. A 'Go to Dashboard' button (with ArrowRight icon and nvi-press) allows immediate navigation, or the page auto-redirects after 2.8 seconds.

steps: Step 1: Check and update your Business Name, Currency, and Timezone, then click Save & Continue.
Step 2: Review or edit your default branch details. Add more branches if needed, then click Continue.
Step 3: Choose receipt format, customise header/footer text, preview it, then click Save & Continue (or Skip).
Step 4: Add team member invites with email, role, optional name/phone, and branch access. Click Send Invites (or Skip).
Step 5: The Ready screen confirms setup is complete. Click Go to Dashboard or wait for auto-redirect.

warnings: You cannot proceed to the system until Step 1 (Business Profile) and Step 2 (Branch Setup) are both marked complete.
At least one branch is required before you can complete Step 2.
Steps 3 (Receipt Setup) and 4 (Invite Team) can be skipped and completed later from Settings.
If the system shows a Read Only banner, your account is temporarily restricted. Contact support.
Branch access checkboxes only appear if you have more than one branch. Leave empty to grant access to all branches.

workflow.step: Step 4: Send invites or skip, then auto-redirect to app dashboard from the Ready screen.
workflow.expected_result: Onboarding exits cleanly and app access continues with setup flags persisted.

common_errors.likely_cause: Branch setup completion was submitted with zero active/non-archived branches.
fix_steps: Create at least one branch before pressing Continue on branch setup.
Reload branch list and verify the branch is not archived.
Retry onboarding completion after branch exists.

common_errors.error_symptom: Onboarding save actions fail with PERMISSION_IS_REQUIRED.
common_errors.likely_cause: User role lacks required permissions for business/settings update actions.
fix_steps: Ask admin to grant business.update and settings.write permissions.
Retry onboarding action after permission update takes effect.
If role is branch-scoped, verify scope allows current setup actions.

common_errors.likely_cause: Session lost business context or token/session state is stale.
fix_steps: Re-login to refresh session and business context.
Confirm active business is selected before returning to onboarding.
Retry onboarding save operations after session refresh.

related_pages.reason: Onboarding starts after successful Login when setup is incomplete.
Business settings is a direct follow-up for deeper policy/profile adjustments.
Branch settings is the operational follow-up for managing branch records.
Users setup is the next step for team configuration after onboarding core setup.

entries.title: Reset Password
entries.overview: Use this page if you have forgotten your password. Enter your email address and the system will send you a password reset link. If your email is linked to more than one business, you will need to choose which business to reset the password for.

before_you_start.text: You need access to the email address you registered with.

elements.name: Email field
elements.description: Enter the email address your account is registered under.

elements.name: Business selector
elements.description: Appears only if your email is linked to more than one business. Choose which business you want to reset the password for.
elements.notes: This appears after you submit your email the first time.

elements.name: Send Reset Link button
elements.description: Submits your email and sends a password reset link to your inbox. Check your email (and spam folder) after clicking.

elements.name: Two-stage flow
elements.description: First enter your email to receive a reset link. If you belong to multiple businesses, a business selector appears to specify which business context.

elements.name: Business selector (optional)
elements.description: Appears only if your email is associated with multiple businesses. Select which business to reset the password for.

steps: Enter your email address.
Click Send Reset Link.
If prompted, choose your business from the dropdown and click Send Reset Link again.
Check your inbox for the reset email and click the link inside it.

warnings: The reset link expires after a short time. Use it promptly after it arrives in your email.
If you do not receive the email within a few minutes, check your spam or junk folder.
This page only sends the reset link — to actually set your new password, follow the link in the email.

related_pages.reason: You arrive here from Login when you forget your password.
After clicking the email link, you land here to set your new password.

entries.title: Set New Password
entries.overview: This page is where you create your new password after clicking the reset link in your email. The reset token is filled in automatically from the link. Enter your new password, then submit.

before_you_start.text: You must have received and clicked the password reset link sent to your email.

elements.name: Token field
elements.description: The reset token from your email. Usually filled in automatically when you arrive via the email link. If it is blank, paste the token from the email manually.

elements.name: New Password field
elements.description: Your new password. Must meet the minimum requirements shown below the field.

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your new password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Set Password button
elements.description: Submits the token and new password. If successful, you will be redirected to the Login page after 1.5 seconds.

elements.name: Token auto-fill
elements.description: The reset token is automatically filled from the URL. You do not need to enter it manually.

elements.name: Auto-redirect on success
elements.description: After setting your new password, you are automatically redirected to the login page after 1.5 seconds.

steps: Confirm the token field is filled (it should be automatic).
Type your new password.
Click Set Password.
You will be redirected to Login — sign in with your new password.

warnings: Reset tokens expire after a short time. If you see a token expired error, go back to the Password Reset page and request a new link.
Your new password must meet the minimum requirements. A password hint is shown below the field.
After setting your new password, you will be redirected to Login automatically.

related_pages.reason: You must request a reset link here before you can use this page.
After setting your new password, sign in here.

entries.title: Point of Sale (POS)
entries.overview: Premium point-of-sale interface with warm gold-accented dark theme. The header bar shows cashier identity (name and avatar), branch and shift status, and session performance stats. Product cards feature warm dark backgrounds with gold hover glows and emerald pricing. The cart zone uses a distinct darker shade for visual separation, with gold-tinted totals container, per-method colored payment pills (emerald cash, blue card, purple mobile, amber bank), and warm gold quick-amount buttons. The Complete Sale button uses a gold gradient with pulse animation. Three responsive layouts: desktop split-pane, tablet tab interface, and phone bottom-sheet cart.

elements.name: Branch Selector (Scan Panel)
elements.description: Choose which branch you are selling from. This determines which stock is deducted. Locked once items are in the cart.
elements.notes: You cannot change branch mid-sale. Empty the cart first if you need to switch.

elements.name: Customer Selector (optional)
elements.description: Attach a customer to this sale. Optional — leave blank for walk-in customers. Required if you want to track credit balances per customer.

elements.name: Product Search / Barcode Input
elements.description: Type a product name, SKU, or barcode and press Enter or click Add. The system will find the matching variant and add it to the cart. Supports typeahead suggestions as you type.

elements.name: Add Button
elements.description: Adds the searched or scanned product to the cart. Same as pressing Enter in the search field.

elements.name: Start / Stop Scan Button
elements.description: Activates the device camera to scan barcodes. Click again to stop. Automatically picks the rear camera if available.
elements.notes: Browser must be granted camera permission. If denied, you'll see a message — use text search instead.

elements.name: Product Panel
elements.description: Shows all available products in a grid. Click any card to instantly add it to the cart. Filter using the search box at the top of the panel.

elements.name: Cart Items
elements.description: Each item in the cart shows the product name, quantity input, unit selector, unit price input, line discount input, and a Remove button. Edit any field directly.
elements.notes: If a price list is active, unit price is pre-filled. Min price warnings show in red if you go below the limit.

elements.name: Cart Discount
elements.description: Enter a flat discount amount to apply across the entire cart. This is separate from per-item line discounts.

elements.name: Totals Panel
elements.description: Shows Subtotal, Discounts (line + cart), VAT, and Total. Updates live as you edit the cart.

elements.name: Payment Method Selector
elements.description: Choose how the customer is paying: Cash (emerald), Card (blue), Mobile Money (purple), Bank Transfer (amber), or Other. Each method has a distinct color. You can add multiple payment rows for split payments.

elements.name: Payment Amount Input
elements.description: Enter the amount for each payment method. For cash, you can enter more than the total — the system calculates change.

elements.name: Add Payment Button
elements.description: Adds another payment row to the payment section, enabling split payments across multiple methods (e.g. part cash, part mobile money). Each row has its own method selector and amount field — total must equal the sale amount to complete.

elements.name: Credit Sale Checkbox
elements.description: When checked, allows completing the sale with partial or no payment — the balance is recorded as a customer credit debt. Only visible if credit sales are enabled in Business Settings.
elements.notes: Requires the customer to be selected and a credit due date to be set.

elements.name: Credit Due Date
elements.description: Appears when Credit Sale is checked. Set the date by which the customer should settle the balance.

elements.name: Complete Sale Button
elements.description: Gold gradient CTA button with pulse animation that finalises the sale. Deducts stock, issues a receipt, and records all payments. If offline, the sale is queued for later sync.

elements.name: Connect Printer Button
elements.description: Connects to a USB/Bluetooth ESC/POS receipt printer. Only needed if you use a hardware printer.

elements.name: Hardware Print Toggle
elements.description: When enabled, receipts print automatically to the connected hardware printer after each completed sale.

elements.name: Print Last Receipt Button
elements.description: Reprints the receipt from the most recently completed sale to the connected hardware printer.

elements.name: Preview & Print Button
elements.description: Opens a receipt preview modal with Compact and Detailed view options. From the preview you can print using the browser print dialog.

elements.name: Parked Sales feature
elements.description: Park a sale in progress to serve another customer, then resume it later. Parked sales are stored locally and shown in a list. Click Resume to continue where you left off.

elements.name: Session sale counter
elements.description: Displays how many sales you have completed and the total revenue in this login session. Resets when you log out.

elements.name: Pending sync badge
elements.description: Shows the count of offline sales waiting to be synced to the server. If this stays above zero, check your internet connection.

elements.name: PIN lock and attempt counter
elements.description: If the offline PIN is enabled, you must enter it to unlock the POS when offline. After 5 failed attempts, the PIN is locked for a cooldown period.

elements.name: Popular items section
elements.description: Shows your most frequently sold items for quick adding to the cart. Based on your recent sales history.

elements.name: Barcode scan sound toggle
elements.description: Enable or disable an audio beep when a barcode is successfully scanned. Toggle via the sound icon in the toolbar.

elements.name: Cart persistence
elements.description: Your cart is automatically saved to localStorage. If you close the browser or refresh, the cart is restored when you return.

elements.name: Mobile tab navigation
elements.description: On phone-sized screens, the POS uses tab navigation: Products (browse/search), Cart (view items), Pay (complete sale). Swipe or tap tabs to switch.

elements.name: Customer price list
elements.description: When a customer with an assigned price list is selected, product prices automatically update to reflect their special pricing.

elements.name: Quick cash amounts
elements.description: Pre-set cash amount buttons (formatted with K/M for thousands/millions) for fast cash payment entry without typing.

elements.name: Payment method icons
elements.description: Five payment methods with unique colored icon containers: Cash in emerald, Card in blue, Mobile Money in purple, Bank Transfer in amber, and Other in neutral. Each has a description to help cashiers select correctly.

elements.name: Live clock
elements.description: A real-time clock display in the POS header, ticking every second. Useful for shift reference without leaving the POS screen.

elements.name: Receipt template selector
elements.description: Choose between THERMAL (receipt printer) and A4 (full-page) receipt formats.

elements.name: Unit conversion hint
elements.description: A dynamic hint below the unit selector on each cart item showing the stock conversion. For example: '2 boxes = 10 pieces in stock'. Only appears when the variant uses different base and sell units.

before_you_start.text: You need at least one active product variant with a selling price in the catalog.
Your role needs the sales.write permission. Without it, the Complete Sale button is disabled.
If shift tracking is enabled in Business Settings, you must open a shift for your branch before you can complete sales.

steps: Select your branch from the Branch Selector.
Optionally select a customer.
Search for products or scan barcodes to build the cart.
Adjust quantities, unit, unit price, or line discounts as needed.
In the Totals panel, enter payment amount and method.
Click Complete Sale — the receipt is issued automatically.
The POS detects no internet connection automatically.
If an offline PIN is required, enter it in the PIN field that appears.
Build and complete the sale as normal.
The sale is queued with a local receipt number — it syncs when you go back online.

warnings: You cannot change the branch once items are in the cart. Empty the cart first if you need to switch branches.
For non-cash payments (Card, Mobile Money, Bank Transfer), the payment total must exactly match the sale total — there's no change calculation for non-cash. Use Cash for overpayment.
If shift tracking is turned on in Business Settings and no open shift exists for your branch, the Complete Sale button will be blocked.
Large discounts may trigger an approval workflow — the sale will not complete immediately if approval is required.
Offline sales are queued locally. Do not close the browser or clear site data before syncing, or queued sales may be lost.

related_pages.reason: Open a shift here before starting POS if shift tracking is enabled.
Check available stock before a sale to avoid stock-out failures at checkout.
View, reprint, and process refunds on completed sales receipts.
Controls credit enablement, shift tracking, VAT rate, and receipt template used by POS.

entries.title: Price Lists
entries.overview: Create and manage named price lists that override your default product pricing. The hero impact summary uses emerald for savings and amber for price increases. Each item row shows a strikethrough default price alongside the colored list price for instant comparison. Assigned customers appear as blue pills, and the customer assignment section features a blue left border. Use the Price Lists Wizard for bulk percentage or flat-amount adjustments across an entire list.

elements.name: Name input (create form)
elements.description: Enter a unique name for the new price list — for example 'Wholesale' or 'VIP Customers'. The name must be unique within your business and cannot be blank (inside the modal).

elements.name: "Create price list" button (header) + modal
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a small modal for creating a new price list (single name field). Previously this was an inline collapsible form on the page — moved into a modal.

elements.name: Price list card
elements.description: Shows the list name, its current status (ACTIVE / INACTIVE / ARCHIVED), and how many variant price overrides it contains. An ACTIVE list affects pricing wherever it is assigned. Click Edit to change the name or status.

elements.name: Edit button (per list)
elements.description: Pencil icon in the card header opens an edit modal containing the name field and status selector (Active / Inactive / Archived). Previously this was an inline form inside the card — now opens as a modal.

elements.name: Status selector (edit mode)
elements.description: ACTIVE — the list is applied to any branch or customer it is assigned to. INACTIVE — the list exists but pricing is not applied even where assigned, useful for pausing a list temporarily. ARCHIVED — hides the list from selection menus and stops all pricing application (inside the modal).

elements.name: Save / Cancel (edit mode)
elements.description: Save commits your name or status changes immediately. Cancel reverts all fields to the last saved state without making any changes (inside the modal).

elements.name: "Assign price" button (header) + modal
elements.description: A second button in the page header opens a modal for adding a price override to a list: list selector + variant selector + price input. Previously this was a blue-bordered Card sitting below the create form — moved into a modal.

elements.name: Price list selector (item form)
elements.description: Choose which price list this variant price override belongs to. All your existing price lists are shown here (inside the modal).

elements.name: Variant selector (item form)
elements.description: SmartSelect showing all product variants in your catalogue. Search by product name, variant name, or SKU to find the right one (inside the modal).

elements.name: Price input (item form)
elements.description: The custom price to charge for this variant when the list is active. Enter the amount in your business currency. This replaces the variant's default price wherever this list is applied — it does not affect the default price (inside the modal).

elements.name: Remove item button
elements.description: Permanently removes the variant's price override from the list. The variant immediately reverts to its default price at any branch or customer using this list.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Loads the next page of price list cards when you have more lists than the initial display.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Price List form)
elements.description: The create form is hidden behind a 'New Price List' button. Closes after creation (inside the modal).
