elements.name: Table view (ViewToggle)
elements.description: Toggle between card and table views. Table shows columns: Name, Status, Item Count, Customer Count, and Actions.

elements.name: Undo last change
elements.description: Reverts the most recent price change or item addition to a price list. Only available immediately after a change is made.

elements.name: Archive list action
elements.description: Trash icon in the card header prompts a confirmation dialog before moving the list to "Archived" status (soft-delete, not hard-delete). Confirmation is new — previously this was a silent one-click action. Matches the pattern already used for Undo last change.

elements.name: Default vs list price comparison
elements.description: For each item in a price list, shows the default variant price with strikethrough alongside the colored list override price (emerald if lower, amber if higher) for instant visual comparison.

elements.name: Variant image thumbnails
elements.description: Each price list item shows a small variant image for visual identification.

elements.name: Customer count per list
elements.description: Shows how many customers are assigned to each price list.

before_you_start.text: You need the price-lists.manage permission to create, edit, or delete price lists and their items.
Products and variants must already exist in the catalogue before you can add items to a price list. Create products first from the Catalog page.
To link a price list to a branch or customer, do that from the Branches or Customers page — it cannot be done from this page.

steps: Enter a name in the Name input at the top of the page.
Click Create. The list appears in the list below with ACTIVE status.
Use the Add Item form to begin adding variant price overrides to the new list.

common_tasks.task: Add a variant price override to a list
steps: In the Add Item form, select the target price list from the Price list selector.
Search for and select the variant using the Variant selector.
Enter the custom price in the Price input.
Click Add. The override is saved and applied immediately wherever the list is assigned.
Find the list card and click Edit.
Change Status to INACTIVE.
Click Save. The list is preserved with all its overrides but stops affecting pricing at branches or for customers.

warnings: Setting a price list to ARCHIVED hides it from all selection menus. Any branch or customer already assigned to an archived list stops receiving custom pricing and falls back to default prices immediately.
Creating a price list does not automatically apply it anywhere. You must go to the Branches or Customers page to assign the list — otherwise it has no effect on sales.
For bulk adjustments (e.g. increase all prices by 10%), use the Price Lists Wizard instead of editing overrides one by one.

entries.title: Bulk Price Update Wizard

related_pages.reason: Create and manage price lists before running the bulk update wizard.

entries.overview: Four-step pricing wizard with colored option cards: blue/purple for scope selection, emerald/blue/amber for adjustment mode. Impact preview shows colored stat boxes (blue total, red increases, emerald decreases, amber average). MetricComparison arrows colored by direction. Apply step shows dynamic progress with emerald success and red failure counts.

elements.name: PageHeader (hero)
elements.description: Page hero with eyebrow 'Pricing control', title 'Bulk price update wizard', subtitle, and two badges showing 'Bulk update' and the current step name. An 'ArrowLeft Back to price lists' link returns to the main price lists page.

elements.name: KPI strip (4 cards)
elements.description: Four KPI cards across the top: Current Step (ClipboardList icon, shows step name), Target Variants (Package icon, amber, shows count), Preview Rows (Eye icon, blue, shows count), Selected List (ListOrdered icon, emerald, shows list name). Each card has an icon container and hover animation.

elements.name: WizardSteps + ProgressBar
elements.description: A Card containing a ProgressBar (step X of 4 as percentage) and the WizardSteps component showing all four steps with numbered pills. Completed steps show checkmarks, the active step is highlighted.

elements.name: Scope step — List selector
elements.description: SmartSelect dropdown to choose which price list to update. A label with ListOrdered icon appears above it.

elements.name: Scope step — Option cards (ALL vs EXISTING)
elements.description: Two prominent option cards in a 2-column grid. ALL (Layers icon): 'Apply pricing changes to every variant in your catalogue, including those not yet on this list.' EXISTING (ListFilter icon): 'Only update variants that already have a price override on this list.' Selected card has gold border, ring glow, and tinted background. Both cards use nvi-press tactile click animation.

elements.name: Adjust step — Mode cards (PERCENT / ADD / SET)
elements.description: Three option cards in a 3-column grid, each centered with an icon, title, and description. PERCENT (Percent icon): 'Change by percentage'. ADD (Plus icon): 'Add or subtract a fixed amount'. SET (DollarSign icon): 'Set an exact price for all'. Selected card has gold border and ring glow.

elements.name: Adjust step — Value input
elements.description: CurrencyInput field for the adjustment value with nvi-focus-pulse animation on first focus. Below the input, a hint appears showing 'This will affect X variants' with an Info icon (animated with nvi-bounce-in).

elements.name: Preview step — Impact stat boxes (4 cards)
elements.description: Four impact metric cards in a row: Variants affected (Package icon, amber), Price increases (TrendingUp icon, red), Price decreases (TrendingDown icon, emerald), Average change (Percent icon, blue, shows both % and absolute amount). Cards use nvi-stagger entrance and nvi-card-hover.

elements.name: Preview step — Doughnut chart
elements.description: Card with 'Impact distribution' header (PieChart icon). Contains the Doughnut chart visualizing increases (green), decreases (red), and unchanged (grey) proportions. Legend at bottom.

elements.name: Preview step — Preview table
elements.description: Scrollable list of variant rows inside a Card. Each row shows variant name, MetricComparison (old price strikethrough arrow new price, color-coded up=emerald down=red), and a percentage change badge (red for increases, emerald for decreases, animated with nvi-bounce-in). Rows have colored borders — red-tinted for increases, emerald-tinted for decreases. New rows appear with nvi-stagger animation.

elements.name: Show More button (Preview)
elements.description: Centered button with ChevronDown icon, rounded-xl with nvi-press. Loads 12 more rows into the preview table. Only visible when there are more variants than currently shown.

elements.name: Apply step — Summary card
elements.description: Gold-tinted summary card with FileCheck icon showing 'Applying changes to X variants on [list name]'.

elements.name: Apply step — Batch progress bar
elements.description: Visible during application. Shows a ProgressBar tracking batch processing progress (e.g. '3 / 5' batches completed). Updates in real time as each batch of 20 variants completes.

elements.name: Apply step — Result summary
elements.description: Appears after application completes. On full success: green-tinted card with CheckCircle icon showing 'X prices updated successfully' plus nvi-save-flash green pulse animation on the entire step card. On partial failure: amber-tinted card with AlertTriangle icon showing 'X updated, Y failed'.

elements.name: Apply Now button
elements.description: Gold CTA with Zap icon, rounded-xl with nvi-press. Shows a Spinner and 'Applying...' text while processing. Disabled without price-lists.manage permission (shows tooltip). Cannot be clicked while already applying.

elements.name: Back / Next buttons
elements.description: All navigation buttons use nvi-press animation and rounded-xl styling. Back has ArrowLeft icon, outlined style. Next has ArrowRight icon, gold filled style. Both navigate between wizard steps without losing data.

before_you_start.text: You need the price-lists.manage permission to access and use this wizard.
A price list must already exist. Create one from the Price Lists page first.
If using EXISTING scope, the list must already have at least one variant price override on it — otherwise nothing is updated.

common_tasks.task: Increase all prices on a list by 10%
steps: On the Scope step, select the price list from the dropdown.
Choose the ALL scope card to include every variant in your catalogue.
Click Next (ArrowRight). On the Adjust step, select the PERCENT card and enter 10.
Click Next. Review the impact dashboard — check the stat boxes and scroll through the preview table to confirm prices are correct.
Click Next to reach the Apply step. Review the summary, then click Apply Now.

common_tasks.task: Reduce existing list items by a flat amount
steps: Select the list and choose the EXISTING scope card, then click Next.
Select the ADD card and enter the amount as a negative number (e.g. -500 to reduce each price by 500).
Click Next. Review the preview table — decreases show in emerald with green badges.
Click Next and then Apply Now. Watch the batch progress bar as changes are committed.

warnings: Applying the wizard is permanent and cannot be undone. Take note of current prices or export them before running the wizard if you need a backup.
ALL scope with SET mode will set every variant in your catalogue to the same fixed price — this is almost never what you want. Use EXISTING scope or PERCENT/ADD mode to avoid unintended overwrites.
Percentage adjustments on very small prices may produce unexpected results due to rounding. Always review the full Preview table before clicking Apply.
Changes are applied in batches of 20. If the browser is closed mid-apply, some variants may be updated while others are not. Wait for the progress bar to complete.

entries.title: Purchase Orders
entries.overview: A procurement command center for creating and tracking purchase orders through their full lifecycle. Each order card features pipeline dots showing progression: emerald for completed steps, gold for the current step, and muted for future steps. Supplier details sit in blue-tinted containers, overdue orders flash red AlertTriangle alerts, and reorder suggestions appear in amber suggestion cards. Contextual action buttons use distinct colors — emerald for Approve, blue for Receive — creating clear visual hierarchy across the procurement workflow.

elements.name: Open Orders KPI
elements.description: Shows the total number of purchase orders currently loaded (based on active filters). Uses a ClipboardList icon in an emerald icon container.

elements.name: Overdue KPI
elements.description: Count of purchase orders past their expected delivery date that have not been completed. Red AlertTriangle icon. A high count means supplier follow-up is needed.

elements.name: Pending Approval KPI
elements.description: Count of purchase orders waiting for a manager to approve before they can proceed to receiving. Amber Clock icon. If this number is persistently high, there may be an approval bottleneck.

elements.name: Approved / Ready KPI
elements.description: Count of orders that are approved, partially received, or fully received — orders actively in progress or recently completed. Emerald CheckCircle icon.

elements.name: PO Pipeline (card view)
elements.description: A visual step indicator at the top of each order card showing the order's journey: Draft, Pending, Approved, Partial, Received. The current step is highlighted in gold, completed steps show emerald checkmarks, and future steps are muted. Cancelled and Closed orders show a special badge instead.

elements.name: PO Pipeline dots (table view)
elements.description: Compressed version of the pipeline shown as small dots in the first table column. Each dot represents a pipeline step — emerald dots are completed, gold dot is the current step, and muted dots are future steps.

elements.name: Order card — Supplier header
elements.description: Each card shows the supplier name with a Truck icon and the reference number below. The order total value is displayed prominently on the right.

elements.name: Order card — Items summary
elements.description: Shows item count and total quantity ordered (e.g. '5 items, 200 qty') with a Package icon.

elements.name: Order card — Expected delivery
elements.description: Shows expected delivery date. If overdue, displays a red AlertTriangle badge with days count (e.g. '3 days overdue'). If upcoming within 7 days, shows amber Clock warning. Otherwise shows a neutral CalendarClock date.

elements.name: Order card — Expandable line items
elements.description: Click 'N items' to expand and see individual line details: variant name, quantity, unit cost, and line total. Click again to collapse.

elements.name: Order card — Contextual actions
elements.description: Actions change based on order status. Draft orders show Edit and Approve buttons. Pending orders show Approve. Approved orders are ready for receiving. Each action uses appropriate icons (CheckCircle for approve, Eye for edit).

elements.name: Open Wizard Link
elements.description: Opens the step-by-step Purchase Order Wizard — a guided alternative to the inline create form. Uses a Wand2 icon.

elements.name: Search / Filters Bar
elements.description: Filter orders by keyword, status, branch, supplier, or date range. Wrapped in a Card component. Click Show Advanced for date filters.

elements.name: Branch Selector (Create form)
elements.description: Select which branch this order is for. Stock received against this order will go to this branch.

elements.name: Supplier Selector (Create form)
elements.description: Select the supplier you're ordering from. Only active suppliers appear. If a supplier has a lead time set, the system shows an estimated delivery date in a Truck-icon card.

elements.name: Expected Date (Create form)
elements.description: Optional. Set the date you expect delivery. If the selected supplier has a lead time configured, the system suggests a date automatically.

elements.name: Line item cards (Create/Edit forms)
elements.description: Each order line is a mini Card component with: variant search (AsyncSmartSelect), quantity TextInput, unit cost TextInput, unit selector (filtered to show only the variant's base and sell units with labels), and a red Trash2 icon button to remove. Add new lines with the Plus button. A blue info hint box below the lines explains how units work for purchases.

elements.name: Create Button
elements.description: Submits the new purchase order with a CheckCircle icon. If your business requires approval, the order is set to Pending Approval status.

elements.name: Reorder Suggestions panel
elements.description: An expandable section with a Lightbulb icon header showing variants below their reorder point. Each suggestion is an amber mini-card with Package icon showing variant name and suggested quantity. Click 'Use Suggestions' to auto-fill the create form and open it.

elements.name: Update Button
elements.description: Saves edits to the selected purchase order with a CheckCircle icon. Only available for orders in DRAFT or APPROVED status.

elements.name: Approve Button (per order card/row)
elements.description: Emerald button that approves a purchase order in Pending Approval status. Uses CheckCircle icon in card view, bordered button in table view.
elements.notes: Requires purchases.write permission. Disabled if you lack the permission.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card view (pipeline journey cards) and table view (compact rows with inline pipeline dots and sortable columns).

elements.name: Create order button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with Plus icon. Opens the Create Purchase Order modal. Only shown to users with the purchases.write permission.

elements.name: Create Purchase Order modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Create order header button. Contains branch and supplier selectors, expected delivery date, line items (variant + quantity + unit cost + unit), and — when the selected branch has stock below reorder points — an inline Reorder Suggestions panel showing which variants to include (with a one-click 'Use Suggestions' button to populate the line items). ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful create.

elements.name: Edit Purchase Order modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened by clicking the Edit (Pencil) action on a DRAFT order row. The existing line items and expected date are pre-populated so you can adjust in place — no need to retype the order. If the order was already APPROVED when edited, a Banner info notice appears because the edit may require re-approval. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving.

elements.name: Overdue PO highlighting
elements.description: Purchase orders past their expected delivery date are highlighted with red AlertTriangle badges showing exact days overdue. In table view, the entire row has a red-tinted background.

elements.name: Supplier lead time indicator
elements.description: Shows the supplier's configured lead time with a Truck icon. If no lead time is set, a Clock icon hint appears.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills with dots: DRAFT, PENDING, APPROVED, PARTIAL, RECEIVED, CLOSED, CANCELLED.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Supplier, Branch, Status, Expected Date, Items, and Total. Table wrapped in a Card component.

elements.name: Empty state
elements.description: When no orders exist, shows a floating ClipboardList icon with a message and a Create button.

elements.name: Banner notifications
elements.description: Uses the Banner component for success/error/warning messages (replacing legacy StatusBanner). Approval notices use a warning Banner with dismiss button.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each purchase order has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. PO-001). Shown in order labels, cards, and table rows for easy identification and search.

before_you_start.text: You need at least one active supplier set up before creating purchase orders.
Your role needs the purchases.write permission to create, update, or approve orders.
To use reorder suggestions, products in your catalog must have reorder points configured.

steps: Select a branch and supplier in the Create Order form.
Optionally set an expected delivery date.
Add line items — choose variant, enter quantity and unit cost.
Or click Use Reorder Suggestions to auto-fill low-stock items.
Click Create. If approval is required, the order goes to Pending Approval.
Find the order in the list — it will show a Pending Approval badge.
Click the Approve button on the order card.
If your role can approve directly, status changes to Approved. Otherwise an approval request is submitted.

warnings: You cannot remove lines from a purchase order once it has been saved — you can only reduce the quantity. Plan your lines carefully before creating.
Each variant can only appear once per order. Duplicate variants in the same order will cause the update to fail.
Editing an approved order may trigger the approval workflow again and put it back into Pending Approval status.
The supplier must be active (not archived) to create or update an order against them.

related_pages.reason: Set up and activate suppliers here before creating purchase orders.
After an order is approved, go here to record the stock you actually received.

entries.title: Purchase Order Wizard
entries.overview: A 4-step guided form to create a purchase order and optionally receive the goods in the same session. The wizard covers: selecting a supplier and branch, adding order lines (product, quantity, unit cost), reviewing totals, then recording received quantities with optional batch codes and expiry dates. If the order total triggers an approval threshold, the PO is created but stock is held until an approver acts.

elements.name: PageHeader
elements.description: Page hero with eyebrow, title, and subtitle. A 'Back to Orders' button with ChevronLeft icon links to the Purchase Orders list.

elements.name: Current Step KPI
elements.description: Card with ClipboardList icon in a blue container showing the name of the active wizard step (Details, Lines, Review, or Receive).

elements.name: Valid Lines KPI
elements.description: Card with Check icon in a green container showing the count of order lines that have a variant, quantity, and unit cost filled in.

elements.name: Order Total KPI
elements.description: Card with DollarSign icon in an accent container showing the running total of all valid lines (quantity x unit cost), formatted as currency.

elements.name: WizardSteps + ProgressBar
elements.description: Shared WizardSteps component showing four step pills (Details, Lines, Review, Receive) with the active step highlighted and completed steps checked. A ProgressBar above shows step N of 4.

elements.name: Supplier selector (Details step)
elements.description: The supplier this order is being placed with, labeled with a Truck icon. Only ACTIVE suppliers appear. If the supplier has a lead time configured, a smart hint card appears with Lightbulb icon showing the estimated delivery date.

elements.name: Branch selector (Details step)
elements.description: The branch where the ordered goods will be received, labeled with a Building2 icon. Stock is added to this branch when the order is received. You cannot change the branch after the PO is created.

elements.name: Expected date (Details step)
elements.description: Date picker labeled with CalendarClock icon. If the selected supplier has a lead time, a hint card with Lightbulb icon shows 'Supplier typically delivers in X days'. This date is informational and does not trigger automatic actions.

elements.name: Line item card (Lines step)
elements.description: Each line item is wrapped in its own Card component with hover effect. Contains: variant image thumbnail (or Package icon fallback), async variant search, labeled TextInput for quantity, labeled CurrencyInput for unit cost, labeled unit selector (filtered to show only the variant's base and sell units with labels), and a red Trash2 icon button to remove the line. A blue info hint box below the lines explains how units work for purchases.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Rounded-xl button with Plus icon and accent hover. Adds a new blank line card to the order.

elements.name: Remove Line button
elements.description: Red-tinted icon button with Trash2 icon on each line card. Removes that line from the order before it is created.

elements.name: Reorder suggestions hint
elements.description: A small Card with Lightbulb icon linking to the Purchase Orders list where reorder suggestions are available.

elements.name: Review manifest (Review step)
elements.description: Designed as an order manifest. Top section shows supplier info (Truck icon, blue), branch (MapPin icon, green), and expected date (CalendarClock icon, amber) in a summary card. Below, each valid line appears as a mini Card with Package icon, variant name, quantity/cost details, and line total. A prominent grand total card at the bottom shows the DollarSign icon and total amount.

elements.name: Create PO button
elements.description: Rounded-xl CTA button with Send icon. Creates the purchase order in the system. After clicking, the PO is locked and the wizard advances to the Receive step.

elements.name: Receive step
elements.description: Each line shows a Card with the variant name, expected quantity details, and a ProgressBar showing completion percentage (green at 100%, amber for partial, red at 0%). Quantity input uses labeled TextInput, cost uses labeled CurrencyInput, unit uses labeled SmartSelect. Progress bars animate in with nvi-bounce-in.

elements.name: Batch code input (per line)
elements.description: Labeled TextInput for batch/lot number. Only visible when batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings. Critical for tracing perishable goods and regulated items.

elements.name: Expiry date input (per line)
elements.description: Labeled date picker for expiry dates. Only visible when batch tracking is enabled. Items with different expiry dates should be split into separate lines.

elements.name: Receive button
elements.description: Rounded-xl CTA button with Check icon. Records all quantities as received and updates stock levels immediately. Shows orbit spinner while processing.

elements.name: Approve Order button
elements.description: Rounded-xl button with Check icon (green). Appears on the Receive step to approve the created PO inline.

elements.name: Finish Later button
elements.description: Rounded-xl button with X icon linking back to Purchase Orders list. Allows returning to receive goods later from the Receiving page.

elements.name: Approval notice banner
elements.description: Banner component with warning severity. Appears when the PO total exceeds the approval threshold. Dismissible with close button.

elements.name: Back / Next buttons
elements.description: Rounded-xl buttons with ChevronLeft (Back) and ChevronRight (Next) icons. Navigate between wizard steps without losing data. After PO creation, earlier steps are locked.

elements.name: EmptyState (no lines)
elements.description: EmptyState component with Package icon shown on the Receive step when there are no lines to receive.

before_you_start.text: You need the purchases.write permission.
The supplier and destination branch must already exist — create them first if needed.
Enable batch tracking in Business Settings if you need to record batch codes and expiry dates on received goods.

steps: On the Details step, select the branch where goods will be received, choose the supplier, and set the expected delivery date. Click Next.
On the Lines step, add one line per product: select the variant, enter quantity, unit cost, and unit of measure. Click Next.
On the Review step, check all quantities, costs, and the total. Click Create PO to lock the order.
The wizard advances to the Receive step — receive immediately or skip to receive later from the Receiving page.

common_tasks.task: Receive goods immediately after creating the PO
steps: After creating the PO, you are on the Receive step automatically.
Adjust each quantity to match what actually arrived (the ordered quantity is pre-filled).
If batch tracking is enabled, enter the batch code and expiry date for each line.
Click Receive. Stock levels at the branch update immediately.
On the Receive step, enter only the quantities that actually arrived for each line — leave lines at 0 if nothing arrived for that item.
Click Receive to update stock for the delivered portion.
Return to the Receiving page later to record the remaining goods when they arrive — the PO stays open.

warnings: If an approval is triggered, stock will not be added to the branch until the approval is granted on the Approvals page. The PO shows as PENDING in the meantime.
Unit cost entered here forms the cost basis for stock valuation and profit calculations in reports. Enter the correct supplier price — incorrect values affect margin reporting.
The PO cannot be edited after creation. If you made an error (wrong supplier, wrong branch, wrong cost), contact your supervisor — the PO may need to be voided and a new one raised.

entries.title: Purchases
entries.overview: Record supplier purchases, track payment progress, and manage your buying activity. Each purchase card features a hero cost number colored by payment status: emerald when fully paid, amber when partially paid, and red when unpaid. A ProgressBar tracks payment completion with per-method colored pills (Cash emerald, Card blue, Mobile purple, Bank amber). Section headers use colored left borders — blue for purchase details and amber for payment sections — creating clear visual hierarchy across the page.

elements.name: Total Purchases KPI
elements.description: Count of purchase records in the current filtered view with ShoppingCart icon. Apply date filters to understand purchasing volume over a period.

elements.name: This Month Spend KPI
elements.description: Total spend for purchases created this calendar month, formatted as currency with TrendingUp icon. Helps track buying velocity.

elements.name: Unpaid Balance KPI
elements.description: Sum of all outstanding balances across visible purchases with AlertCircle icon. Shows how much you still owe suppliers.

elements.name: Active Branch KPI
elements.description: Shows the currently scoped branch with Building2 icon. Displays 'All branches' if no branch filter is active.

elements.name: Search / Filters Bar
elements.description: Filter purchases by keyword, status (DRAFT, APPROVED, RECEIVED, etc.), branch, supplier, and date range. ListFilters component with advanced toggle.

elements.name: Create purchase button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with ShoppingCart icon. Opens the Create purchase modal. Only shown to users with the purchases.write permission.

elements.name: Create purchase modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Create purchase header button (or from the Reorder action on an existing purchase, which pre-fills supplier + lines). Organised into sections: Basic Info (branch + supplier selects) and Line Items (one row per line with variant, quantity, unit, unit cost, and remove button). ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful create.

elements.name: Line Item Card
elements.description: Each purchase line is a mini Card component with nvi-card-hover animation. Contains AsyncSmartSelect for variant, TextInput for quantity, SmartSelect for unit (filtered to show only the variant's base and sell units with labels), CurrencyInput for unit cost (rounded-xl), and a red-tinted remove button with Trash2 icon. A blue info hint box below the lines explains how units work for purchases.

elements.name: Purchase Card (card view)
elements.description: Transaction card with cost as the hero number (large, bold) colored by payment status (emerald paid, amber partial, red unpaid). Shows supplier with Building2 icon, line item summary (Package icon: '3 items, 150 units'), branch with MapPin icon, relative time with Clock icon, payment status ProgressBar, per-method colored payment history pills, and inline expandable payment form.

elements.name: Payment Status Indicator
elements.description: Visual payment status: paid shows emerald circle with Check icon, partial shows amber ProgressBar with percentage and per-method colored pills, unpaid shows red circle with AlertCircle icon. Colors match the hero cost number on each card.

elements.name: Record payment modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Record payment action on any unpaid purchase (card or table row). Shows remaining balance in the header, payment method select, amount input, and conditional reference / custom method label fields. ESC or backdrop click closes. Closes automatically after a successful recording and refreshes the list.

elements.name: Table View
elements.description: Wrapped in Card component. Columns: Supplier (with Building2 icon), Branch, Status (StatusBadge), Payment (colored dot + percentage), Date (relative time), Total (right-aligned). SortableTableHeaders on all data columns. Shared inline payment panel at bottom.

elements.name: Empty State
elements.description: EmptyState component with floating ShoppingCart icon (nvi-float animation). Shows when no purchases exist.

elements.name: Reorder Button
elements.description: Opens the Create purchase modal pre-filled with the same supplier and line items from a previous purchase. Adjust as needed and submit as a new purchase.

elements.name: Offline PIN verification
elements.description: Card with Lock icon. When working offline, a PIN verification form with TextInput appears before purchase creation is allowed.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills with nvi-status-fade animation showing purchase status: DRAFT, PENDING, APPROVED, PARTIAL, RECEIVED, CANCELLED.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Supplier, Branch, Status, Date, Total, and Payment status.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each purchase has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. PUR-001) for easy identification. Shown below cost in card view and below supplier name in table view.

elements.name: Banner (notifications)
elements.description: Replaces StatusBanner. Displays success/error/warning/info messages with severity-based styling, dismiss button, and optional action. Uses Banner component from notifications system.

before_you_start.text: You need at least one active supplier. Set up suppliers before recording purchases.
Your role needs the purchases.write permission to create purchases or record payments.

steps: Select a branch and supplier in the Create Purchase form.
Add a line for each product: select the variant, enter quantity and unit cost.
Click Create Purchase. The purchase is saved with a DRAFT status.

common_tasks.task: Record a payment against a purchase
steps: In the Record Payment form, select the purchase from the Purchase Selector.
Choose the payment method and enter the amount.
For Bank Transfer or Mobile Money, enter the reference number.
Click Record Payment.

warnings: Bank Transfer and Mobile Money payments require a reference number — the form will warn you if it's missing.
The supplier must be active to create a purchase against them. Archived suppliers won't appear in the selector.
Purchases created offline are queued and synced when you reconnect — do not clear browser storage before syncing.

related_pages.reason: Set up your suppliers here before recording purchases.
Stock levels update when purchases are received — check here after posting.

entries.title: Receipts
entries.overview: View the full history of sales receipts across all branches. Uses a standard ListPage layout with KPI strip, card and table views, and expandable action sections. From this page you can find a specific receipt, preview and reprint it, process a refund for a completed sale, settle a customer's outstanding credit balance, or handle a return when the original receipt cannot be found. Each action is permission-controlled — viewing is open to all, but refunds, credit settlement, and no-receipt returns each require specific permissions.

elements.name: KPI strip
elements.description: Four metric cards at the top of the page: Total receipts (Receipt icon, gold container), Today's receipts (Calendar icon, emerald container), Outstanding credit (DollarSign icon, amber container), and Current filter (Filter icon, blue container showing active filter description). Each card has a colored icon container with the metric value displayed prominently.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Find receipts by receipt number, customer name, or product name. Use this when a customer brings in a receipt and you need to pull up the original sale quickly.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restrict results to receipts from a specific branch. Use this when you are a branch manager reviewing your branch's sales, or when a customer made their purchase at a different branch and you need to filter to that location.

elements.name: Payment method filter
elements.description: Filter receipts by payment method (Cash, Card, Mobile Money, Bank Transfer, Other). Useful for reconciliation by payment type.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restrict results to a specific time window. Combine with Branch filter for shift-end or daily reconciliation.

elements.name: Receipt transaction card
elements.description: Each receipt card tells the transaction story: receipt number header with Hash icon (monospace font), hero total amount (large bold), payment method icon badges (Banknote for Cash, CreditCard for Card, Smartphone for Mobile, Building for Bank), customer name with Users icon, branch with Building2 icon, cashier with User icon, relative timestamp with Clock icon, and a status dot (green for completed, amber for credit outstanding). Cards have nvi-card-hover animation and gold ring highlight when selected.

elements.name: Receipt detail modal
elements.description: Opens as a centered modal dialog when a receipt is selected. Shows the full ReceiptPreview with line items, totals, and payment details. The modal also hosts the Refund form and Credit Settlement form (when applicable) so everything for the selected receipt is in one place. Includes Compact/Detailed mode toggle buttons, Reprint button, and a close button (X icon). ESC closes, clicking the backdrop closes, body scroll is locked while open, and focus is trapped inside for keyboard users.

elements.name: Action buttons per card
elements.description: Each receipt card has icon action buttons at the bottom: Eye (view detail), Printer (reprint), and RotateCcw (refund, danger variant — only shown if user has sales.write permission). All buttons use the ActionButtons component with nvi-press animation.

elements.name: Reprint button
elements.description: Sends the receipt to the connected thermal printer (ESC/POS protocol) with Printer icon. If no printer is connected or configured, the receipt preview still opens and can be printed through the browser's print function as a fallback.

elements.name: Refund form
elements.description: Appears inside the receipt detail modal when a receipt with a sale is selected. Contains a reason text input, a return-to-stock checkbox, and a red-outlined submit button. Requires the sales.write permission.

elements.name: Credit settlement form
elements.description: Appears inside the receipt detail modal when the selected receipt has an outstanding credit balance. Displays the outstanding amount and due date, a payment method select, amount input, reference, and a custom method label input when 'Other' is selected. Submit button with DollarSign icon. Requires the sales.credit.settle permission.

elements.name: Return button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with RotateCcw icon. Opens the Return Without Receipt dialog. Only visible if you have the sales.return.without-receipt permission.

elements.name: Return Without Receipt dialog
elements.description: Centered modal opened from the Return button in the page header. Contains a branch selector, optional customer selector, a reason field, a return-to-stock checkbox, and a list of item rows (variant / quantity / unit price). A Plus button adds more rows. Submit closes the dialog on success and refreshes the list. ESC or the backdrop closes without submitting. Requires the sales.return.without-receipt permission.

elements.name: Table view
elements.description: Card-wrapped table with icon column headers (Hash for receipt number, Users for customer, Calendar for issued date, DollarSign for total). Receipt number column shows monospace font with status dot. Total is right-aligned. Payment method shown as icon pill badge. Eye and Printer icon action buttons per row.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card layout (transaction story cards with full detail) and table layout (compact rows for scanning large volumes).

elements.name: Connect printer button
elements.description: Header action button with Printer icon to connect an ESC/POS thermal printer. Hardware print checkbox toggles between hardware and browser printing.

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of receipts. Apply filters first to narrow the result set — searching an unfiltered full receipt history can return thousands of records.

elements.name: Empty state
elements.description: When no receipts match the current filters, shows a floating Receipt icon with nvi-float animation and 'No receipts found' message.

elements.name: Average stats row
elements.description: Below filters, shows average sale value and average items per sale across the current page of receipts.

elements.name: Preview mode toggle
elements.description: Toggle between Compact and Detailed receipt preview modes with rounded-xl toggle buttons. Compact shows a summary; Detailed shows all line items, payments, and customer information.

elements.name: Settlement custom method label
elements.description: When the settlement payment method is 'OTHER', a TextInput with label appears to enter a custom label describing the payment method.

elements.name: Keyboard shortcuts (modals)
elements.description: Both the receipt detail modal and the Return Without Receipt dialog support ESC to close, backdrop click to close, and keep keyboard focus inside the dialog while it is open.

before_you_start.text: No special permission is required to view receipts.
The sales.write permission is required to process refunds.
The sales.credit.settle permission is required to settle a customer's credit balance.
The sales.return.without-receipt permission is required to process returns without the original receipt.

steps: Search for the receipt by number or customer name.
Click the receipt card or the Eye icon to open the receipt detail modal.
Click the Reprint button (Printer icon) in the modal header.
The receipt is sent to the connected printer. If no printer is connected, use the browser's print function from the preview.
Find and open the original receipt using the search or filters.
In the receipt detail modal, scroll to the Refund sale section.
Enter an optional reason and choose whether to return items to stock.
Click Refund sale. Payment is reversed and returned items are added back to stock at the original branch.
A refund receipt is generated automatically and can be printed.
Search for a receipt for the customer, or use the filters to find their records.
Click the receipt to open the detail modal. The Credit settlement section appears automatically when there is an outstanding balance.
Select the payment method, enter the amount being paid, and confirm. The customer's outstanding credit balance reduces immediately.
Click the Return button in the page header to open the Return Without Receipt dialog.
Select the branch, optionally select a customer, and enter a reason.
Add return items using the Plus button — select variant, enter quantity and unit price for each.
Toggle return-to-stock if items should be added back to inventory.
Click Submit return.

warnings: Refunds are irreversible — once confirmed, the payment is reversed and stock is returned. Verify you have the correct receipt and the correct lines selected before confirming.
Settling credit updates the customer's balance immediately and cannot be undone. Double-check the payment amount before confirming.
Return Without Receipt bypasses normal receipt validation. Only use it when the receipt genuinely cannot be found — misuse can create stock discrepancies that are difficult to trace and correct.

entries.title: Receiving
entries.overview: Record the arrival of goods against an existing purchase order or purchase. The hero quantity on each receiving card displays in emerald, with colored source badges distinguishing the origin: emerald for Purchase, blue for Purchase Order, and muted for Manual entries. Quality notes appear in amber-tinted containers with MessageSquare icons, the barcode scanner zone uses a blue highlight, and the receipt confirmation zone is purple-tinted. Supports partial deliveries, batch code tracking, and expiry date recording.

elements.name: KPI strip
elements.description: Four metric cards at the top of the page: Recent receives (total count with PackageCheck icon), Manual entries (receives not linked to a PO or purchase, ClipboardEdit icon), Batched lines (receives with batch codes, Layers icon), and Target mode indicator (shows current source type with ShoppingCart or Truck icon). Each card has a colored icon container.

elements.name: Source type selector
elements.description: Two icon buttons to choose whether to receive goods against a Purchase Order (Truck icon in blue) or a Purchase (ShoppingCart icon in emerald). The selected type has a highlighted border and background. This controls which documents appear in the Document selector.

elements.name: Document selector
elements.description: Select the specific purchase order or purchase you are receiving goods against. Only documents in a receivable state appear (e.g. approved POs with outstanding quantities). The document's supplier, branch, and order lines load automatically once you select it.

elements.name: Receiving line card
elements.description: Each receiving line sits inside its own card with labeled form fields: a variant search (async select with barcode scan option), quantity (number input), unit selector, unit cost (currency input), and quality note (text input). If batch tracking is enabled, batch code input with a ScanBarcode icon button and expiry date picker also appear. A trash icon button removes the line.

elements.name: Quantity received (per line)
elements.description: Enter the actual quantity delivered for this line with a labeled field. You can enter less than the outstanding quantity to record a partial delivery — the remaining balance stays open on the PO for a future receiving session. Enter 0 if nothing arrived for that item.

elements.name: Batch code input with scanner (per line)
elements.description: Enter the batch or lot number from the supplier's packaging label, or tap the ScanBarcode icon button to activate barcode scanning. When scanning is active, a gold 'Scanning...' indicator appears below the field with a bounce-in animation. Required if batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings.

elements.name: Expiry date input (per line)
elements.description: Enter the expiry date for perishable or regulated items. Required if the variant has expiry tracking enabled. Stock with different expiry dates from the same delivery should be split into separate receiving lines so each batch has its own expiry recorded.

elements.name: Quality note (per line)
elements.description: A labeled text input per receiving line for recording quality observations about the received goods. Notes are aggregated into the override reason on submission.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Shows a Plus icon. Adds a new receiving line for an additional item. Use this if you need to receive a variant that was not on the original purchase order — for example, a bonus item or a substitution sent by the supplier.

elements.name: Remove Line button (trash icon)
elements.description: A trash icon button on each line card that removes that line from the receiving session before you submit. Disabled when only one line remains. Lines cannot be removed after submission.

elements.name: Record Receiving button
elements.description: Gold CTA button with PackageCheck icon. Records all receiving lines and updates stock levels at the branch immediately. Shows a spinner while processing. This action cannot be undone — verify all quantities, batch codes, and expiry dates before clicking.

elements.name: Override reason field
elements.description: A labeled text input next to the document selector. When overriding the expected quantity, provide a reason for the discrepancy (e.g. partial delivery, damaged items).

elements.name: Receiving receipt card (card view)
elements.description: Each receiving line displays as a card with: emerald hero quantity (large bold number with unit), variant name, unit cost, colored source badge (Purchase with ShoppingCart icon in emerald, Purchase Order with Truck icon in blue, or Manual with ClipboardEdit icon in muted), quality note (amber-tinted container with MessageSquare icon if present), batch and expiry chips (Hash icon for batch code, Calendar icon for expiry date), and a footer with relative time and formatted date. Cards have hover lift animation.

elements.name: Table view
elements.description: Wrapped in a Card component. Columns: Variant (with Package icon), Quantity/Unit, Unit Cost (right-aligned), Source (icon badge), Batch Code (Hash icon chips, conditional), Quality Note (MessageSquare icon, truncated), and Date. Sortable column headers.

elements.name: Header badges
elements.description: Two badges next to the page title: a Live Queue badge (gold with Radio icon) and a tracking mode badge showing either Batch Tracking (Layers icon) or Standard (Package icon).

elements.name: Empty state
elements.description: When no receiving records exist, shows a floating PackageCheck icon with a message and a button to open the receiving form.

elements.name: Receive stock button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with PackageCheck icon. Opens the Receive stock modal. Only shown to users with the purchases.write permission.

elements.name: Receive stock modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Receive stock header button (or the empty-state CTA). Hosts the full receiving form: source type toggle (Purchase / PO), document selector, override reason, and one or more line items with variant, quantity, unit, cost, optional batch code (auto-generate + barcode scan), expiry date, and quality note. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after successful recording; the list auto-refreshes.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Variant, Quantity, Unit Cost, Source, and Date.

before_you_start.text: You need the purchases.write permission.
A purchase order or purchase must already exist and have outstanding quantities — you cannot receive against a PO that is already fully received or cancelled.
Enable batch tracking in Business Settings before receiving if you need to record batch codes and expiry dates.

common_tasks.task: Receive a full delivery against a purchase order
steps: Set Link type to Purchase Order and select the PO from the Document selector.
The lines from the PO are pre-filled with the outstanding quantities. Verify each quantity matches what arrived.
If batch tracking is enabled, enter batch codes and expiry dates for each line.
Click Submit. Stock levels at the branch update immediately.
Select the PO from the Document selector.
For lines that arrived, enter the actual quantity received. For lines that did not arrive, enter 0 or leave them.
Click Submit. The received quantities are added to stock. The PO stays open with the remaining balance for a future receiving session.

common_tasks.task: Receive an unlisted or bonus item
steps: Select the related PO or Purchase from the Document selector.
Click Add Line and select the variant that arrived.
Enter the quantity, unit cost, and any batch details.
Click Submit to record the receiving and update stock.

warnings: Submitted receiving records update stock immediately and cannot be edited or deleted. Verify all quantities, batch codes, and expiry dates before clicking Submit.
Partial receiving leaves the PO open — come back to this page and select the same PO when the remaining goods arrive to complete the receiving.
Batch codes must be unique within a branch and variant combination. If you enter a duplicate batch code, the system will warn you before allowing submission.

entries.title: Reports
entries.overview: This route redirects automatically to the Reports Overview section — the entry point for the full analytics dashboard. The dashboard has five sections accessible via tabs: Overview (business summary KPIs and totals), Sales & Profit (revenue trends, gross margin, and top products), Customers (spending patterns, credit, and new vs returning), Inventory (stock values, movements, and low-stock alerts), and Operations (shift reconciliation, transfers, and approvals). Use the date range and branch filters on each section to focus the data.

elements.name: Automatic redirect
elements.description: Navigates you directly to the Reports Overview section on page load. No interaction is needed — the browser redirects automatically. If you want a specific section, use the section tabs once you land on the Reports dashboard.

before_you_start.text: You need the reports.read permission to access the Reports dashboard.

common_tasks.task: Go directly to a specific report section
steps: Navigate to Reports — the page redirects to the Overview section automatically.
Use the section tabs at the top of the dashboard to switch to Sales & Profit, Customers, Inventory, or Operations.

entries.title: Reports Dashboard
entries.overview: Business analytics dashboard with five reporting sections. Each section shows KPI cards and charts relevant to that area of the business. All data can be filtered by date range and branch. Use this page for performance reviews, end-of-period analysis, and operational monitoring.

elements.name: Section tabs
elements.description: Five tabs switch between reporting areas. Overview — business-wide summary of revenue, profit, transactions, and active customers for the period. Sales & Profit — revenue trends over time, gross margin analysis, top-selling products, and payment method breakdown. Customers — new vs returning customers, top customers by spend, outstanding credit balances, and customer activity trends. Inventory — current stock value by branch, stock movement summary (sales out, stock received, adjustments, transfers), and low-stock alerts. Operations — shift variance summary, transfer activity and status, approval queue totals, and expense breakdown by category.

elements.name: Date range filter
elements.description: Sets the reporting period for all KPI cards and charts on the current section. Changing the date range reloads all data. Common presets include Today, This Week, This Month, and Last Month — or set a custom From/To range for any period.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts all report data to a specific branch. If no branch is selected, data from all branches is aggregated into a business-wide view. Use this to compare branches or review a specific location's performance.

elements.name: KPI cards (Overview section)
elements.description: Summary metrics for the selected period: total revenue, gross profit, net transactions, average sale value, and number of active customers. Use these as the headline figures for any performance review.

elements.name: KPI cards (Sales & Profit section)
elements.description: Revenue and margin metrics: total sales by period, gross margin percentage, cost of goods sold, and revenue from top product categories.

elements.name: KPI cards (Customers section)
elements.description: Customer activity metrics: total customers, new customers in the period, returning customer rate, and total outstanding credit balance across all customers.

elements.name: KPI cards (Inventory section)
elements.description: Stock health metrics: total stock value across all branches, number of variants with low or zero stock, total stock received in the period, and total stock adjustments.

elements.name: KPI cards (Operations section)
elements.description: Operational metrics: total shift variance (cash discrepancy across all closed shifts), number of pending approvals, total transfers in transit, and total expenses for the period.

elements.name: Charts (per section)
elements.description: Visual breakdowns that complement the KPI cards. Examples: revenue over time (line chart), top products by units sold (bar chart), stock movement categories (pie chart), customer spending tiers (bar chart). Charts update automatically when date range or branch filters change.

elements.name: Export report button
elements.description: Downloads the current section's data as a CSV file, reflecting the date range and branch filter currently applied. Available on most sections. Use this for sharing data with external tools or for deeper analysis in a spreadsheet.

elements.name: Export PDF button
elements.description: Generates a branded, print-ready PDF of the currently active section. The PDF includes a header with the business name, period, branch, and currency; the workspace KPI strip; and full detail tables for that section (top products, customer sales, low stock, expiring batches, staff performance, stock count variance, etc., depending on section). The library is loaded only when you click the button, so it does not slow down the page. Use this for board reports, audits, or sharing snapshots with people who do not log in to the system.

elements.name: Stock Purchase Costs card
elements.description: Total cost of stock added through purchase-type adjustments (Unrecorded Purchase, Initial Stock) and opening stock imports. This amount is deducted from gross profit in the net profit calculation.

before_you_start.text: You need the reports.read permission to access the Reports dashboard.
Your subscription tier must include access to reports — lower tiers may have restricted access to some sections.
Data reflects completed transactions only — pending, draft, or approval-held records are not included in report totals.

common_tasks.task: View sales summary for the current month
steps: Go to the Sales & Profit section tab.
Set the date range to This Month.
Review the KPI cards for total revenue and gross margin, and the charts for revenue trends and top products.
Go to the Inventory section tab.
Set Branch filter to the branch you want to review (or leave blank for all branches).
The Stock Value KPI card shows the current total inventory value for the selected branch.

common_tasks.task: Export a report section to CSV
steps: Navigate to the section you want to export.
Set the date range and branch filter as needed.
Click Export report. A CSV file downloads to your device with the section's data.

warnings: Reports show data from completed transactions only — real-time figures at the POS may differ slightly from what appears here until transactions are fully processed.
Changing the date range or branch filter reloads all data automatically — give the page a moment to update before reading figures.

entries.title: Search
entries.overview: Global search with per-section colored identity: recent searches in blue, saved searches in amber, and popular items in emerald. The search bar uses a neutral container, while result section headers are color-coded — blue for Products, purple for Customers, amber for Receipts, and cyan for Transfers. Type at least two characters for live auto-suggest, then submit for full results grouped by entity type. Each result links directly to the relevant record.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Type your search query here. After two characters, live suggestions appear showing matching variant names, SKUs, customer names, and receipt numbers. Press Enter or click Search to run a full search across all entity types.

elements.name: Search button
elements.description: Runs a full search for the current query across all entity types. Use this when you want complete results beyond the auto-suggest preview, or after refining your query.

elements.name: Total matches count
elements.description: Total number of records matched across all entity types combined. If this is very high, refine your query to be more specific — for example, include a SKU or receipt number.

elements.name: Products section
elements.description: Blue-headed section listing products whose name matches the query, showing each product's name and its variants. Click a product to navigate to its catalogue detail page where you can view pricing, stock levels, and settings.

elements.name: Variants section
elements.description: Lists product variants matching the query by SKU or variant name. Click a variant to open its detail page. Use this when you have a SKU from a physical label or a supplier document and need to find the exact variant in the system.

elements.name: Receipts section
elements.description: Amber-headed section listing receipts whose receipt number matches the query. Click a receipt to open its full detail page, where you can reprint it or initiate a refund.

elements.name: Customers section
elements.description: Purple-headed section listing customers whose name matches the query. Click a customer to open their profile, which includes their credit balance, purchase history, and contact details.

elements.name: Transfers section
elements.description: Cyan-headed section listing stock transfer records matching the query, showing the source and destination branch names. Click a transfer to view its full detail and current status.

elements.name: Barcode scanner
elements.description: Scan a barcode using your device camera to instantly search for the matching product or variant.

elements.name: Recent searches
elements.description: Shows your recent search terms in a blue-tinted section, stored locally. Click a recent search to re-run it. Clear all with the clear button.

elements.name: Popular items
elements.description: Shows your most frequently sold items in an emerald-tinted section with their sales count.

elements.name: Saved searches
elements.description: Save a search query for quick access later in an amber-tinted section. Saved searches persist across sessions.

elements.name: Save this search button
elements.description: Saves the current search query with a custom name.

elements.name: Type filter tabs
elements.description: Filter results by type: All, Products, Customers, Receipts, Transfers. Each tab shows a count badge.

elements.name: Results grid layout
elements.description: Search results displayed in a two-column grid on larger screens. Products and Variants in separate sections.

elements.name: KPI cards (result counts)
elements.description: Three KPI cards showing count of matching Products, Customers, and Transfers.

elements.name: Typeahead suggestions
elements.description: As you type, matching suggestions appear in a dropdown below the search input.

before_you_start.text: No special permissions are required to use Global Search — all users can access it.
Type at least 2 characters to activate the auto-suggest. Single-character queries return no results.

common_tasks.task: Find a product by name or SKU
steps: Type the product name or SKU into the search input.
Results appear in the Products section (by name) or Variants section (by SKU).
Click the result to navigate directly to the product or variant detail page.

common_tasks.task: Find and reprint a receipt by number
steps: Type the receipt number (or part of it) into the search input.
Click the receipt in the Receipts section.
The receipt detail page opens — use the reprint or refund options there.

warnings: Results are limited to your current business only — you cannot search across multiple businesses.

If your role restricts access to a module (e.g. you cannot access Customers), results from that module may still appear in search — but clicking them will show a permission error rather than the record.

entries.title: Branch Settings
entries.overview: Branch location profiles with blue Building2 icon containers, per-contact colored icons (amber MapPin for address, emerald Phone for contact, purple Clock for operating hours), blue edit ring highlight, and colored left accent borders. Each branch card shows a status dot, price list tag, active user count, and expandable performance metrics.

elements.name: Total branches KPI
elements.description: Shows total number of branches with a Building2 icon in a gold accent container. Your subscription plan sets a maximum branch limit.

elements.name: With price list KPI
elements.description: How many branches have a price list assigned, shown with a Tag icon in a blue container. Branches without a price list use default product prices at POS.

elements.name: Active users KPI
elements.description: Total active users across all branches, shown with a Users icon in an emerald container.

elements.name: Current page KPI
elements.description: Shows the current page number with a ListFilter icon in an amber container. Use pagination controls at the bottom to navigate between pages.

elements.name: Name input (create form)
elements.description: Required. The display name for the new branch — shown in branch selectors throughout the system including POS, stock movements, transfers, and reports. Must be unique within your business.

elements.name: Address input (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The physical street address of the branch. Shown on printed receipts and in some reports.

elements.name: Phone input (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The branch's contact phone number. If entered, it prints on receipts issued at this branch and appears in the branch directory.

elements.name: Opening / Closing time (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Set the daily operating hours for the new branch. These are displayed as a formatted time range on branch cards (e.g. 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM).

elements.name: Price List selector (create form)
elements.description: Optionally link an existing price list to this branch. Searchable dropdown that loads price lists from the server. Price lists must be created first on the Price Lists page.

elements.name: Create Branch button
elements.description: Saves the new branch immediately with a Plus icon. The branch becomes available across the system as soon as it is created.

elements.name: Branch location card
elements.description: Each branch is displayed as a location profile card with: blue Building2 icon header, name in bold, address with amber MapPin icon, phone with emerald Phone icon, operating hours badge with purple Clock icon, price list tag badge, active user count badge with Users icon, and an expandable performance section. Card has a colored left accent border.

elements.name: Edit button (per branch)
elements.description: Pencil icon button that enters edit mode for that branch card. In table view, clicking Edit auto-switches to card view where inline editing is available. Edit mode shows a blue ring highlight around the card.

elements.name: Save / Cancel (edit mode)
elements.description: Save button with Check icon commits edits immediately. Cancel button with X icon reverts all fields without making changes.

elements.name: Operating Hours fields (edit mode)
elements.description: Opening Time and Closing Time text inputs in edit mode. Set the daily operating hours for each branch. Displayed as a formatted AM/PM range on branch cards.

elements.name: Branch performance panel
elements.description: Click 'Performance' on a branch card to expand a panel showing three mini stat boxes: Sales today (ShoppingCart icon, emerald), Sale count (Hash icon, blue), and Stock units (Package icon, amber). Click again to collapse.

elements.name: Active user count badge
elements.description: Emerald badge with Users icon showing how many users are assigned and active at each branch, displayed in both card and table views.

elements.name: Table view (ViewToggle)
elements.description: Toggle between card view and table view using the Cards/Table buttons. Table view wraps in a Card component and shows columns: Name (with Building2 icon), Address (with MapPin icon), Phone, Hours (with Clock icon), Price List (as tag), Users (with Users icon), and Edit action.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Name, Address, and Phone.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filter branches by name using the search input with a Search icon. Located above the branch list.

elements.name: Empty state
elements.description: When no branches exist, a floating Building2 icon is shown with a title and description prompting you to create your first branch.

before_you_start.text: You need the settings.write permission to create or edit branches.
Price lists must be created on the Price Lists page before they can be assigned to a branch here.

steps: Open the Create branch collapsible section.
Enter the branch name (required). Optionally add address, phone, opening/closing times.
Select a price list from the searchable dropdown if this branch uses custom pricing.
Click Create branch. The branch is immediately available across the system.

common_tasks.task: Assign a price list to an existing branch
steps: Find the branch card and click the Edit (Pencil) button.
Select the price list from the searchable Price List dropdown.
Click Save (Check icon). New sales at this branch will now use the custom prices.
Find the branch card in card view.
Click the Performance button to expand the performance panel.
Review today's sales total, sale count, and stock units. Click again to collapse.

warnings: Branch names must be unique within your business. Duplicate names are rejected when you try to save.
You cannot delete a branch from this page. Branches with any transaction history cannot be deleted — contact support if a branch needs to be permanently removed or archived.
Changing a branch's price list takes effect on the next new sale, not retroactively. Past receipts at that branch are not affected.
Editing branches is only available in card view. Clicking Edit in table view will auto-switch to card view.

entries.title: Business Settings
entries.overview: Configuration command center with 11 collapsible sections, each with a unique color identity and left border accent: blue for business profile, amber for subscription, purple for requests, emerald for approvals, cyan for stock policies, amber for POS policies, blue for notifications, purple for localization, emerald for units, blue for support, amber for changelog, and red for the danger zone. Colored icon containers and section-specific save buttons.

elements.name: PageHeader with edit/save actions
elements.description: Unified page header with eyebrow, title, subtitle, and badge pills. The actions slot contains the Edit (Pencil icon), Save (Check icon), and Cancel (X icon) buttons plus an unsaved-changes amber badge when dirty. Replaces PremiumPageHeader.

elements.name: KPI strip — 4 cards
elements.description: A 4-column grid of Card KPIs with icon containers: Business (Building2/gold), Subscription (CreditCard/gold with tier-colored badge — amber=Starter, blue=Business, gold=Enterprise), Roles (Shield/blue), Users (Users/emerald). Each has nvi-card-hover animation.

elements.name: Settings search
elements.description: A Card-wrapped search bar with Search icon that filters visible settings sections by keyword. Only the configurable sections (Approval, Stock, POS, Notifications, Localization) are filtered.

elements.name: Business Info section (Building2)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with blue left border and blue Building2 icon container in the header. Shows business name and read-only business ID in a 2-column grid. ChevronDown toggle. nvi-slide-in-bottom animation.

elements.name: Subscription section (CreditCard)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with amber left border and amber CreditCard icon container in the header, plus tier-colored badge (amber/blue/gold). Shows tier/status text, subscription warnings as Banner, trial delete notice as error Banner, end-date and days-remaining sub-cards, and 4 usage ProgressBars (users, branches, products, devices) with color coding (green/amber/red). Grace period shows amber badge.

elements.name: Subscription Requests section (Send)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with Send icon header. Contains a 3-column form: type SmartSelect, tier SmartSelect, reason TextInput. Send Request button with Send icon and nvi-press. Request history as sub-Cards showing type, tier, status, reason, response, timestamp. EmptyState with Send icon when empty.

elements.name: Read-only Banner
elements.description: An info Banner shown when settings are not in edit mode, telling the user to click Edit to make changes.

elements.name: Approval Settings section (CheckSquare)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with purple left border and purple CheckSquare icon container in the header, section timestamp badge, and Reset button (RotateCcw icon) when editing. Contains AnalogToggles for stock adjust, refund, purchase, transfer, expense — each with a CurrencyInput threshold. Discount thresholds sub-section with TextInput and CurrencyInput. All inputs use nvi-border tokens.

elements.name: Stock Policies section (Package)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with emerald left border and emerald Package icon container in the header, timestamp badge, Reset button. AnalogToggles for negative stock and batch tracking. SmartSelects for FIFO/FEFO, valuation method, expiry policy, transfer batch policy. TextInputs with labels for expiry alert days and low stock threshold. Hint text at bottom.

elements.name: POS Policies section (ShoppingCart)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with cyan left border and cyan ShoppingCart icon container in the header, timestamp badge, Reset button. SmartSelect for receipt template. AnalogToggles for branch contact, credit, price edit, shift tracking, refund return. TextInputs with labels for receipt header/footer, shift variance, discount thresholds. Offline limits sub-section with 3-column TextInputs and tier-cap hints. Banner warning when offline not available.

elements.name: Notification Settings section (Bell)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with Bell icon header, timestamp badge. Channel toggles (email, WhatsApp with Enterprise gate). Recipients sub-section with 3 sub-Cards (Global, Email, WhatsApp) each containing Checkbox grid for role selection, AnalogToggle for branch-scoped. Events sub-section with per-group sub-Cards containing per-event AnalogToggles with hint text.

elements.name: Localization section (Globe)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with Globe icon header, timestamp badge. 3-column grid: offline price variance TextInput, read-only currency display with hint, timezone SmartSelect (editing) or static (read-only), date format SmartSelect (editing) or static. Hint text at bottom.

elements.name: Units section (Ruler)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with Ruler icon header. UnitHelpPanel. 3-column create form: label TextInput, code TextInput, type native select. Add Unit button with Plus icon and nvi-press. Unit list as sub-Cards showing label, type, custom/global badge. EmptyState with Ruler icon when empty.

elements.name: Expense Categories section (Receipt)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with amber left border and amber Receipt icon container in the header. Lists all expense categories (system and custom) as sub-Cards showing code in mono font, label, and a 'System' badge for built-in categories. Custom categories have a red Trash2 delete button. Below the list, a 2-column create form with code TextInput (auto-uppercased) and display name TextInput, plus an Add Category button with Plus icon and nvi-press. EmptyState with Receipt icon when empty.

elements.name: Support Access Requests section (HeadsetIcon)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with HeadsetIcon header. Lists pending and resolved support requests as sub-Cards with reason, status, scope, duration. Pending requests show Approve (Check) and Reject (X) buttons with nvi-press. EmptyState with HeadsetIcon when empty.

elements.name: Changelog section (History)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with History icon header. Lists recent settings changes with action and timestamp. EmptyState with History icon when empty.

elements.name: Danger Zone section (AlertTriangle)
elements.description: Collapsible Card with red left border and red AlertTriangle icon container. Contains 3-column TextInputs with labels for business ID, password, and DELETE confirmation. Delete button with Trash2 icon in red. All inputs use red border styling to visually warn.

elements.name: Banner messages
elements.description: All page-level feedback uses the Banner notification component (replaces StatusBanner). Supports success/error/warning/info severities with dismiss button.

elements.name: Section update timestamps
elements.description: Each settings section header shows a timestamp badge with the last-updated date.

elements.name: Reset to Defaults buttons
elements.description: Approval, Stock, and POS sections show a RotateCcw icon button in the header when in edit mode. Resets that section's settings to defaults.

elements.name: Unsaved changes warning
elements.description: An amber badge with nvi-status-fade appears in the PageHeader actions area when draft settings differ from saved. Browser also warns on navigation.

elements.name: Unit Help Panel
elements.description: An expandable help panel in the Units section explaining how units of measurement work, with examples.

before_you_start.text: Requires 'settings.write' permission to edit or save any settings.
Requires 'subscription.request' permission to submit subscription change requests.
Requires 'business.delete' permission to access the delete business panel.
The browser will warn you if you navigate away with unsaved settings changes.
Setting up approval thresholds requires understanding your operational workflows — ask your accountant or operations manager before enabling approvals.
Stock valuation method changes affect how the system calculates cost of goods — consult your accountant before changing this.

steps: Click 'Edit' to enter settings edit mode.
In Approval Defaults, toggle 'Purchase approval' on.
Set the threshold amount — purchases above this value will require approval.
Click 'Save Settings'.
Click 'Edit' to enter settings edit mode.
In POS Policies, update the 'Receipt footer' field.
Click 'Save Settings'.

common_tasks.task: Add a custom unit of measure
steps: In the Units section, enter the label (e.g. 'Kilogram'), code (e.g. 'KG'), and select the unit type.
Click 'Create Unit'.
In the Expense Categories section, enter a category code (e.g. 'MARKETING') and a display name (e.g. 'Marketing & Advertising').
Click 'Add Category'. The new category will appear in the expense creation form.
In the Subscription Requests section, select 'Upgrade' as the type.
Select the target tier.
Optionally enter a reason.
Click 'Send Request'. The platform team will review and respond.
Scroll to the Support Access Requests panel.
Review the request details (reason, scope, duration).
Click 'Approve' or 'Reject'.

warnings: Deleting the business is irreversible — all data including sales history, stock records, and user accounts will be permanently erased.
Changing the stock valuation method (FIFO/LIFO/AVERAGE) affects cost calculations for all future stock movements — this should only be changed in consultation with your accountant.
Enabling BLOCK expiry policy will prevent selling expired batches — ensure your team is aware before activating this.
Unsaved settings changes will be lost if you navigate away without clicking 'Save Settings' (the browser will warn you first).
Offline limits (max hours, max sales, max value) cap what POS devices can do while disconnected — set them too low and cashiers may be blocked during a connectivity outage.

common_errors.likely_cause: WhatsApp notifications cannot be activated because the user account has no phone number registered. WhatsApp requires a valid mobile number to send messages.
fix_steps: Go to Settings → Team Members (User Management).
Find the user who needs to receive WhatsApp messages.
