Enter their mobile phone number in the Phone field.
Click 'Save', then return here to activate WhatsApp notifications.

entries.title: My Profile
entries.overview: Your account at a glance. The profile page opens with a hero card showing your avatar, name, email, status, and role badges, followed by four KPI cards (Roles, Permissions, Status, Membership). Below that, a Personal Information card and Membership card sit side by side, a collapsible Permission Dashboard groups permissions by module with progress bars and risk indicators, a Request Access form lets you ask for missing permissions, and a Login History timeline shows your recent sessions.

elements.name: Profile hero card
elements.description: Top-of-page card with a large avatar (XL size, deterministic colour), your full name, email with Mail icon, phone with Phone icon, account status badge, and gold-outlined role pills showing each assigned role name with a Shield icon.

elements.name: Edit button (hero card)
elements.description: Opens the Edit Profile panel where you can update your name and phone number. Email is shown read-only. Uses Pencil icon and rounded-xl styling with nvi-press animation.

elements.name: KPI — Roles (Shield icon)
elements.description: Card with a Shield icon container showing the number of roles currently assigned to you. Each role grants a bundle of permissions. If this shows 0, you likely have very limited access — contact your administrator.

elements.name: KPI — Permissions (Key icon)
elements.description: Card with a Key icon container showing the total number of individual permissions you hold across all your roles. If a feature is blocked and this count seems low, you may be missing a required permission.

elements.name: KPI — Status (UserCheck icon)
elements.description: Card with a UserCheck icon container showing your account status as a coloured StatusBadge. ACTIVE means normal access; SUSPENDED means your account has been temporarily disabled by an administrator.

elements.name: KPI — Membership (Calendar icon)
elements.description: Card with a Calendar icon container showing your membership status as a coloured StatusBadge. ACTIVE means you are a current team member; PENDING means your invitation has not yet been fully accepted.

elements.name: Edit Profile panel
elements.description: Slides in with nvi-slide-in-bottom animation when Edit is clicked. Contains labelled TextInput fields for Name and Phone, a read-only Email field with a hint, and Save (Check icon) / Cancel (X icon) buttons with nvi-press animation.

elements.name: Personal Information card (User icon)
elements.description: Left column card with a User icon header. Shows Name, Email, and Phone as icon-led rows inside bordered containers, each with an uppercase label and the current value.

elements.name: Membership card (Building2 icon)
elements.description: Right column card with a Building2 icon header. Shows Member Since (with relative days count), Membership Status as a StatusBadge, Last Login timestamp, and Account Created date — each as icon-led bordered rows.

elements.name: Role assignments card (Shield icon)
elements.description: Card listing each assigned role as a bordered tile with a Shield icon, role name, approval tier StatusBadge (System Owner / Management / Staff), and branch scope. Uses nvi-stagger animation on the grid.

elements.name: Permission Dashboard (Key icon)
elements.description: Card with a Key icon header and subtitle. Permissions are grouped by module into collapsible sub-cards. Each module card shows the module name, a progress bar (X of Y granted), and a chevron toggle. Expanding a module reveals permission chips with coloured risk icons: red AlertTriangle for high, amber AlertCircle for medium, green Shield for low.

elements.name: Request Access card (Lock icon)
elements.description: Card with a Lock icon header. Contains a SmartSelect for choosing a permission you do not yet hold, a TextInput for an optional reason, and a Send button with nvi-press animation. If you already have all permissions, the selector shows a confirmation message.

elements.name: Login History card (Clock icon)
elements.description: Card with a Clock icon header. Shows the last 5 login timestamps as a mini timeline, each entry in a bordered row with a Clock icon container, the formatted date, and IP/device metadata when available.

elements.name: Change Password link (Lock icon)
elements.description: In the PageHeader actions area. Navigates to the Change Password page. Displayed as an outlined button with a Lock icon and rounded-xl styling.

elements.name: Display size selector (Type icon)
elements.description: Card with a blue Type icon header. Lets you choose from 4 font scale options: Small (0.9x), Default (1x), Large (1.1x), and Extra Large (1.25x). Each option is shown as a button with a letter 'A' at the corresponding size. Clicking an option immediately changes the text size across the entire system by adjusting the root font-size. Includes a live preview section showing how body text, labels, and metadata will appear at the selected size. Your preference is saved in your browser and applied automatically on future visits.

elements.name: Notification Preferences section
elements.description: Embedded NotificationPreferences component showing your notification channel toggles. Appears between the Request Access card and Login History card.

before_you_start.text: All users can view their own profile — no special permission is required.
Access requests require approval from a user with the approvals.write permission. If no one in your business has this permission, requests cannot be processed — contact support.

common_tasks.task: Edit your name or phone number
steps: Click the Edit button in the profile hero card at the top of the page.
Update your Name or Phone in the text fields that appear.
Click Save to persist your changes, or Cancel to discard.
Scroll to the Permission Dashboard section.
Click any module card to expand it and see individual permission chips with risk indicators.
If a specific permission is missing, it will not appear — use the Request Access form below.
Scroll to the Request Access section.
Select the permission you need from the selector.
Enter a clear, specific reason explaining your use case.
Click the Send button. An administrator will be notified and will approve or reject your request.
Scroll to the Display size card on this page, or click the 'A' icon in the top bar for a quick toggle.
Click one of the 4 size options: Small, Default, Large, or Extra Large.
The change takes effect immediately across the entire system. Your preference is saved in your browser.

warnings: Email cannot be changed from this page. Contact your administrator to update your email address.
Access requests go to administrators with the approvals.write permission. If your request sits unanswered, check with your administrator or contact support if no one has that permission.

entries.title: Roles
entries.overview: Security command center with purple master-detail layout: selectable role cards with purple highlight, and a permission matrix with per-module colored borders (blue catalog, cyan stock, emerald sales, amber purchases, purple transfers, pink settings). Risk badges use distinct colors (red HIGH, amber MEDIUM, emerald LOW). Module presets as colored pills (emerald Read, amber Standard, red Full).

elements.name: KPI — Visible Roles (Shield icon, gold)
elements.description: Card with a gold Shield icon container showing the count of roles in the current filtered view. Without any filters applied, this is the total number of roles in the business.

elements.name: KPI — Permissions (Key icon, blue)
elements.description: Card with a blue Key icon container showing the total number of individual permission codes available in the system. This is the full catalogue from which any role's permission set is drawn.

elements.name: KPI — Selected Role (UserCog icon, emerald)
elements.description: Card with an emerald UserCog icon container displaying the name of the role currently open in the permission editor. Shows a dash when no role is being edited.

elements.name: KPI — Assigned Permissions (CheckSquare icon, amber)
elements.description: Card with an amber CheckSquare icon container showing the count of permissions currently checked for the selected role. Updates live as you toggle checkboxes.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filters the role list by name as you type (350 ms debounce). Useful in businesses with many custom roles.

elements.name: Scope filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to system roles (built-in, cannot be deleted) or custom roles (created by your business). System roles can have their permissions edited but cannot be renamed or removed.

elements.name: Permission count filter
elements.description: Filter to roles that have at least one permission assigned, or to roles with no permissions at all. Roles with no permissions assigned are effectively inactive — users holding them can log in but cannot do anything.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all search and filter inputs and reloads the full role list.

elements.name: Role cards (left panel)
elements.description: Each role appears as a selectable card in the left panel with: Shield icon (or Lock for System Owner), role name, system badge if applicable, tier badge (grey/amber/emerald/blue with Shield icon), user count with Users icon, and a Clone button (Copy icon). Clicking a role opens its permission matrix in the right panel. The selected role gets a gold ring highlight. Cards have nvi-card-hover animation.

elements.name: Create role form (left panel bottom)
elements.description: Card at the bottom of the left panel with a Plus icon header, TextInput for role name, SmartSelect for approval tier, and a gold Create button with Plus icon. Creates the role with zero permissions. Requires 'roles.create' permission.

elements.name: Clone button (per role card, Copy icon)
elements.description: Creates a duplicate of the role with all its permissions copied via a confirmation dialog (notify.confirm). The new role is named '[Role Name] (Copy)'. Requires 'roles.create' permission.

elements.name: Permission matrix header (right panel)
elements.description: Shows the selected role name with Shield icon, tier badge, permission count text (X of Y), ProgressBar showing granted vs total with percentage, and a risk breakdown showing high/medium/low counts with colored icons (AlertTriangle red, AlertCircle amber, ShieldCheck green). Close button (X icon) deselects the role.

elements.name: Approval authority section
elements.description: Card with Shield icon header showing a SmartSelect for tier level and Save button. Only shown for non-system roles. Sets the hierarchy level: None (tier 0), Staff (tier 1), Management (tier 2). The System Owner has tier 3. This level determines who can assign roles, approve requests, deactivate users, and manage permissions for lower-level roles.

elements.name: Permission search (Search icon) + Module filter
elements.description: TextInput with Search icon prefix for filtering permissions by code, title, or description. SmartSelect for narrowing to one module. Both inside a Card in the right panel.

elements.name: Module preset pills row
elements.description: Horizontal row of pill-style preset toggles, one group per module. Each group shows the module icon + name, then three colored preset buttons: emerald Read, amber Standard, red Full. Clicking a preset sets all permissions in that module to the corresponding level. Buttons have nvi-press animation.

elements.name: Permission group cards (one per module)
elements.description: Each module is a collapsible Card with per-module colored left border and: module icon (Package for catalog, Layers for stock, ShoppingCart for sales, Truck for purchases, etc.), module name, granted/total count, ProgressBar, and expand/collapse chevron. Clicking expands to show individual permission rows. Groups have nvi-expand animation.

elements.name: Permission rows (within module group)
elements.description: Each permission is a labeled checkbox row with: checkbox (gold accent when checked, gold border highlight), permission title, risk badge (red HIGH with AlertTriangle, amber MEDIUM with AlertCircle, emerald LOW with ShieldCheck icon), description in muted text, and raw permission code. Rows have nvi-stagger entrance animation.

elements.name: Permission guide panel
elements.description: Card with Key icon header explaining Read (green ShieldCheck), Standard (amber AlertCircle), and Full (red AlertTriangle) preset levels and their risk associations.

elements.name: Save Permissions button
elements.description: Gold button with CheckSquare icon and nvi-press animation. Saves the current checkbox state as the complete permission set. This replaces the role's entire permission set — any permissions not checked will be removed. Changes take effect immediately. Requires 'roles.update' permission.

elements.name: Empty state (no role selected)
elements.description: When no role is selected, the right panel shows a centered message with a floating ShieldQuestion icon (nvi-float animation): 'Select a role to view permissions'.

elements.name: Empty state (no roles)
elements.description: When the role list is empty, shows a floating ShieldQuestion icon with 'No roles yet' and a hint to create one.

elements.name: System Owner locked state
elements.description: The System Owner role card shows a Lock icon instead of Shield and is visually dimmed. It cannot be clicked to edit or cloned. Its permissions are fixed by the system.

elements.name: Pagination controls
elements.description: Below the role list in the left panel. Page navigation with page size selector.

before_you_start.text: Requires 'roles.create' permission to create new roles.
Requires 'roles.update' to edit a role's permissions.
The System Owner role is permanently locked — it cannot be edited or deleted.
Permission changes take effect immediately — existing sessions for users with this role will reflect changes on their next server request.

The role hierarchy level (approval authority) determines who can manage whom. Set this carefully when creating custom roles — a role with the wrong level may have too much or too little authority over other users.

steps: Enter a descriptive role name in the create form.
Click 'Create'. The role is created with no permissions.
Click 'Edit Permissions' on the new role to configure its access.
Set the role hierarchy level (approval authority) — choose None for basic staff, Staff level for supervisors, or Management level for senior managers.
The system has four levels: None (0), Staff (1), Management (2), and System Owner (3).
A user's level is the highest level among all their assigned roles.
Users can only approve requests, assign roles, deactivate users, and modify permissions for roles and users at a LOWER level than their own.
Example: A Staff-level manager can approve requests from None-level cashiers, but cannot approve requests from other Staff-level or Management-level users.
Only the System Owner can modify approval policies and approve support access requests.
You cannot create a role with a level equal to or higher than your own.

common_tasks.task: Configure a cashier role with POS access and read-only catalog
steps: Create a role named 'Cashier' (or open an existing one).
Click 'Edit Permissions'.
Use the Module filter to select 'catalog', then click the 'Read' preset.
Switch the Module filter to 'sales', then click the 'Standard' preset.
Review the checked permissions, then click 'Save Permissions'.

common_tasks.task: Audit which permissions a role has
steps: Click 'Edit Permissions' on the role.
Scroll through the list or use the Permission search to inspect individual permissions.
The KPI — Assigned Permissions shows the total count.
Close the editor without saving if you made no intentional changes.

warnings: Saving permissions replaces the role's entire permission set — permissions that are unchecked will be removed immediately.
The Full preset includes high-risk permissions (delete, anonymise, force-close shifts) — review risk badges before applying.
The System Owner role cannot be modified. Changes to all other roles, including system presets, take effect immediately.
Setting a custom role to Management level gives users with that role authority to approve requests, assign roles, and deactivate users at Staff and None levels. Only do this for trusted senior staff.
A user with multiple roles inherits the highest hierarchy level among all their roles. Assigning a Management-level role to a cashier would give them management authority across the system.

entries.title: Team Members
entries.overview: Team member profiles with gradient avatar containers (blue-to-purple), purple role badge pills, amber invite zone with left border accent, and per-action colored buttons (blue edit, amber resend, red archive). Notification preferences filtered by user role (RBAC), and branch assignment available on invite. Status dots with glow shadows.

elements.name: KPI — Visible Users (Users icon, gold)
elements.description: Count of users shown in the current list, respecting any active filters. Displayed with a gold Users icon container. The full user count (no filters) tells you the total team size.

elements.name: KPI — Active (UserCheck icon, emerald)
elements.description: Count of users with ACTIVE status — those who can currently log in. Displayed with an emerald UserCheck icon container.

elements.name: KPI — Pending Invites (Send icon, amber)
elements.description: Count of users who have been invited but have not yet completed sign-up (mustResetPassword flag). Displayed with an amber Send icon container.

elements.name: KPI — Roles Assigned (Shield icon, blue)
elements.description: Total number of role assignments across all visible users. A user with two roles counts as two. Displayed with a blue Shield icon container.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filters the user list by name or email address as you type (350 ms debounce). Wrapped in a Card container.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to users with a specific account status: ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, DEACTIVATED, or PENDING.

elements.name: Role filter
elements.description: Shows only users who have a specific role assigned. Useful for auditing who holds a high-privilege role.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (Invite User form)
elements.description: The invite form is hidden behind an amber-themed 'Invite User' collapsible section with an amber left border accent. Contains labeled TextInput fields for email, name (optional), phone (optional), role selector, and branch access checkboxes (with Building2 icons). The form closes after a successful invitation. Send button has Send icon with nvi-press animation.

elements.name: Branch access checkboxes (invite form)
elements.description: When branches exist, checkboxes appear below the invite fields allowing you to restrict the new user to specific branches. Leave all unchecked to grant access to all branches. Each checkbox shows a Building2 icon and branch name.

elements.name: Team member profile card (card view)
elements.description: Each user is displayed as a profile card with: gradient AvatarInitials container with status dot overlay (green=active, grey=inactive, red=suspended), bold name with online indicator, StatusBadge, contact row with Mail and Phone icons, purple role pills with Shield icons and tier indicators, notification preferences indicator (Bell icon with active event count or 'Not configured'), and Activity button with Clock icon. Cards have nvi-card-hover animation.

elements.name: Edit mode (card view)
elements.description: Clicking the Pencil icon on a user card (or Pencil in table — auto-switches to card view) opens inline edit mode with a gold ring highlight. Contains labeled TextInput fields for name, email, phone (with country code hint), status SmartSelect, notification preferences section (Bell icon header, WhatsApp locale selector, RBAC-filtered event checkboxes with business channel greying), and role assignments section (Shield icon header, add role with SmartSelect + branch + Plus button, existing roles as mini Cards with Trash2 remove). Save (Check icon) and Cancel (X icon) buttons at bottom.

elements.name: Notification preferences section (edit mode)
elements.description: Within edit mode, shows Bell icon header with WhatsApp locale selector and event checkboxes. Events are filtered by the user's role permissions — only events the user's roles grant access to are shown. Events disabled at the business level (in Business Settings) appear greyed out with 'Disabled in business settings' hint. Uses nvi-expand animation.

elements.name: Role Assignments section (edit mode)
elements.description: Within edit mode, shows Shield icon header. Existing assignments displayed as mini Card components with role name, Building2 branch indicator, and Trash2 remove button. Add new assignments with role SmartSelect, optional branch SmartSelect, and Plus icon Assign button. Uses nvi-expand animation.

elements.name: Action buttons (per user card)
elements.description: Color-tinted icon-only action buttons: blue Pencil (edit), amber Send (resend invite — only shown for users with pending password reset), red Archive (deactivate). All have nvi-press animation and hover colour transitions.

elements.name: Table view
elements.description: Card-wrapped table with AvatarInitials (xs) in name column, Mail/Phone icons, gold-outlined role badges, StatusBadge, and icon action buttons. SortableTableHeader on Name, Email, Status columns. Clicking Pencil auto-switches to card view for editing.

elements.name: Online status indicator
elements.description: A green dot next to the user's name in both card and table views, indicating the user is currently online with an active session.

elements.name: Tier badge
elements.description: A coloured badge next to each role: grey for None, amber for Staff, green for Management, blue for System Owner.

elements.name: Activity panel
elements.description: Click the Clock icon Activity button on a user card to expand a panel showing sales count, last action performed, and when it was performed. Uses nvi-expand animation.

elements.name: Empty state (floating Users icon)
elements.description: When no users exist, a centred empty state with a floating Users icon (nvi-float animation) and a prompt to invite the first team member with a Send Invite CTA button.

elements.name: Pagination
elements.description: PaginationControls with page navigation and page size selector below the user list.

before_you_start.text: Requires 'users.create' to invite new users.
Requires 'users.update' to edit profiles or manage role assignments.
Requires 'users.deactivate' to deactivate accounts.
Roles must be created first on the Roles page before they can be assigned to users.
Branch-scoped role assignments limit what the user can access to only that branch's data.

steps: Open the 'Invite User' section.
Enter the person's email address, optionally their name and phone.
Select their initial role.
Optionally check branch access boxes to restrict to specific branches.
Click the Send button. They will receive an email with a sign-up link valid for 48 hours.

common_tasks.task: Restrict a user's access to a single branch
steps: Click the Pencil icon on the user's card to enter edit mode.
In the Role Assignments section, remove any existing global role assignments (those without a branch) using the Trash2 icon.
Select the appropriate role, select the branch, and click the Plus icon to assign.

common_tasks.task: Assign an additional role to an existing user
steps: Click the Pencil icon on the user's card.
In the Role Assignments section, select the role and optionally a branch.
Click the Plus icon to assign.
Click the Pencil icon on the user's card.
In the Notification Preferences section, set the WhatsApp locale if needed.
Toggle event checkboxes to enable or disable specific event types. Greyed-out events are disabled at the business level.
Click Save (Check icon).

common_tasks.task: Deactivate a user who has left
steps: Find the user in the list.
Click the Archive icon. Confirm in the dialog.
Their access is revoked immediately. Historical records (sales, audit logs) are preserved.

warnings: Deactivating a user is immediate — they lose login access the moment you confirm. Reactivate via Edit → Status = ACTIVE.
Removing a user's only role assignment leaves them with no permissions — they will see a restricted view on next login.
Invitation links expire after 48 hours. If a user did not receive or act on the invite, send a new one.
Notification event checkboxes only show events the user's roles grant access to. If an event is missing, check their role permissions.
Events greyed out with 'Disabled in business settings' cannot be toggled here — enable the channel in Business Settings first.

common_errors.likely_cause: A user account is missing a phone number, which is required before WhatsApp notifications can be activated in Business Settings. This error appears when you try to enable WhatsApp without a mobile number on the user profile.
fix_steps: Find the user in the Team Members list.
Click the Pencil icon on that user.
Enter their mobile phone number in the Phone field.
Click Save. WhatsApp notifications can now be activated for this user in Business Settings.

entries.title: Shifts
entries.overview: Shift management with emerald glow dots for open shifts and red close form accents. Performance metrics use colored stat boxes (emerald for sales count, blue for revenue, purple for average transaction). Branch shown in blue pills. Cash variance colored by accuracy (emerald exact match, amber close, red mismatch). Open shift form has emerald left border, close form has red.

elements.name: Active Shifts KPI
elements.description: Shows the count of currently open shifts with a Clock icon in an emerald container. Uses animated FlipCounter digits.

elements.name: Today's Sales KPI
elements.description: Displays total revenue from loaded shift performance data with a ShoppingCart icon in a gold container. Shows '---' until performance data is loaded.

elements.name: Avg Transaction KPI
elements.description: Average transaction value across loaded shift performance data with a TrendingUp icon in a blue container. Shows '---' until performance data is loaded.

elements.name: Current Shift Duration KPI
elements.description: Live FlipClock showing elapsed time of the earliest open shift. Shows '---' when no shifts are open. Uses a Clock icon in an emerald container.

elements.name: "Open shift" button (header)
elements.description: A green button in the page header opens a modal for starting a new shift. The modal collects branch, opening cash, and optional notes. Previously this was an inline collapsible-section form — it has been moved into a modal.

elements.name: Branch selector (Open Shift form)
elements.description: Choose which branch you are opening the shift for. Uses SmartSelect with nvi-select-container styling. Only branches your role has shift access to appear here.

elements.name: Opening cash input
elements.description: CurrencyInput with a label above it. Enter the total cash in the till drawer at the start of the shift. This figure is the baseline for calculating the expected closing cash.

elements.name: Notes input (Open Shift)
elements.description: TextInput with label. Optional free-text notes about the shift opening — float discrepancies, handover notes, or special circumstances.

elements.name: Open Shift button
elements.description: Play icon + nvi-press animation. Creates and opens the shift for the selected branch. Shows Spinner during submission. Disabled without shifts.open permission.

elements.name: "Close shift" button (header)
elements.description: A red button in the page header appears only when there are OPEN shifts — it opens a modal for closing a shift. The modal collects the open-shift selector, closing cash, and optional variance reason. If the variance exceeds the configured threshold, the backend returns `approvalRequired` and the modal shows an info toast; the shift stays OPEN until an approver acts. Previously this was an inline collapsible-section form — moved into a modal.

elements.name: Shift selector (Close Shift form)
elements.description: Lists only shifts that are currently open. Displays branch name and opened timestamp for each option.

elements.name: Closing cash input
elements.description: CurrencyInput with a label above it. Enter the physical cash counted in the till at shift end. The system calculates variance automatically.

elements.name: Variance reason input
elements.description: TextInput with label. Explain any difference between counted and expected cash. Required when variance exceeds the configured threshold.

elements.name: Close Shift button
elements.description: Square icon + nvi-press animation. Closes the selected shift and records the variance. If variance exceeds the approval threshold, an approval request is generated. Disabled without shifts.close permission.

elements.name: Shift session card
elements.description: Each shift renders as a session card with nvi-card-hover animation. Contains: large coloured status dot (green=open, grey=closed), Building2 icon + branch name header, reference number as muted badge with Hash icon, duration section (FlipClock for open shifts, ProgressBar for closed), cash summary row with opening cash ArrowRight closing cash, variance colour-coded (green/red/muted) with AlertTriangle icon, relative timestamps, and expandable performance section.

elements.name: Status dot indicator
elements.description: Large coloured dot on each shift card and table row. Green with glow shadow for OPEN shifts, muted grey for CLOSED.

elements.name: Duration ProgressBar
elements.description: On closed shift cards and table rows, shows hours worked out of 12h max. Green under 8h, amber 8-10h, red over 10h.

elements.name: Cash summary row
elements.description: Shows opening cash with DollarSign icon, ArrowRight to closing cash. Variance amount appears at the end, colour-coded with AlertTriangle icon when non-zero.

elements.name: Performance section (expandable)
elements.description: On closed shift cards, click the TrendingUp Performance button to expand three mini stat boxes: sale count, total revenue (yellow), and average transaction (blue). Uses nvi-expand animation. Data loaded on-demand via API.

elements.name: Table view
elements.description: Wrapped in Card component. Columns: status dot, Branch, Opened At (relative + absolute), Closed At, Duration with compact ProgressBar, Opening Cash, Closing Cash, Variance (colour-coded with AlertTriangle), Status badge, and a TrendingUp icon button for performance.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting in table view. Available on Branch, Opened At, Closed At, Opening Cash, Closing Cash, Variance, Status.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills: green for OPEN shifts, grey for CLOSED.

elements.name: Reference number badge
elements.description: Each shift has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. SHF-001). Shown as a muted badge with Hash icon under the branch name in both card and table views.

elements.name: Empty state
elements.description: When no shifts exist, shows a floating Clock icon with nvi-float animation, 'No shifts recorded yet' title, and a description prompting to open the first shift.

elements.name: ViewToggle (cards/table)
elements.description: Toggle between card and table views in the page header. Cards show session-style shift cards, table shows a detailed sortable grid.

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Cursor-based pagination with page size selector at the bottom of the list.

before_you_start.text: You need the shifts.open permission to open a shift and shifts.close to close one. These are separate permissions — a cashier may have open but not close.
A branch must exist before a shift can be opened for it. Create branches on the Branch Settings page.
Only one shift can be open per branch at a time. If a previous shift was not closed, you must close it before opening a new one.

common_tasks.task: Open a shift at the start of the day
steps: Expand the Open Shift section.
Select your branch from the Branch selector.
Count the opening cash in the till and enter the amount in Opening cash.
Optionally add handover notes.
Click the Open Shift button (Play icon). POS is now enabled at this branch.

common_tasks.task: Close a shift at end of day
steps: Expand the Close Shift section.
Count the physical cash remaining in the till.
Select the open shift from the Shift selector.
Enter the counted amount in Closing cash. If it differs from the expected amount, enter a reason in Variance reason.
Click the Close Shift button (Square icon). The shift is closed and any approval workflow triggers automatically.
Find the closed shift in card or table view.
In card view, click the Performance button at the bottom of the card. In table view, click the TrendingUp icon.
Review the sale count, revenue total, and average transaction value.

warnings: Only one shift can be open per branch at a time. If a previous shift was never closed, the Open Shift button is blocked for that branch — close the old shift first.
If shift tracking is enabled in Business Settings, POS is blocked for a branch until a shift is open. Staff will see an error at checkout until someone opens the shift.

Large cash variances at close may trigger an approval request. The POS at that branch remains usable while the approval is pending — but the variance is flagged for manager review in the Approvals page.

entries.title: Create Business Account
entries.overview: Use this page to register your business on the system. Fill in your business name, your own name, your email, a password, and choose a subscription plan. After submitting, you will be sent a verification email — you must verify it before you can log in.

before_you_start.text: You need a valid email address that you can access — a verification link will be sent there.
If you were invited by an existing business, do not use this page. Use the Accept Invite page instead.

elements.name: Business Name field
elements.description: The official name of your business. This is what will appear across the system and on receipts.

elements.name: Owner Name field
elements.description: Your full name as the business owner. This becomes your display name in the system.

elements.name: Email field
elements.description: Your email address. This is your login username and where verification and notification emails will be sent.

elements.name: Password field
elements.description: Choose a strong password. Must meet the minimum requirements shown below the field (length, complexity).

elements.name: Show / Hide Password button
elements.description: Toggles whether your password is visible or hidden as you type.

elements.name: Subscription Plan dropdown
elements.description: Choose the plan that fits your business: Starter (basic features), Business (full features), or Enterprise (advanced multi-branch). You can change this later.
elements.notes: This is a trial — you will not be charged immediately.

elements.name: Create Account button
elements.description: Submits your details and creates the business account. You will be redirected to the email verification page.

elements.name: KPI preview cards
elements.description: The three cards at the top (Business Name, Owner, Status) update live as you fill in the form to confirm what you have set.

elements.name: Trial hint text
elements.description: Shows which subscription tier the trial will start with and what it includes.

elements.name: Password show/hide toggle
elements.description: Toggle password visibility in the password field to verify what you typed.

elements.name: Real-time KPI status
elements.description: KPI cards update in real-time as you fill out the form, showing status like 'set', 'pending', 'creating', 'ready'.

steps: Fill in your Business Name, Owner Name, and Email.
Set a password that meets the requirements.
Choose a subscription plan from the dropdown.
Click Create Account.
Check your email for the verification link and follow it.

warnings: You cannot log in until you verify your email address. Check your inbox (and spam folder) after registering.

Each email address can be registered under multiple businesses, but each business needs its own registration. Users with an existing account can create additional businesses — the system links the existing account instead of requiring a new one.
Your subscription plan choice here is for the free trial only — you can change it later in Settings.

related_pages.reason: After registering, you are redirected here to verify your email.
After verification, log in here.

entries.title: Stock On Hand
entries.overview: Monitor current inventory levels across branches using redesigned stock health cards. Each card displays a three-stat layout (on-hand, in-transit, total) in color-tinted containers, a progress bar for stock health, stock value, and color-coded days remaining (red critical, amber warning, green healthy). KPI cards feature icons with tinted backgrounds and FlipCounter animations. Reorder suggestions appear below filters with a lightbulb icon header. Define reorder points in a collapsible form with labeled TextInput fields.

elements.name: KPI – Snapshots
elements.description: Icon-card with Database icon in blue-tinted container. Shows total branch-variant pairs with stock data matching the current filters. FlipCounter animates the number on load.

elements.name: KPI – Reorder Points
elements.description: Icon-card with AlertCircle icon in amber-tinted container. Shows the number of active reorder-point rules defined for the current branch scope. FlipCounter animates the count.

elements.name: KPI – Suggestions
elements.description: Icon-card with Lightbulb icon in emerald-tinted container. Shows the number of variants currently below their minimum reorder threshold that need restocking.

elements.name: KPI – Active Branch
elements.description: Icon-card with Building2 icon in gold-tinted container. Shows the branch currently selected in the top-bar branch selector. Stock snapshots are scoped to this branch by default.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Find stock entries by typing part of the variant name or parent product name. Useful for a quick single-item stock check without setting multiple filters.

elements.name: Branch Filter
elements.description: Restricts snapshot results to a single branch. Always set this when checking stock for a specific location — leaving it unset combines all branches, which can be misleading for restocking decisions.

elements.name: Variant Filter
elements.description: Narrows the list to one specific variant. Combine with Branch Filter to see exactly how much of one SKU is on hand at one location.

elements.name: Status Filter
elements.description: Filter by the variant's catalogue status: ACTIVE (currently sold), INACTIVE (suspended), or ARCHIVED (discontinued). In day-to-day stock checks, leave this set to ACTIVE so discontinued items do not clutter the view.

elements.name: Category Filter
elements.description: Restricts results to variants belonging to a specific product category. Useful for doing a category-level stock review — for example, checking all 'Dairy' variants across branches in one view.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between stock health card grid and table view for the snapshots list.

elements.name: Stock health indicator
elements.description: Redesigned card layout showing a three-stat row with on-hand (blue tint), in-transit (amber tint), and total (emerald tint) quantities in rounded containers. Low-stock cards show a red left border accent. Each card includes product image with zoom, stock value, color-coded days remaining, and a progress bar for visual stock health.

elements.name: Days remaining warning
elements.description: Color-coded days-remaining indicator on each stock card and table row. Red with AlertTriangle icon and bounce animation for critical (7 days or fewer), amber for warning (8-30 days), muted for healthy (over 30 days). Helps prioritise restocking at a glance.

elements.name: Snapshots List
elements.description: Shows each branch + variant combination with on-hand quantity, in-transit quantity, and total. Card view uses stock health cards; table view uses sortable columns wrapped in Card with image thumbnails.

elements.name: Pagination Controls
elements.description: Navigate pages of snapshot records and adjust page size.

elements.name: Reorder Points – Branch Selector
elements.description: Labeled branch selector in the reorder form Card. Reorder points are branch-specific — the same variant can have different minimum thresholds at different locations.

elements.name: Reorder Points – Variant Selector
elements.description: Labeled variant selector in the reorder form Card. Once saved, the system compares this variant's on-hand quantity at the chosen branch against the Min Quantity threshold.

elements.name: Min Quantity Input
elements.description: TextInput with label for the stock level threshold. When on-hand quantity drops below this number, the variant appears in Reorder Suggestions.

elements.name: Reorder Quantity Input
elements.description: TextInput with label for the suggested order quantity. This value appears in Reorder Suggestions as a guide — it does not automatically create a purchase order.

elements.name: Save Reorder Point Button
elements.description: Saves the reorder rule with Save icon. Uses rounded-xl styling and nvi-press animation. Requires stock.write.

elements.name: Reorder Points Table
elements.description: Clean mini-table inside a Card (glow disabled) listing all active reorder rules with their branch, variant, minimum, and reorder quantities.

elements.name: Reorder Suggestions Panel
elements.description: Card panel with Lightbulb icon header showing variants below their minimum stock level in a clean table grid. Empty state shows muted 'no suggestions' text.

elements.name: FlipCounter KPI animation
elements.description: KPI icon-cards use animated FlipCounter numbers that roll up when data loads. Each KPI has a distinct icon and color-tinted container for visual identity.

elements.name: Stock value display
elements.description: Calculated as quantity times default cost. Shown on each stock health card with currency formatting and in the table view column.

elements.name: ProgressBar stock level
elements.description: Color-coded visual bar on each stock health card. Red for 5 or fewer units, amber for 6-20, green for above 20. Provides at-a-glance stock health assessment.

elements.name: Low stock colour coding
elements.description: Stock cards with 5 or fewer units show a red left border accent. Table rows use red/amber tinted backgrounds. AlertTriangle icon appears inline for critically low quantities.

elements.name: Bulk Stock Count button
elements.description: A button in the page header with rounded-xl styling that navigates to the Stock Count Wizard for counting multiple variants at once.

elements.name: Image thumbnails
elements.description: Variant images shown in both card and table views with rounded-lg corners and nvi-img-zoom hover effect. Cards show a larger 48px thumbnail; table shows a compact 32px version.

elements.name: Adjust button (per card/row)
elements.description: A quick action button with Pencil icon on each stock card and table row. Uses nvi-press animation and rounded-xl styling.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Variant, Branch, Quantity, Stock Value, Days Remaining, and Status.

elements.name: In-transit quantity
elements.description: Shows the quantity of stock currently in transit (via transfers) for each variant. Displayed in its own amber-tinted stat box in card view and as a separate column in table view.

before_you_start.text: Variants and products must exist before any stock snapshots will appear.
stock.read is required to view snapshots and reorder data; stock.write is required to save reorder points.
Set the correct branch in the top bar or use the branch filter — stock data is branch-specific.

common_tasks.task: Check how much stock is on hand for a variant
steps: Use the Variant Filter to select the variant.
Use the Branch Filter to select the branch.
Read the On Hand quantity from the snapshot card or table row.

common_tasks.task: Set a reorder point to trigger restocking alerts
steps: In the Reorder Points section, select a branch and a variant.
Enter the Min Quantity (the threshold) and Reorder Quantity (how much to order).
Click Save Reorder Point.
Confirm the rule appears in the Reorder Points table below.

warnings: Reorder suggestions are read-only indicators — they do not automatically create purchase orders.
Saving a reorder point with the wrong branch or variant will silently set incorrect thresholds.
In-transit quantity includes stock from approved purchase orders that have not yet been received.
Stock counts and adjustments are done on separate pages — quantities on this page are read-only.

related_pages.reason: Variants must exist for stock snapshots to appear.
View the full history of stock changes for any variant.
Correct stock quantities when they don't match physical inventory.
Reconcile physical inventory with system quantities.

entries.title: Stock Adjustments
entries.overview: Make controlled corrections to stock quantities with a redesigned card-first interface. Each adjustment card highlights the quantity change as the hero element — green for additions, red for reductions — alongside the variant name, reason badge with contextual icon, branch tag, and relative timestamp. Four KPI cards at the top summarise total adjustments, positive count, negative count, and net change. The page uses the standard ListPage layout with PageHeader, Banner notifications, and EmptyState. Works in offline mode — adjustments are queued locally via IndexedDB and synced when reconnected.

elements.name: KPI – Total Adjustments
elements.description: Total adjustment records from the server. Displayed in a Card with an ArrowUpDown icon container. After submitting an adjustment, this count should increase. If it does not change, check for a Banner error.

elements.name: KPI – Positive Adjustments
elements.description: Count of ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE movements in the current page. Shown with a TrendingUp icon in an emerald icon container.

elements.name: KPI – Negative Adjustments
elements.description: Count of ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE movements in the current page. Shown with a TrendingDown icon in a red icon container.

elements.name: KPI – Net Change
elements.description: Sum of all quantity values in the current page. Shown with a Scale icon in a blue icon container. Turns green when net-positive, red when net-negative.

elements.name: Offline PIN Panel
elements.description: Appears when you are offline and a PIN is configured. Wrapped in a Card with a Lock icon. Enter your PIN and click Unlock to enable offline adjustment submission.

elements.name: Branch Selector (Adjustment form)
elements.description: Required. The branch where the physical stock change happened. Getting this right is critical — an adjustment to the wrong branch will misstate stock at two locations simultaneously.

elements.name: Variant Selector (Adjustment form)
elements.description: Required. The specific product variant (SKU) being adjusted. Uses AsyncSmartSelect with search.

elements.name: Quantity Input
elements.description: Required. Uses TextInput with label. Enter a positive number — the direction is set by the Type selector.

elements.name: Unit Selector
elements.description: The unit of measure for the quantity entered. The selector shows only the variant's base unit and sell unit, with labels indicating which is which (base/sell). If no variant is selected, all units are shown.

elements.name: Type Selector (Positive / Negative)
elements.description: POSITIVE adds stock; NEGATIVE removes stock and requires selecting a loss reason.

elements.name: Batch Selector
elements.description: Optional. Links the adjustment to a specific batch code for expiry or lot tracking. Batch codes can be auto-generated using the Generate button (wand icon), producing codes like BATCH-20260408-001. Manual entry is also supported.

elements.name: Loss Reason Selector
elements.description: Required when Type is NEGATIVE. A hint with Info icon below explains financial impact.

elements.name: Gain Reason Selector
elements.description: Required when Type is POSITIVE. A hint with Info icon below explains financial impact.

elements.name: Financial Impact Hint
elements.description: A text line with Info icon below the reason selector explaining the financial consequence of the selected reason.

elements.name: Reason Notes Input
elements.description: Uses TextInput with label. Optional free text for adjustment context. Stored in the movement record and visible in audit logs.

elements.name: Submit Adjustment Button
elements.description: Posts the adjustment with a Plus icon and nvi-press animation. In offline mode, queues it locally. Uses rounded-xl styling.

elements.name: Submit adjustment / Create batch buttons (page header)
elements.description: Two header action buttons. 'Submit adjustment' (Plus icon, gold) opens the Adjustment modal. 'Create batch' (Layers icon) opens the Batch modal. Both require the stock.write permission.

elements.name: Adjustment modal
elements.description: Centered modal opened from the Submit adjustment header button. Contains branch, variant, quantity, unit, type (POSITIVE/NEGATIVE), batch, loss/gain reason, financial-impact hint, and optional notes. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. After a successful save the list auto-refreshes.

elements.name: Batch modal
elements.description: Centered modal opened from the Create batch header button. Contains branch, variant, batch code (with a wand button to auto-generate), and optional expiry date. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: In the PageHeader actions slot. Switch between card grid and table view for the adjustment history.

elements.name: History Filters
elements.description: ListFilters with branch, type, reason (TextInput), and date range filters. Uses rounded-xl DatePickerInputs.

elements.name: Adjustment Card (card view)
elements.description: Card with nvi-card-hover animation. Hero: quantity change in large bold text — green for positive, red for negative. Reason badge with contextual Lucide icon (Hammer for damage, ShieldAlert for loss/theft, Clock for expiry, Check for correction). Branch tag with Building2 icon. Footer: actor with User icon and relative time. Approved adjustments show the approver's name with a green checkmark.

elements.name: Adjustment Table (table view)
elements.description: Wrapped in Card. Rows have hover highlights. Image column shows Package icon fallback. Quantity is color-coded. Reason column shows contextual Lucide icon. Time column shows relative time with full datetime tooltip.

elements.name: Empty State
elements.description: EmptyState with floating ArrowUpDown icon (nvi-float). Shows when no adjustments match. Includes a Submit Adjustment CTA with Plus icon.

elements.name: Unit conversion hint
elements.description: A dynamic hint below the unit selector showing conversion calculation when a variant has different base and sell units.

before_you_start.text: stock.write is required for all adjustment and batch operations.
Variants and branches must exist before you can make an adjustment.
If working offline and a PIN is required, have your PIN ready before opening this page.

steps: Select the branch and variant.
Enter the quantity.
Set Type to POSITIVE.
Optionally enter a reason note.
Click Submit Adjustment.

common_tasks.task: Record damaged or lost stock (negative adjustment)
steps: Select the branch and variant.
Enter the quantity to remove.
Set Type to NEGATIVE.
Select the Loss Reason (e.g. Damaged).
Click Submit Adjustment.

warnings: Negative adjustments require a loss reason — the form will not submit without one.
In offline mode, adjustments are queued in IndexedDB and only synced when back online. Do not close the browser tab during offline queuing.
If sync is blocked (a flag shown in the app), offline actions cannot be queued until the block is cleared by an admin.
Batch tracking requires both branch and variant to be selected before batches will load.

related_pages.reason: View the resulting on-hand levels after making adjustments.
Adjustments appear as ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE or ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE movement records.

entries.title: Stock Counts
entries.overview: Reconcile physical inventory with system quantities. The page centres on variance — each card and row highlights whether a count found a shortage, surplus, or exact match, making discrepancies impossible to miss. Submit single-item spot-checks here, or open the Count Wizard for multi-item sessions.

elements.name: KPI – Total Counts
elements.description: Total stock-count variance records across all pages. Reflects the full count of STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movements, not just the current page.

elements.name: KPI – Shortage Total
elements.description: Sum of all shortage quantities on the current page. Displayed in red with an AlertTriangle icon. A high number indicates frequent under-counts that may warrant investigation.

elements.name: KPI – Surplus Total
elements.description: Sum of all surplus quantities on the current page. Displayed in green with an ArrowUp icon. Persistent surpluses may indicate unrecorded purchases or returns.

elements.name: KPI – Exact Matches
elements.description: Number of counts on the current page where the counted quantity exactly matched the expected quantity. Displayed with a Check icon. A higher ratio means better inventory accuracy.

elements.name: Open Wizard button
elements.description: Navigates to the Stock Count Wizard for counting multiple variants in a single guided session. Uses a Wand icon. Use the wizard for scheduled stock takes; use this single-item form for quick spot-checks.

elements.name: ViewToggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switches between card view and table view for the count history. Card view shows variance-hero cards; table view wraps data in a Card container with sortable columns.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection — New Count form
elements.description: Expandable form for submitting a single stock count. Closes automatically after successful submission. Contains Branch selector, Variant selector, Counted Quantity (TextInput), Unit selector, Expected Quantity (read-only TextInput), Batch selector, shortage/surplus reason dropdowns (contextual), and a Reason notes field.

elements.name: Branch Selector
elements.description: Required. The branch where the physical count took place. The Expected Quantity shown is specific to this branch — selecting the wrong branch compares your count against the wrong stock level and creates a false variance.

elements.name: Variant Selector (async search)
elements.description: Required. The specific variant you physically counted. Supports type-ahead search. Choose the exact variant (e.g. 'Rice 2kg', not just 'Rice') to ensure the variance is recorded at the right SKU.

elements.name: Counted Quantity (TextInput)
elements.description: Number input with label. Enter the number of units you physically found. The system calculates variance automatically by comparing this against Expected Quantity. Double-check before submitting; counts are irreversible.

elements.name: Unit Selector
elements.description: The unit of measure for your counted quantity. The selector shows only the variant's base unit and sell unit, with labels indicating which is which (base/sell). If no variant is selected, all units are shown.

elements.name: Unit conversion hint
elements.description: A dynamic hint showing the conversion calculation for the selected variant — base unit equivalent of the quantity entered.

elements.name: Expected Quantity (read-only TextInput)
elements.description: System's current on-hand quantity for this variant at this branch. Read-only, styled with muted text. Used to calculate the variance.

elements.name: Batch Selector
elements.description: Optional. Links this count to a specific batch code. Use for expiry-date audits or verifying a specific supplier delivery.

elements.name: Shortage Reason dropdown
elements.description: Appears only when counted < expected. Options: DAMAGED, LOST, STOLEN, EXPIRED, SHRINKAGE, SOLD_OUTSIDE_POS, CORRECTION, OTHER. A contextual hint below explains the financial impact (e.g. recorded as loss vs no financial impact).

elements.name: Surplus Reason dropdown
elements.description: Appears only when counted > expected. Options: UNRECORDED_PURCHASE, FOUND_STOCK, RETURN_NOT_LOGGED, CORRECTION, OTHER. A contextual hint explains the financial impact.

elements.name: Reason Notes (TextInput)
elements.description: Optional free-text explanation (e.g. 'End of day count', 'Post-delivery recount'). Stored in the movement record and visible in audit logs.

elements.name: Submit Count button
elements.description: Posts the count. Uses a ClipboardCheck icon and rounded-xl styling. The system calculates variance and creates a STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movement record. Requires stock.write permission.

elements.name: Variance-hero count card
elements.description: Each card in card view leads with the variance as the hero element — a large coloured label ('+5 surplus' in green, '-3 shortage' in red, '0 match' in muted gold). Below: variant name, stat boxes for quantity and unit, a reason badge (colour-coded), and a footer with branch (MapPin icon) and relative time (Clock icon). A Recount button appears on hover.

elements.name: Recount button
elements.description: Appears on hover on each card (always visible on mobile). Pre-fills the count form with the same variant and branch, opens the form, and switches to card view if in table view. Uses a RotateCcw icon.

elements.name: Table view (inside Card)
elements.description: Sortable table wrapped in a Card component. Columns: Variant, Quantity (with variance icon + colour), Unit, Branch, Reason (as a coloured badge), Date, and Recount action. Variance column uses AlertTriangle (red), ArrowUp (green), or Check (muted) icons.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Variant, Quantity, Branch, Reason, and Date.

elements.name: EmptyState
elements.description: Shown when no count records exist. Displays a floating ClipboardCheck icon, a title, and a 'Submit Count' action button to open the form.

elements.name: Banner (success / error)
elements.description: Replaces the old StatusBanner. Shows success or error messages after form submission with dismiss button.

before_you_start.text: stock.write is required to submit counts.
The Expected Quantity field shows the system's current value — this is what your counted quantity will be compared against.
For large multi-item counting sessions, use the Count Wizard at /stock/counts/wizard instead of this single-item form.

steps: Select the branch and variant.
Note the Expected Quantity shown in the read-only field.
Enter your physical Counted Quantity.
Optionally select a batch and add a reason note.
Click Submit Count and confirm success.

warnings: Stock counts are not available in offline mode — you must be online to submit.
Submitting a count creates an irreversible STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movement. Double-check your counted quantity before submitting.
If the counted and expected quantities match, no net stock change occurs — but the event is still recorded for audit purposes.

related_pages.reason: Check on-hand levels before and after a count.
Count variance events appear as STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE movement records.
Use the wizard for guided multi-item count sessions.

entries.title: Stock Count Wizard
entries.overview: Guided stock count wizard with colored step borders (blue for count entry, emerald for review). Variance indicators use distinct colors: red for shortage, emerald for surplus, muted for exact match. Hero quantity numbers colored by variance. Navigation buttons colored by action (blue next, emerald submit).

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Shows your position across the two steps with colored step borders: blue Counts step and emerald Review step. Add all count lines on the Counts step, then review the full summary before submitting.

elements.name: Branch selector (per line)
elements.description: Choose which branch this count line is for. Each line is branch-specific. If you are counting across multiple branches in one session, add separate lines for each branch — the wizard supports mixed-branch submissions.

elements.name: Variant selector (per line)
elements.description: Select the product variant you physically counted. Once selected, the System quantity field loads the current recorded stock level for that variant at the selected branch so you can compare against your physical count.

elements.name: Counted quantity input
elements.description: Enter the number of units you physically counted for this variant at this branch. This is your actual physical count — enter what you found, not what the system shows. The system calculates the variance automatically.

elements.name: Unit selector (per line)
elements.description: The unit of measure for the counted quantity. Select the same unit the variant is tracked in — usually the base unit (e.g. Case or Piece).

elements.name: Reason input (per line)
elements.description: Explain why you are submitting this count — for example 'Annual stock take', 'Post-delivery count', 'Shrinkage investigation', or 'Spot check'. The reason is stored on the resulting stock adjustment and is visible in the audit log and stock movement history.

elements.name: Batch selector (per line)
elements.description: If batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings, optionally specify which batch you are counting. Leave blank to count all stock of this variant at this branch regardless of batch.

elements.name: System quantity display
elements.description: Shows the current recorded stock level for this variant at this branch for comparison. The difference between this number and your counted quantity is the variance. A positive variance means you found more than recorded; negative means you found fewer.

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds another count line. Add one line per variant you are counting. You can count multiple variants and multiple branches in a single wizard session.

elements.name: Remove Line button
elements.description: Removes that count line from the session. Use this to correct a mistake before advancing to the Review step.

elements.name: Next button
elements.description: Blue-themed button that validates all count lines and advances to the emerald Review step. Any lines with missing required fields will be highlighted for correction.

elements.name: Review step
elements.description: Full summary table with an emerald step border showing each variant, branch, system quantity, your counted quantity, and the calculated variance. Variance rows are color-coded: red for shortage, emerald for surplus, muted for exact match. Review every line carefully — once you submit, all adjustments are applied immediately.

elements.name: Submit button
elements.description: Emerald-themed button that creates a stock adjustment record for every line with a non-zero variance. Each adjustment updates the branch stock level immediately and appears in the stock movement history and audit log. Lines where your count matches the system quantity exactly create no adjustment.

elements.name: Barcode input and lookup
elements.description: Type or scan a barcode to automatically find and add the matching variant to the count lines. Press Enter to submit the lookup.

elements.name: Per-line batch selector
elements.description: Each count line has a batch selector that loads available batches for the selected branch and variant combination.

elements.name: Variance calculation per line
elements.description: Each line shows the expected quantity, counted quantity, and calculated variance. Variance is colour-coded: emerald for surplus, red for shortage, and muted for an exact match.

elements.name: Expected quantity hint
elements.description: A small hint below each count line showing what the system expects the quantity to be. Compare against your physical count.

elements.name: Invalid lines section
elements.description: In the Review step, lines with errors (missing branch, variant, or quantity) are shown separately with error messages explaining what needs fixing.

elements.name: Partial submit
elements.description: If some lines are invalid, you can submit only the valid lines. A note explains that invalid lines will not be submitted. Use 'Submit Valid Only' to proceed.

elements.name: Visual progress bar
elements.description: A progress indicator at the top showing which step you are on (Counts or Review) with visual dots and connecting lines.

before_you_start.text: You need the stock.write permission.
The branches and variants you are counting must already exist in the system.
If batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings, you can optionally specify a batch for each count line.

common_tasks.task: Submit a stock count for multiple products
steps: Click Add Line for each variant you have physically counted.
For each line, select the branch, variant, enter the counted quantity, choose the unit, and enter a reason.
Click Next to advance to the Review step.
Review the variance column for each line — positive means found more than recorded, negative means fewer.
Click Submit. Stock adjustments are applied immediately for all lines with a variance.

common_tasks.task: Submit a count that reveals shrinkage (missing stock)
steps: Add a count line for the affected variant and branch.
Enter the quantity you physically found (which is less than the system shows).
In Reason, note 'Shrinkage investigation' or describe what may have happened.
Click Next, review the negative variance, then Submit. The stock level is reduced by the variance amount.

common_tasks.task: Count stock across multiple branches in one session
steps: Add one line per variant per branch combination — for example, add a line for 'Warehouse' and a separate line for 'Shop Front' for the same variant.
Fill in all details for each line.
Review the summary on the Review step, then Submit to apply all adjustments at once.

warnings: Each line with a variance creates an immediate stock adjustment — this affects stock levels, reports, and valuation right away. Review the Review step carefully before submitting.
Large variances may trigger approval requests depending on thresholds configured in Business Settings. The adjustment is held until the approval is granted.
You cannot undo a submitted count. If a line was submitted with an incorrect quantity, correct it with another stock count or a manual stock adjustment.

entries.title: Stock Movements
entries.overview: Read-only audit log of every stock change. Each movement has a type (PURCHASE_IN, SALE_OUT, ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE, TRANSFER_IN, etc.), a quantity, the branch, variant, and the user who triggered it. Three view modes are available: Table for dense scanning, Cards for detailed per-event reading with direction indicators, and Timeline for a chronological day-grouped vertical timeline with color-coded dots and time labels. Use filters and date ranges to investigate specific changes.

elements.name: KPI – Movement Rows
elements.description: Total number of movement records matching current filters. Shown with an Activity icon in a gold container. Without filters, the 50 most recent records are shown. Add date, branch, or type filters to narrow to the specific period or event you are investigating.

elements.name: KPI – Total In
elements.description: Sum of all inbound stock quantities on the current page (purchases, positive adjustments, transfer-in, returns-in, opening balances). Shown in green with an ArrowDownToLine icon. Use this to compare incoming vs outgoing volume.

elements.name: KPI – Total Out
elements.description: Sum of all outbound stock quantities on the current page (sales, negative adjustments, transfer-out, returns-out). Shown in red with an ArrowUpFromLine icon.

elements.name: KPI – Net Change
elements.description: Total In minus Total Out for the current page. Positive values are green; negative values are red. A quick health indicator — persistent negative values may signal shrinkage or stock-out trends.

elements.name: Search Input
elements.description: Filter movements by typing part of the variant's name. Results update as you type. Combine with Branch and Type filters to quickly isolate the movements you are investigating without scrolling through the full history.

elements.name: Branch Filter
elements.description: Restricts results to stock movements at a specific branch. Always set this when investigating a branch-specific discrepancy — leaving it unset shows all branches mixed together, making individual-branch analysis unreliable.

elements.name: Movement Type Filter
elements.description: Filter by type: OPENING_BALANCE, PURCHASE_IN, SALE_OUT, ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE, ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE, TRANSFER_IN, TRANSFER_OUT, RETURN_IN, RETURN_OUT, or STOCK_COUNT_VARIANCE.

elements.name: Actor Filter
elements.description: Shows only movements triggered by a specific user. Use this when reviewing a staff member's stock activity or investigating who made a particular change. Combine with a date range and Type filter for the most targeted results.

elements.name: From Date
elements.description: The start of the date range for movement records. Always set a date range for investigations — without filters, only the 50 most recent records load, which may not include the event you are looking for.

elements.name: To Date
elements.description: The end of the date range. Movements on this date are included. Set both From and To together to isolate a specific period, such as a single shift or a delivery window.

elements.name: View Toggle (Cards / Table / Timeline)
elements.description: Switch between three layouts: Table (dense rows with sortable columns), Cards (event cards with direction indicators, hero quantities, and relative timestamps), and Timeline (vertical gold-line timeline grouped by day with animated dots and time labels).

elements.name: Direction indicators
elements.description: Color-coded icons and quantities showing movement direction: green ArrowDown with + prefix for stock in (purchases, positive adjustments), red ArrowUp with - prefix for stock out (sales, negative adjustments), blue ArrowLeftRight for neutral (counts). Applied consistently across all three views.

elements.name: Movement type badges
elements.description: Color-coded StatusBadge labels on each movement (SALE_OUT, PURCHASE_IN, ADJUSTMENT_POSITIVE, etc.) with contextual Lucide icons: ShoppingCart for sales, Truck for purchases and transfers, Wrench for adjustments, ClipboardCheck for counts, RotateCcw for returns.

elements.name: Timeline view
elements.description: A vertical timeline with a gold gradient line running down the left side. Movements are grouped by day with date separators (pill-shaped headers with Calendar icon). Each movement appears as a node on the timeline: time label on the left, color-coded dot on the line (green for in, red for out, with glow shadow), and an event card on the right with type badge, quantity, variant name, branch, and actor. Dots animate in with a bounce effect; events stagger in one by one.

elements.name: Card view event cards
elements.description: Each movement is displayed as an event card with a direction indicator icon (large colored ArrowDown or ArrowUp), hero quantity number, type badge, variant and product name, branch with MapPin icon, actor with User icon and relative time (e.g. '5m ago'), and batch info if present.

elements.name: Export Movements button
elements.description: A button in the page header with a Download icon that navigates to the Exports page to create a stock movements export. Uses rounded-xl styling with nvi-press tactile feedback.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Date, Branch, Variant, Type, Quantity, Batch, and Actor. Table view wraps content in a Card component.

elements.name: Empty state
elements.description: When no movements match filters, shows a centered empty state with a floating Package icon, title message, and description. The icon uses the nvi-float animation for a gentle bobbing effect.

before_you_start.text: Stock movements are created automatically by system operations — you cannot manually create or delete them here.
stock.read (or equivalent) is required to access this page.
Set a date range for investigations — without filters, the most recent 50 records load by default.

steps: Set the Branch and Variant filters.
Set the Type filter to SALE_OUT or ADJUSTMENT_NEGATIVE.
Set a date range covering the suspicious period.
Review the Actor column to see who made each change.

common_tasks.task: Confirm a purchase was received into stock
steps: Set the Branch filter and Movement Type to PURCHASE_IN.
Search for the variant name.
Confirm a PURCHASE_IN movement exists with the expected quantity and date.

warnings: Movements are immutable — they cannot be edited or deleted. If a movement is incorrect, create a corrective adjustment on the Adjustments page.
Load More appends records to the list — scrolling back up will still show older results from the same session.
Without a date range, very large datasets may take longer to load.

related_pages.reason: Check on-hand levels before drilling into movement history.
Create a corrective adjustment if you find an incorrect movement.

entries.title: Supplier Returns
entries.overview: Return case management with amber reason hero containers, colored source badges (emerald for Purchase origin, blue for PO origin), and purple status pipeline dots. Return values displayed in red. Create form has amber left border accent with RotateCcw icon. Each return tells the dispute story with the reason as the visual anchor.

elements.name: KPI strip
elements.description: Five metric cards at the top of the page: Open Cases (RotateCcw icon, gold), Pending (Clock icon, amber), Completed (CheckCircle icon, emerald), Linked Receipts (Link icon, blue), Total Return Value (DollarSign icon, red). Each card has a colored icon container and large numeric value.

elements.name: View toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switches between card view (return case cards with reason hero, status pipeline, and line items) and table view (traditional table with truncated reason tooltips and source badges).

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Filter the return list by keyword — searches across return reasons, supplier names, and variant names.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by return status. PENDING means the return was submitted but is awaiting approval. COMPLETED means fully processed. REJECTED means denied — check whether a stock adjustment is needed.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Restricts the return list to records initiated at a specific branch. Shown with MapPin icon on cards.

elements.name: Supplier filter
elements.description: Restrict the return list to returns sent to a specific supplier. Shown with Building2 icon on cards.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Filters the return list to records created within the selected window. Dates shown as relative time on cards (e.g. '2h ago', '3d ago').

elements.name: Return reason (card hero)
elements.description: The return reason is displayed as the hero element on each card — a gold-bordered panel with a RotateCcw icon and the reason text. If no reason is provided, an italic placeholder is shown.

elements.name: Status pipeline
elements.description: Visual dot pipeline on each card showing the return journey: Pending (amber) -> Completed (blue) -> Credited (emerald). The current step has a ring highlight. Rejected returns show a red dot with a 'Rejected' label.

elements.name: Source link badge
elements.description: Colored icon badge on each card/row showing the linked source document. Purchase links show a blue ShoppingCart badge, Purchase Order links show a violet ClipboardList badge. Clicking navigates to the source page.

elements.name: Branch selector (create form)
elements.description: Labeled selector for the branch from which goods are being returned. Stock is deducted from this branch immediately when the return is submitted (inside the modal).

elements.name: Supplier selector (create form)
elements.description: Labeled selector with Building2 icon for the supplier you are returning goods to. Only ACTIVE suppliers appear (inside the modal).

elements.name: Purchase selector (create form)
elements.description: Optional labeled selector with ShoppingCart icon. Link the return to a specific purchase record for audit trail (inside the modal).

elements.name: Purchase Order selector (create form)
elements.description: Optional labeled selector with ClipboardList icon. Link the return to a specific purchase order (inside the modal).

elements.name: Reason input (create form)
elements.description: TextInput with label. Explain why the goods are being returned. This becomes the hero text on the resulting card (inside the modal).

elements.name: Return line card
elements.description: Each return line is wrapped in its own Card with hover effect. Contains variant selector, labeled quantity TextInput, unit selector, CurrencyInput for unit cost, receiving line selector, and remove button (inside the modal).

elements.name: Add Line button
elements.description: Adds another return line card. Shows Plus icon (inside the modal).

elements.name: "Create return" button (header) + modal
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a modal for creating a new supplier return. The modal contains: branch + supplier selectors, optional purchase + purchase-order link selectors, reason text input, and multi-line items builder (variant + quantity + unit + unit cost + receiving line, with per-line remove). Modal closes on success OR when backend returns `approvalRequired` (in which case the Approval Notice banner displays the pending approval ID). Previously this was an inline collapsible form — moved into a modal.

elements.name: Approval notice
elements.description: Gold-accented banner at the top of the page shown when the backend returns `approvalRequired` — displays the action label and `approvalId`. **Behavior fix:** the create modal closes but form data is preserved, success toast is suppressed, and the list is not reloaded (the return is not yet created, it is awaiting approval). Previously the form was reset and success toast fired incorrectly.

elements.name: Empty state
