elements.description: Shown when no returns exist. Floating RotateCcw icon with nvi-float animation and descriptive text.

elements.name: Pagination controls
elements.description: Standard pagination with page size selector at the bottom of the list.

elements.name: Table view reason column
elements.description: In table view, the reason column is truncated with a tooltip that shows the full text on hover.

before_you_start.text: You need the purchases.write permission.
A receiving record must exist for the items being returned — you can only return stock that was received through this system. The Receiving line selector shows only records that match.

common_tasks.task: Return damaged goods to a supplier
steps: Fill in Branch and Supplier. In Reason, describe the issue clearly — for example 'Items arrived damaged, packaging crushed on delivery'.
Click Add Line and select the variant being returned.
Select the receiving line the goods came from (the receiving line selector shows only matching records).
Enter the quantity being returned and the unit cost.
Click Submit. Stock is deducted from the branch immediately.

common_tasks.task: Return a wrong item delivered by a supplier
steps: Set Branch and Supplier. In Reason, note 'Wrong SKU delivered — received [variant X] but ordered [variant Y]'.
Add a return line for the incorrect variant, select its receiving line, and enter the quantity and cost.
Click Submit. After the return is processed, raise a new purchase order for the correct item.

common_tasks.task: Check the status of a submitted return
steps: Use the Status filter to show PENDING returns.
If a return is PENDING, it is awaiting approval on the Approvals page — ask a manager to review it.
If a return shows REJECTED, check whether a manual stock adjustment is needed to reverse the deduction.

warnings: Stock is deducted from the branch immediately when you submit — even if the return triggers an approval workflow. If the approval is later rejected, the stock deduction is not reversed automatically — a manual stock adjustment will be needed.

The Receiving line selector only shows lines that match the selected variant at the selected branch. If the expected line is missing, the goods may have been received under a different branch, or the receiving record may not exist yet.
Returns cannot be edited after submission. If you submitted incorrect quantities or the wrong variant, contact your supervisor — a correcting stock adjustment may be required.

entries.title: Suppliers
entries.overview: Manage your supplier directory with gradient avatar containers and per-contact colored icons: Phone in emerald, Mail in blue, and MapPin in amber. Lead time displays as a purple pill on each card, and the performance panel shows a dynamic ProgressBar whose color shifts based on the supplier's completion rate. Create and maintain vendor profiles, set lead times for delivery ETA suggestions, track outstanding balances, and review recent order activity.

elements.name: KPI — Suppliers
elements.description: Total number of supplier records matching the current filters. If no filters are set, this is your full supplier count.

elements.name: KPI — Active
elements.description: Count of suppliers with ACTIVE status. Only ACTIVE suppliers appear in purchase order and receiving selectors — if a supplier is missing from those selectors, check they are not INACTIVE or ARCHIVED here.

elements.name: KPI — Needs attention
elements.description: Count of suppliers that are INACTIVE or ARCHIVED. Review these periodically — suppliers that are no longer trading should be ARCHIVED so they no longer appear in purchase workflows.

elements.name: KPI — Lead-time set
elements.description: Count of suppliers that have a lead time (in days) configured. Lead times are used to calculate a suggested delivery ETA when creating purchase orders. If this count is low, consider setting lead times on your regular suppliers.

elements.name: Search bar
elements.description: Filter suppliers by name, phone, or email as you type. Use this to quickly locate a supplier when your list is long.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Narrow the list to ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED suppliers. ARCHIVED suppliers are hidden from purchase workflows but their historical records are preserved.

elements.name: Balance due filter
elements.description: Filter to suppliers with an outstanding balance (Yes) or a clear balance (No). Use this to identify which suppliers you owe money to before running payments.

elements.name: Reset button
elements.description: Clears all active filters and reloads the full supplier list.

elements.name: View toggle (Cards / Table)
elements.description: Switch between card layout (shows full details per supplier) and table layout (better for scanning many suppliers at once).

elements.name: Name field (create form)
elements.description: Required. The supplier's business or trading name. This name appears in purchase order selectors, receiving forms, and reports — use the name you recognise them by.

elements.name: Phone field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The supplier's contact phone number. Stored for reference — not used in automated workflows.

elements.name: Email field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The supplier's contact email address, stored for reference. Used when generating purchase order notification emails if that feature is enabled. Can be added later by editing the supplier record.

elements.name: Address field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The supplier's physical or postal address. Useful for delivery coordination and printed purchase orders.

elements.name: Notes field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. Free-text notes about the supplier — for example payment terms, preferred contact method, or product specialities. Visible to all users who can see this supplier.

elements.name: Lead time days field (create form)
elements.description: Optional. The typical number of days between placing an order and receiving the goods, displayed as a purple pill on the supplier card. When set, the Purchase Order Wizard displays a suggested ETA (today + lead time days) when you select this supplier. Set this accurately for better planning.

elements.name: Create Supplier button
elements.description: Saves the new supplier record. The supplier is immediately available in purchase order and receiving selectors. Requires the suppliers.write permission.

elements.name: Create supplier button (page header)
elements.description: Header action button with Plus icon. Opens the Supplier create modal. Only shown to users with the suppliers.write permission.

elements.name: Supplier create modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Create supplier header button. Contains name (required), phone, email, address, lead time in days, and notes. ESC or backdrop click closes without saving. Closes automatically after a successful create.

elements.name: Edit button (per supplier)
elements.description: Pencil action on each supplier card/row. Opens the Supplier edit modal with all fields plus the status selector (ACTIVE / INACTIVE / ARCHIVED). Requires the suppliers.write permission.

elements.name: Supplier edit modal
elements.description: Centered modal dialog opened from the Pencil action. Shows an avatar alongside name, phone, email, address, lead time, notes, and status. Save commits changes and refreshes the list; ESC or backdrop click cancels.

elements.name: View Activity / Hide Activity toggle
elements.description: Expands or collapses a panel showing the supplier's 5 most recent purchase orders and 5 most recent purchases. Use this for a quick snapshot of your order history with a supplier without leaving the page.

elements.name: Related Notes panel
elements.description: A collapsible notes thread attached to each supplier card. Team members can add timestamped notes visible to everyone — for example, recording a price negotiation outcome or a delivery complaint. Notes are separate from the main Notes field on the profile.

elements.name: Save / Cancel buttons (edit modal)
elements.description: Save commits all edited fields and refreshes the list on success. Cancel (or ESC, or backdrop click) discards all changes and closes the modal without saving.

elements.name: Load More button
elements.description: Fetches the next page of suppliers and appends them to the list. Use this if your search did not return the supplier you expected — they may be on a later page.

elements.name: CollapsibleSection (New Supplier form)
elements.description: The create form is hidden behind a 'New Supplier' button. Click to expand. Closes automatically after successful creation.

elements.name: Performance panel
elements.description: Click 'View Performance' on a supplier card to see their metrics: total orders, completed orders, and completion rate with a dynamic ProgressBar that shifts color based on rate (red for low, amber for moderate, emerald for high). Helps evaluate supplier reliability.

elements.name: RelatedNotesPanel
elements.description: A notes thread attached to each supplier card. Team members can add timestamped notes — useful for recording price negotiations, delivery complaints, or quality feedback.

elements.name: New PO quick link
elements.description: A button on each supplier card that navigates directly to the Purchase Orders page to create a new order for that supplier.

elements.name: Lead time missing warning
elements.description: An amber warning badge shown on supplier cards that have no lead time configured. Setting a lead time helps the system calculate expected delivery dates on purchase orders.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills on each supplier. Green for ACTIVE, amber for INACTIVE, grey for ARCHIVED.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers in table view for sorting by Name, Phone, Email, Lead Time, and Status.

elements.name: Contact info columns (table view)
elements.description: Phone and email are shown as separate columns in table view for quick reference without expanding the supplier card.

before_you_start.text: You need the suppliers.write permission to create or edit supplier records.
Set lead time days accurately — this directly affects the ETA suggestions shown when creating purchase orders.
The Activity panel loads purchase order and purchase data on demand — click View Activity to see it rather than loading it for every supplier at once.

steps: Fill in the Name (required) and optionally phone, email, address, and notes.
If you know the supplier's typical delivery time, enter it in Lead time days.
Click Create Supplier. The supplier is immediately available in purchase workflows.

common_tasks.task: Update supplier contact details or lead time
steps: Find the supplier card and click Edit.
Update the relevant fields.
Click Save. Changes take effect immediately — the new lead time will be used in the next purchase order for this supplier.

common_tasks.task: View recent orders for a supplier
steps: Find the supplier card.
Click View Activity to expand the recent order panel.
The panel shows the 5 most recent purchase orders and 5 most recent purchases for that supplier.

common_tasks.task: Add a note to a supplier
steps: Expand the Related Notes panel on the supplier card.
Type your note and submit. The note is timestamped and visible to all users who can see this supplier.
Set Balance due filter to Yes.
The list shows only suppliers you currently owe money to.
Use this before processing supplier payments to know who to pay.

warnings: Setting a supplier to ARCHIVED hides them from purchase order and receiving selectors. Their historical records are preserved, but you cannot create new orders for an archived supplier — set them back to ACTIVE if needed.
Lead time days affects the ETA hint in the Purchase Order Wizard. If set too low, staff may expect deliveries earlier than the supplier can actually deliver — set it accurately.

entries.title: Transfers
entries.overview: View and manage stock transfers between branches. This page shows all transfers in all statuses — use it to see what is in transit to your branch, receive incoming transfers to update your stock, and track the history of past transfers. To create a new transfer, use the Transfer Wizard.

elements.name: Search input
elements.description: Find transfers by keyword — searches across transfer codes, branch names, and item names. Use this to locate a specific transfer quickly.

elements.name: Branch filter
elements.description: Filter by source or destination branch. Set it to your branch as destination to see only incoming transfers, or as source to see outgoing ones.

elements.name: Status filter
elements.description: Filter by transfer status. PENDING — created, stock deducted from source, not yet in transit. IN_TRANSIT — dispatched by source branch, awaiting confirmation at destination. RECEIVED — destination branch confirmed receipt and stock has been added. PENDING_APPROVAL — waiting for manager approval before receipt can be confirmed.

elements.name: Date range (From / To)
elements.description: Restricts the transfer list to records created within the selected window. Use this when reconciling inter-branch stock movements for a specific period — for example, all transfers made in a particular week or month.

elements.name: Transfer card / row
elements.description: Shows the source branch, destination branch, list of items and quantities being transferred, any transfer fee details (carrier, amount), the current status, and the creation date. Click a transfer to expand its full details.

elements.name: Receive button (per transfer)
elements.description: Opens the receive form for this transfer. Only the destination branch staff should use this — it records what actually arrived and updates stock at the destination. Only visible on transfers with IN_TRANSIT or approved status.

elements.name: Receive quantities form
elements.description: One quantity field per item in the transfer. The expected quantity from the transfer is pre-filled — adjust each to match what physically arrived. If something did not arrive, enter 0 for that item and notify the source branch.

elements.name: Confirm Receive button
elements.description: Records the received quantities and updates stock at the destination branch immediately. The transfer status changes to RECEIVED. This action is final — it cannot be reversed. Verify the quantities before confirming.

elements.name: ViewToggle
elements.description: Switch between card view (more detail per transfer) and table view (better for scanning a large list of transfers at once).

elements.name: PaginationControls
elements.description: Navigate between pages of transfer records. Apply filters first to narrow the result set.

elements.name: "Create transfer" button (header)
elements.description: An accent-colored button in the page header opens a modal dialog for creating a new transfer. The modal collects source branch, destination branch, line items (variant, quantity, optional batch), and optional transfer-fee details (amount, currency, carrier, note). Previously the form was a collapsible section inline on the page — it has been moved into a modal to reduce page clutter.

elements.name: Transfer fee fields
elements.description: Optional fields to record fees associated with a transfer: fee amount, carrier name, and fee notes. Fees are tracked as expenses.

elements.name: Duplicate Transfer action
elements.description: Creates a copy of an existing transfer with the same source, destination, and items. Useful for recurring transfers between the same branches.

elements.name: Transfer fee summary KPI
elements.description: Shows the total transfer fees for the current period.

elements.name: StatusBadge
elements.description: Coloured status pills showing transfer status: REQUESTED, APPROVED, IN_TRANSIT, COMPLETED, CANCELLED.

elements.name: SortableTableHeader
elements.description: Clickable column headers for sorting by Source, Destination, Status, Date, Items, and Fee.

elements.name: Open Wizard link
elements.description: Navigates to the Transfer Wizard for a guided multi-step transfer creation process.

elements.name: Confirmation dialogs
elements.description: Approve and Cancel actions require confirmation before executing. The dialog shows the action and asks for confirmation.

elements.name: Reference number
elements.description: Each transfer has an auto-generated reference number (e.g. TRF-001). Shown as a subtitle under the branch route in both card and table views.

before_you_start.text: You need the transfers.write permission to receive transfers.
Transfers must be created using the Transfer Wizard before they appear here.
Only staff at the destination branch should receive a transfer — receiving at the wrong branch will add stock to the wrong location.

common_tasks.task: View all pending transfers to your branch
steps: Set Status filter to IN_TRANSIT.
Set Branch filter to your branch as the destination.
The list shows all transfers currently in transit to your branch that need to be received.
Find the transfer in the list (filter by IN_TRANSIT status and your branch as destination).
Click Receive on the transfer card.
Verify or adjust the quantities to match what actually arrived.
Click Confirm Receive. Your branch's stock is updated immediately.

common_tasks.task: Find a transfer by source or destination branch
steps: Set Branch filter to the relevant branch.
Optionally apply a Status or Date range filter to narrow further.
The list shows all transfers involving that branch.

warnings: Receiving a transfer updates destination branch stock immediately and cannot be undone. Confirm quantities match what physically arrived before clicking Confirm Receive.
Partial receiving is not supported — enter the quantities that actually arrived for each item. If items are missing, contact the source branch to resolve the discrepancy.

You cannot receive a transfer that is already in RECEIVED status. If a transfer was received with incorrect quantities, a stock adjustment at the destination branch will be needed to correct the difference.

entries.title: Transfer Wizard
entries.overview: Four-step transfer wizard with blue branch selector containers, emerald direction arrows between source and destination, amber fee section with left border accent. Item lines in neutral cards with blue variant icons and red remove buttons. Review step shows a gradient manifest card. Receive step has dynamically colored progress bars (green/amber/red based on completion percentage).

elements.name: Step indicator
elements.description: Shows current position: Details → Items → Review → Receive. Earlier steps are locked once you click Create Transfer on the Review step — you cannot go back to change the source branch or items after that point.

elements.name: Source branch selector
elements.description: The branch that is sending the stock, displayed inside a blue container. Stock is deducted from this branch's inventory when the transfer is created — not when it arrives at the destination. Ensure the source branch has sufficient stock before creating the transfer.

elements.name: Destination branch selector
elements.description: The branch that will receive the stock, displayed inside a blue container. An emerald directional arrow connects source to destination, reinforcing the transfer flow. Stock is only added to this branch when the transfer is received and confirmed. The source and destination cannot be the same branch.

elements.name: Fee amount input
elements.description: Optional. The cost of shipping, transport, or logistics for this transfer. Displayed inside an amber-accented section with a left border to visually separate fee details from transfer items. Recorded for expense tracking purposes only — it does not affect stock levels or the items being transferred.

elements.name: Fee currency input
elements.description: The currency the transfer fee is charged in. Defaults to your business currency. Change this if the fee is invoiced in a foreign currency.

elements.name: Fee carrier input
elements.description: Optional. The name of the transport company or carrier handling the delivery — for example 'DHL', 'Own Vehicle', or 'Third-party Courier'. Stored for reference and reporting.

elements.name: Fee note input
elements.description: Optional. Additional notes about the transfer fee — for example a waybill number, tracking reference, or any special delivery instructions.

elements.name: Item row (Items step)
elements.description: One row per item being transferred. For each row, select the variant to transfer, enter the quantity to send, and optionally select a specific batch if batch tracking is enabled. The quantity you enter is deducted from the source branch immediately on transfer creation.

elements.name: Batch selector (per item)
elements.description: Available when batch tracking is enabled in Business Settings. Select which batch the transferred stock should come from. Only batches with available stock at the source branch appear. Use this when you need to move a specific batch of perishable or regulated items.

elements.name: Add Item button
elements.description: Adds another item row with emerald Plus icon styling. Use this when transferring multiple different variants in a single transfer — one row per variant.

elements.name: Remove Item button
elements.description: Removes this item row from the transfer before it is submitted. Use it if you added the wrong variant or need to adjust the item list. Once the transfer is created and submitted, individual lines cannot be removed — a corrective transfer in the opposite direction is required.

elements.name: Review step
elements.description: Full summary of the transfer with source and destination branches, all items and quantities, and fee details. Review carefully — clicking Create Transfer locks all details and deducts stock from the source branch immediately.

elements.name: Create Transfer button
elements.description: Creates the transfer record and immediately deducts the specified quantities from the source branch. The transfer is now in transit — the destination branch must confirm receipt separately to update their stock.

elements.name: Receive step
elements.description: After creating the transfer, optionally confirm receipt in the same session. Useful for same-day internal moves (e.g. from warehouse to shop floor in the same building). Enter the quantity received per item. To receive later, skip this step and use the Transfers page.

elements.name: Receive button
elements.description: Records the receipt and updates destination branch stock immediately. If an approval is required for the transfer total, this button is blocked until the approval is granted on the Approvals page.

elements.name: Approval notice banner
elements.description: Appears when the transfer total exceeds the approval threshold set in Business Settings. The transfer is created and stock is deducted from the source branch, but the destination cannot receive it until a user with approvals.write permission approves it.

elements.name: Copy from previous transfer
elements.description: Load items from a recent transfer as a template. Select from a list of recent transfers.

elements.name: Fee section
elements.description: Optional fields for transfer fees: amount, currency, carrier name, and notes.

elements.name: KPI cards
elements.description: Four KPI cards: Current Step, Draft Items, Valid Items, Transfer Created.

elements.name: Receive step (expected vs received)
elements.description: Enter quantities actually received at the destination. Compare against expected quantities. Progress is colour-coded dynamically: green when received matches expected, amber when close but not exact, red when significantly under or over the expected amount.

elements.name: Finish Later button
elements.description: Save progress and return to the Transfers list. Resume later.

elements.name: Visual progress bar
elements.description: Step indicator: Details, Items, Review, Receive.

before_you_start.text: You need the transfers.write permission.
Both the source and destination branches must already exist.
Enable batch tracking in Business Settings before creating the transfer if you need to move stock by specific batch.

common_tasks.task: Create a transfer between two branches
steps: On the Details step, select the source branch (sending) and destination branch (receiving). Optionally add fee details. Click Next.
On the Items step, add one row per variant. Select the variant and enter the quantity to send. Click Next.
On the Review step, verify all details. Click Create Transfer — stock is deducted from the source branch immediately.
Skip the Receive step if the destination will receive it later from the Transfers page.

common_tasks.task: Create and receive a transfer in one session (same-day internal move)
steps: Complete the Details, Items, and Review steps and click Create Transfer.
On the Receive step, verify or adjust the received quantities.
Click Receive. Destination branch stock is updated immediately.

common_tasks.task: Transfer a specific batch of stock
steps: On the Items step, select the variant to transfer.
Use the Batch selector to choose the specific batch to move from the source branch.
Enter the quantity and continue to Review, then Create Transfer.

warnings: Source branch stock is deducted immediately when the transfer is created — before the destination receives it. Ensure the source branch has enough stock before creating the transfer.
Destination branch stock is updated only when the transfer is received and confirmed. Until then, the stock is in transit — counted at the source but not yet at the destination.
If an approval is triggered, the destination cannot receive the transfer until it is approved. The source branch stock is already deducted during this wait.

entries.title: Verify Email
entries.overview: This page confirms your email address after signing up. When you arrive here via the link in your email, verification runs automatically. If it does not, you can submit the token manually. You can also resend the verification email if the first one expired or did not arrive.

before_you_start.text: You must have received a verification email after signing up.

elements.name: Token field
elements.description: The verification code from your email. If you arrived via the email link, this is pre-filled and verification runs automatically. If the field is blank, paste the token from the email.

elements.name: Verify button
elements.description: Manually submits the token to complete verification. Use this if verification did not run automatically.

elements.name: Resend Verification Email button
elements.description: Sends a new verification email to your registered address. Use this if the original email expired, was deleted, or never arrived.

elements.name: Auto-verification on load
elements.description: If the verification token is in the URL, verification starts automatically when the page loads. No button click needed.

elements.name: Two redirect pathways
elements.description: On success: if a session is created automatically, you go straight to the Dashboard. Otherwise, you are redirected to the Login page.

elements.name: Resend verification email
elements.description: If your verification link expired, enter your email and click Resend to get a new verification email.

steps: Click the link in the verification email sent to you after signup.
If verification runs automatically, you will be logged in and taken to the Dashboard.
If it does not run automatically, paste the token from the email into the Token field and click Verify.
Click Resend Verification Email.
Check your inbox for the new email.
Click the new link or paste the new token into the Token field.

warnings: Verification links expire after a set time. If yours has expired, use the Resend button to get a new one.
You cannot log in to the system until your email is verified.
If the resend button fails, make sure your original signup email and business ID are still in the page URL.

related_pages.reason: You arrive here after completing the signup form.
After verification, sign in here.
If verification creates a session automatically, you land here.

entries.title: Change Password
entries.overview: A security-focused page for changing your account password. Features a centered Card layout with a ShieldCheck icon, password requirements hint, and real-time mismatch validation. Accessible from your Profile settings via a back link.

before_you_start.text: You must be logged in to your account.
You need to know your current password.

elements.name: PageHeader
elements.description: Page header with 'Security' eyebrow, 'Change password' title, and subtitle about keeping your account secure.

elements.name: Back to Profile link
elements.description: A subtle link with ChevronLeft icon above the form card. Navigates back to your profile settings page.

elements.name: ShieldCheck icon
elements.description: Large shield icon in a tinted gold container at the top of the form card, with a security hint message below it.

elements.name: Current Password field
elements.description: TextInput with Lock icon label. Enter your existing password to verify identity. Eye/EyeOff toggle to show or hide the password.

elements.name: New Password field
elements.description: TextInput with KeyRound icon label. Enter your new password. Eye/EyeOff toggle. Below the field, a muted hint shows password requirements (minimum 8 characters, mix of letters, numbers, and symbols).

elements.name: Confirm Password field
elements.description: TextInput with Check icon label. Re-enter the new password. Eye/EyeOff toggle. Shows a real-time red error message if the passwords do not match.

elements.name: Update Password button
elements.description: Full-width gold button with Lock icon. Submits the password change. Disabled until all three fields are filled. Shows 'Saving...' state while processing.

elements.name: Banner feedback
elements.description: Error messages shown via a dismissible Banner above the form card. Success is shown as a toast notification.

elements.name: Form auto-clear on success
elements.description: After successfully changing your password, all form fields are cleared automatically.

steps: Enter your current password in the first field.
Type your new password in the New Password field.
Re-type the new password in the Confirm Password field.
Click 'Update Password'.
If successful, you will see a toast notification and all fields will clear.

warnings: If you have forgotten your current password, use the 'Forgot Password' link on the login page instead.
After changing your password, any other devices where you are logged in will continue to work until their session expires.

related_pages.reason: Access change password from your profile settings.

entries.title: Business Suspended
entries.overview: This page appears when your business account has been suspended. While suspended, you cannot access any business features. Contact support to resolve the suspension.

before_you_start.text: This page appears automatically — you do not navigate to it manually.

elements.name: Suspension notice
elements.description: Displays the reason for suspension and instructions for contacting support to resolve it.

elements.name: Sign Out button
elements.description: Clears your session and redirects to the login page. The only action available on this page.

elements.name: Full-screen layout
elements.description: The suspension notice is displayed centered on a full-screen page with no navigation sidebar.

steps: Read the suspension reason displayed on the page.
Contact support using the provided contact information.
Follow any instructions given by the support team.
Once resolved, you will be able to access your business again.

warnings: You cannot access sales, stock, or any business features while suspended.
Only the platform administrator can lift a suspension.

related_pages.reason: You may be redirected here after login if your business is suspended.
